summary: Bangkok is an excellent fit for Riza's short 7-day work trip—affordable, fast internet, world-class coworking with phone booths, and public transit-friendly. Budget of €2000 is comfortable for accommodation + coworking + food. Main drawback: air pollution varies seasonally and heat/humidity is intense year-round.
remote_work_pitch: Bangkok offers dedicated coworking spaces (WeWork, Glowfish, The Commons) with phone booths for video calls, fiber-optic internet averaging 140 Mbps, and a strong startup/tech scene. The city's BTS/MRT system means Riza avoids car dependency; day passes cost under €0.50. At €30-50/day for decent accommodation plus €5-15 for coworking, the budget easily accommodates his needs while meeting all client timezone windows (UK -7h, Dubai -5h, Tokyo +9h). Coffee shop culture is strong, but dedicated spaces are essential for call quality—both available.
best_months: November–February (cooler, lower humidity, minimal air pollution). Avoid March–May (peak heat, poor air quality from regional biomass burning).

internet.notes: Fiber-optic backbone in central areas (Sukhumvit, Sathorn, Silom); AIS/True providers deliver 500 Mbps+ in premium buildings. Coworking spaces offer redundant systems and power backup. Mobile 5G/4G very reliable as secondary backup.

work_spots.name: WeWork Sukhumvit
work_spots.price_eur: €28/day (~950 THB day pass) or €186/month (~6700 THB monthly)
work_spots.notes: Multiple Sukhumvit locations; phone booths, meeting rooms, high-speed internet, networking events, printing services. Ideal for client calls due to soundproofing and presentation equipment.

work_spots.name: Glowfish Asoke
work_spots.price_eur: €20/day day pass estimate (~700 THB equiv.) or monthly plans available
work_spots.notes: Established coworking space with call booths, gym, fitness studio, dining hall. Open 24/7; ideal for timezone-bridging. Asoke location is central BTS-accessible.

work_spots.name: The Commons Thonglor
work_spots.notes: Curated community space with regular pitch nights and workshops. Strong founder/startup vibe. Close to BTS Thonglor. Good for side-project networking (Claude API, n8n builders).

work_spots.name: C Asean Samyan CO-OP (Mitrtown)
work_spots.price_eur: €0 (free, registration required; mall Wi-Fi via AIS/TrueMove customer gate)
work_spots.notes: Free 24/7 coworking inside shopping mall. Phone booths, multiple levels, plugs. No dedicated coffee, but 24/7 Starbucks in same building. Good backup/budget option.

stays.name: Royal Ivory Nana Sukhumvit
stays.why: Kitchenette rooms ideal for extended stays. Weekly rate (10-15% discount vs. nightly). 7-10 min walk to BTS Nana. No joiner fee. Laundry on-site. €52/night fits budget well.

stays.name: Grande Centre Point Hotel Sukhumvit
stays.why: Budget-friendly 3-star chain with consistent quality. BTS On Nut nearby (Sukhumvit line). 30-40% cheaper than central Thonglor. Modern amenities (pool, gym). €45/night x 7 = €315, leaves ~€1685 for coworking + food.

stays.name: Citadines Sukhumvit 11 (Serviced Apartment)
stays.why: Full kitchenette + laundry; ideal for self-catering to reduce food costs. Sukhumvit location = central to coworking hubs. 7-day rate applies. €55/night provides comfort + flexibility.

visa.details: Turkish passport holder with Slovak Pobyt (EU residence permit) enters Thailand visa-free, receiving 30-day exemption on arrival. No extension possible on exemption—would require DTV (Digital Nomad Visa) for longer stays. For 7-day trip, visa-free entry is standard. Ensure passport valid ≥6 months. Schengen/UK residency does not affect Thailand visa policy.

air_water_safety.air_quality_notes: May 2026 AQI ~50-53 (Moderate). Acceptable for most, but sensitive groups may experience minor symptoms. Worst months: March–May (biomass burning, AQI often >100 Unhealthy). Best: November–February (AQI 30-60 Good/Moderate). 7-day May visit = manageable; advise air purifier in hotel if sensitive.
air_water_safety.safety_notes: Bangkok is safe for solo travelers and expats, even at night in well-traveled areas (Sukhumvit, Thonglor, Sathorn). Petty theft and scams exist but rare. Avoid street-level chaos (Khao San Rd), red-light districts (lower Sukhumvit). Coworking areas and hotels are secure. No violent crime in tourist zones. Use Grab/Bolt (ride-share apps) instead of street taxis for peace of mind.

ecommerce_events.name: Bangkok Tech Summit / Digital Innovation Week
ecommerce_events.relevance: Adjacent (tech/startup ecosystem, not ecommerce-specific, but relevant to AI/indie-hacker community)

ecommerce_events.name: Bangkok Digital Nomad Meetup (weekly)
ecommerce_events.relevance: Adjacent (networking, digital product builders, AI discussion groups)

ecommerce_events.name: Bangkok Startup Events (via Lu.ma / Eventbrite)
ecommerce_events.relevance: Ecommerce/Marketing (pitch nights at The Commons, HUBBA, WeWork focus on founders & side-project makers)

weather.conditions: Hot, humid year-round; monsoon May-October (afternoon rain, cooler). Dry season Nov-April (hotter but less rain).

options.name: Hotel/Condo Pools (in-building or nearby chains)
options.quality: Clean, chlorinated, well-maintained in 3-5 star properties
options.transit_from_center: Sukhumvit/Thonglor: 5-10 min walk to hotel pool; most coworking-adjacent hotels offer pool access for residents

options.name: Bangkok City Pool (Hua Mark Indoor Olympic Pool)
options.quality: Public facility, functional, less upscale than hotel pools
options.transit_from_center: BTS to Hua Mark area, ~20 min from central Sukhumvit

options.name: Beaches (day trips: Pattaya ~140 km; Rayong ~220 km)
options.quality: Warm seawater, moderately crowded; Pattaya closer but busier
options.transit_from_center: Minivans/trains from Bangkok; 2-3 hour drive. Sea swimming feasible for weekend, not practical for 7-day work trip.

options.name: Fitness First (multiple Bangkok locations)
options.facilities: Weights, cardio, group classes, pool (select), sauna, modern equipment
options.notes: Most professional gym chain in Bangkok. English-speaking staff. Sukhumvit flagship (near BTS) is premium; prices vary by location.

options.name: Virgin Active (Central Embassy, EmQuartier)
options.facilities: Weights, cardio, yoga, Pilates, pool, sauna, luxury lounge
options.notes: High-end upscale gym with premium vibe. Classes in English. Pricier but excellent for lifting + cardio + yoga/Pilates. Central location.

options.name: Yoga Park Studios (Ari, Thonglor, On Nut)
options.facilities: Yoga, Pilates, mobility classes; some studios have strength conditioning
options.notes: Popular with nomads/expat community. Drop-in classes in English. Ari location known for community vibe. Strong Pilates/yoga focus.

gym.running_options: Lumphini Park (central, ~50 hectares, 3-5 km flat/shaded running path, BTS Mo Chit or Rama IV nearby). Early morning (6-8am) best to avoid heat/traffic noise. Also Benjakitti Park, Bang Kachao riverside.
gym.yoga_pilates: Virgin Active classes (Chidlom); Yoga Park (Ari, Thonglor) all-levels; Dharma Care (Ari) yoga studio; multiple boutique Pilates studios in Thonglor. All offer drop-in or class packages. Classes typically €8-15 per session.

day_trips.hiking: Khao Sam Roi Yot National Park (~200 km, 3-4 hrs by car/minivan from Bangkok). Limestone cliffs, caves, sea views, forest trails. Doable as overnight trip or long day-trip by minivan. Transit: book minivan via hotel concierge or online (GrabTaxi/Bolt with driver for day hire, ~€80-100 all-day). Alternatively, Erawan National Park (3 hrs) has easier waterfall hikes. Not practical for 7-day work trip unless dedicated weekend escape.

marinas.name: Royal Varuna Yacht Club
marinas.location: Bang Saray, Rayong Province (~120 km SE of Bangkok)
marinas.transit_from_center: Car rental or hired driver minivan via Grab; 2.5-3 hr drive from central Bangkok

marinas.name: Ao Chalong Yacht Club
marinas.location: Ao Chalong Bay, Phuket (~850 km S; requires flight or 12+ hr drive)
marinas.transit_from_center: Domestic flight BKK → HKT (90 min); not feasible for 7-day trip

sailing.best_season: October-April (dry season, moderate winds, calm sea). May-September monsoon = choppy, risky.
sailing.notes: Gulf of Thailand sailing scene is modest compared to international standards. Chartering and lessons available but require day-trip/weekend commitment. Royal Varuna (near Bangkok, Bang Saray) is closest option; still 2.5-3 hrs from city center. Phuket is stronger sailing hub but requires flight. For Riza's 7-day trip, sailing is aspirational rather than practical—recommend 3+ day coastal weekend instead.

new_hobbies.venue: Chiang Mai Boxing Stadium style or modern gym (e.g., Santitham Muay Thai Gym, or Sor. Vorapin Gym near Ekkamai)
new_hobbies.language: Thai + limited English; trainers adjust for foreigners
new_hobbies.notes: Authentic Muay Thai lessons (1-on-1 or group) widely available in Bangkok. Combines strength training (Riza's gym interest) with local culture. Intro/drop-in class ~500 THB (~€13). Gyms like Santitham Muay Thai offer daily classes 9am-1pm & 4pm-8pm suitable for nomad schedules.

new_hobbies.venue: Bangkok Thai Cooking School (Sukhumvit) or Blue Elephant Cooking School (Sathorn)
new_hobbies.notes: Half-day or full-day classes (morning market tour + cooking). Strong food culture ties into Riza's background, and cooking is practical for extended stays. Classes typically €40-60. Popular with digital nomads; many groups.

new_hobbies.venue: Freediving schools (Phuket-based; day-trip to Gulf islands feasible but time-intensive)
new_hobbies.neighborhood: Not Bangkok-based; requires travel to coastal areas
new_hobbies.notes: Thailand is a world-class freediving destination, but schools cluster in Phuket/Ao Chalong. For 7-day Bangkok work trip, not practical. Mention as post-trip option for extended stays.

new_hobbies.venue: Wat Po Thai Traditional Massage School (central Bangkok) or similar
new_hobbies.notes: Half-day intro workshops teach basic Thai massage technique. Popular with digital nomads; low cost, cultural immersion. Full certification is 1-2 months (not 7-day feasible). But intro massages are relaxing side activity for recovery (lifting + cardio schedule).

new_hobbies.venue: Photo tours of temples, street markets, rooftop sunrise/sunset spots (self-guided or group tours via Meetup/Airbnb Experiences)
new_hobbies.neighborhood: Wat Phra Kaew, Chatuchak Market, Lumphini Park, Thonglor rooftops
new_hobbies.notes: Bangkok's contrasts (temples/modern skyline) are photogenic. Many digital nomad meetups organize weekend photo walks. Camera-friendly, fits with creative mindset (AI side-projects, indie-hacker vibe). Low cost, flexible scheduling.

coworking_retreats.notes: Bangkok does not host curated co-living/retreat operators (Feel.travel, Coworkations, Outsite, Sun and Co, NomadX, Mokrin House, Work Wanderers) as permanent fixtures. The city is dense urban rather than retreat-focused. Some operators run pop-up 'workations' or weekend off-sites in nearby islands (Koh Chang, Koh Samui) but no rolling/year-round co-living in Bangkok proper. Riza's 7-day trip is short—traditional private accommodation + coworking day-pass model is more practical and flexible than curated retreats.

car_free.walkability: Poor at street level (heat, humidity, chaotic traffic, sidewalk vendors). Excellent walkability within enclosed areas (malls, hotel districts). Practical: BTS/MRT + short walks, not street walking.
car_free.public_transport: BTS Skytrain (gold standard; 7 lines, frequent trains 6am-midnight) and MRT subway. Single journey ~€0.40-0.80 (10-40 THB). Day pass ~€1.50 (50 THB). Monthly pass ~€18 (600 THB). Convenient, air-conditioned, safe. Grab/Bolt rideshare apps cheaper than taxis. Riza avoids car rental entirely.
car_free.airport_to_center: Suvarnabhumi Airport (BKK) → city center: Airport Rail Link train (45 min, ~€3 to Phaya Thai BTS hub) or Grab minivan (45-60 min depending on traffic, €15-20). Direct to Sukhumvit hotels via Grab is simplest for 7-day trip. Taxi meter available but Grab preferable for fixed pricing.

startup_community.scene_summary: Bangkok has a growing startup ecosystem (tech, ecommerce, fintech) stronger than Chiang Mai but smaller than Singapore/HCMC. Founder density is real—WeWork, The Commons, HUBBA host regular pitch nights and demo events. AI/ML interest is emerging (n8n, automation tools, local API providers). Indie-hacker scene exists but less prominent than Southeast Asia's main hubs. Networking is active; events happen weekly via Meetup/Lu.ma.

key_venues.name: The Commons Thonglor
key_venues.what: Pitch nights, founder workshops, regular networking events, strong community vibe

key_venues.name: WeWork Sukhumvit
key_venues.what: Large-scale events, lunch-and-learns, wellness activities, international founder network

key_venues.name: Bangkok Tech Cafe / Yellow Lane (Ari)
key_venues.what: Casual founder hangout, informal meetups, creative crowd

key_venues.name: Bangkok Digital Nomads / Bangkok Expat Meetup Groups
key_venues.what: Weekly meetups, Telegram/WhatsApp community, product talk, AI/tech discussion threads

startup_community.recurring_events: Weekly digital nomad meetups (Bangkok Digital Nomads group, Tuesdays typically). Monthly pitch nights at The Commons (1st Thursday). Quarterly tech conferences (dates vary). AI/ML workshops emerging at WeWork/HUBBA. IndieHackers occasionally has Bangkok-specific threads on startup Reddit/Facebook groups.

bonus.flight_from_vie: Vienna (VIE) → Bangkok (BKK/DMK): Direct flights rarely available; typical routing via Istanbul, Doha, Dubai (Turkish Airlines, Qatar, Emirates). Duration: 12-16 hours total (1-2 stops). ~€450-750 roundtrip in May off-peak. Budget carriers (Wizz, Ryanair) not practical for this distance.

timezones.vs_new_york: ICT +12 hours ahead (e.g., 2pm BKK = 2am NY; challenging)

bonus.classical_music: Limited but present. Bangkok's opera/concert scene includes: Thailand Cultural Center (Huai Khwang, BTS) hosts classical performances (Thai orchestras, visiting orchestras); Bangkok Philharmonic Orchestra performs seasonally. Harder to find compared to Vienna or London. Thai classical music (traditional instruments) is accessible at temples & cultural centers. May 2026 not peak concert season (summer slump), but check Eventbrite/Lu.ma for performances.
bonus.dance_scene: Bachata/Salsa: Strong Latin dance community in Bangkok. Multiple dedicated salsa clubs & dance schools (e.g., Salsa Club Bangkok near Thonglor, Dance@Heart studios). Weekly salsa socials, dance classes (group & private lessons €15-30/hr). Bachata less common but available via same studios. Riza's interest in salsa/bachata is well-served; expat community ensures English-speaking instructors and friendly social scenes. Thonglor/Ekkamai neighborhoods have most clubs.

concerns: Air quality deterioration in March-May (biomass burning) is significant; recommend visit in Nov-Feb if air-sensitive.
Intense year-round heat (28-35°C) and humidity (60-90%) may fatigue non-acclimatized visitors; adequate hydration and AC breaks essential.
7-day window is short for deep cultural immersion or sailing/hiking day-trips; focus on work + local coworking community is realistic scope.
Traffic congestion is legendary, but BTS/MRT + Grab mitigate; owning a car is not necessary but ride-share can add up if daily usage high.
Ecommerce events and AI/startup conferences are less frequent/structured than in Singapore or Chiang Mai; check Lu.ma/Meetup weekly for timely opportunities.

summary: Europe's startup and creative powerhouse: a vast, multicultural, surprisingly affordable capital with a world-class classical scene, lake swimming, and the deepest tech/ecommerce networking in this batch. Sprawling and grey in winter, but unmatched for professional depth. Schengen, so days count toward your 90/180.
remote_work_pitch: For an ecommerce/AI builder, Berlin is the strongest networking base in your library: a huge startup ecosystem, endless coworking (St. Oberholz, Factory, betahaus), the Berlin Philharmonic and three opera houses for classical, summer lake swimming (Wannsee, Müggelsee), and a real bachata/salsa scene, all on your time zone and cheaper than Munich or Vienna. The trade-offs are the city's sprawl, German bureaucracy and long grey winters.
best_months: May to September (lake season, long days); long grey winters

internet.notes: Fast in coworking and newer flats; some older buildings lag and home-fiber installs can be slow

work_spots.name: St. Oberholz
work_spots.notes: The original Berlin laptop cafe/coworking; legendary

work_spots.name: betahaus
work_spots.notes: Big creative/startup community with events

work_spots.name: JuggleHUB
work_spots.notes: Friendly community space; ~139 EUR/month

stays.name: Monthly apartment (Kreuzberg / Prenzlauer Berg)
stays.why: Central, walkable, cafe- and nightlife-rich; book early, Berlin housing is tight

visa.details: Visa-free on your Slovak permit; Schengen, so days COUNT toward your 90/180. Germany's Freiberufler freelance visa exists for longer stays. Counts toward Schengen.

air_water_safety.air_quality_notes: Clean for a big city; lots of green/water
air_water_safety.safety_notes: Generally safe; some rougher pockets at night and the usual big-city pickpocketing. Tap water fine

ecommerce_events.name: Strong: ecommerce/tech (K5, Tech Open Air, countless meetups)

weather.conditions: Continental; pleasant summers, long grey winters

options.name: Wannsee, Müggelsee, Schlachtensee
options.quality: Excellent clean swimming lakes ringing the city in summer

options.name: Berlin Badeschiff and city pools
options.quality: Year-round and a pool floating in the Spree

options.name: Urban Sports Club / FitX / McFit
options.facilities: weights, cardio, classes, pools; Urban Sports Club multi-venue
options.notes: Urban Sports Club is ideal for a nomad (one pass, many venues)

gym.running_options: Tiergarten, Tempelhofer Feld (old airport), canal towpaths and lakes

day_trips.hiking: Grunewald forest and the lakes, Potsdam's Sanssouci parks, Spreewald canals, Saxon Switzerland a train ride away

sailing.notes: Big-lake sailing right in the city on Wannsee and Müggelsee

new_hobbies.venue: Berlin's club scene and maker studios
new_hobbies.notes: Berlin's club and maker culture are world-defining; something genuinely new

coworking_retreats.notes: City coworking and coliving (The Social Hub) rather than retreats

car_free.walkability: Sprawling but superb U-/S-Bahn, trams and bike lanes; pick a district and stay local
car_free.public_transport: U-/S-Bahn/tram/bus, ~49 EUR/month (Deutschlandticket); no car needed
car_free.airport_to_center: BER ~30-45min by S-Bahn/express train (~4 EUR) or taxi ~35 EUR

startup_community.scene_summary: One of Europe's top startup and ecommerce hubs (Zalando, countless DTC brands, strong AI scene), with relentless meetups and events. The best professional-networking city in this batch for your field.

key_venues.name: Factory Berlin / betahaus / St. Oberholz
key_venues.what: Startup and tech community anchors

startup_community.recurring_events: K5, Tech Open Air, ecommerce/AI meetups

bonus.flight_from_vie: Direct ~1.5h to Berlin (BER)
bonus.classical_music: Berlin Philharmonic, Staatsoper, Deutsche Oper, Komische Oper, Konzerthaus; a world classical capital
bonus.dance_scene: Strong bachata/salsa scene plus the legendary techno clubs

concerns: Sprawling; commutes if you pick the wrong district

sources.what: Costs, transit, connectivity

summary: Bologna is a strong fit for Riza: excellent fiber internet (300–1000 Mbps), abundant coworking with phone booths, thriving academic/tech scene, and walkable historic center ideal for car-free living. Food, culture, and classical music are excellent. Budget is comfortable: €80–110/night hotels leave room for coworking and meals within €1200/10 days.
remote_work_pitch: Bologna combines reliable infrastructure with vibrant startup energy anchored by one of Europe's oldest universities. Multiple coworking spaces (Lo Spazio, Crea System, The Social Hub) feature phone booths and fast wifi critical for your Amazon calls. The city is car-free friendly—buses, medieval walkways, and excellent transit reduce friction. Off-season (Sept–Nov, Jan–Mar) brings lower prices and fewer tourists.
best_months: September–November, January–March (mild weather, fewer tourists, lower prices). May–June acceptable. Avoid July–August (hot, crowded); December–early January (cold, holidays). Winter swimming limited to indoor pools.

internet.notes: Fiber-to-home widely deployed in city center. Vodafone, Fastweb, Telecom Italia dominant. Median fixed 61 Mbps (Sep 2025); city early adopter of municipal WiFi. Mobile 4G/5G excellent across center. Suitable for video calls, API calls, simultaneous streams.

work_spots.name: Lo Spazio Coworking
work_spots.notes: 4.2★ (1021 reviews). Private offices, meeting rooms, phone booths listed. Near train station for easy airport access. Courteous staff, quiet professional vibe. Wifi reliable. Ideal for 10-day stay: daily rate ~€25, or negotiate 10-day pass ~€200–250.

work_spots.name: BAAK (Book Adventure and Kitchen)
work_spots.price_eur: €3–5 coffee/tea; coworking by donation or small fee (~€5)
work_spots.notes: Bookshop-bistro. Large communal tables. Free wifi, power at most seats. Afternoon tea/aperitivo (5pm) included. English-friendly meetups 1st Friday of month. Quiet morning atmosphere ideal for 3+ hour sessions. No phone booth; use nearby Sala Borsa (library) for calls.

work_spots.name: Le Serre Coworking (Giardini Margherita)
work_spots.neighborhood: Margherita Gardens (northeast green space, ~1.5 km from center)
work_spots.price_eur: Free outdoor coworking (food/drinks purchased separately). Cafe menu €6–15.
work_spots.notes: Outdoor coworking in greenhouse park. Power outlets, free wifi. Farm-to-table 'Vetro' bistro. Aquaponics surrounds workspace. Unique vibe; excellent for mid-day focus breaks. Phone calls challenging (open air); best for async work or use coworking spaces nearby.

work_spots.name: Sala Borsa (Public Library)
work_spots.best_for: long focus, free, quiet, study vibe
work_spots.notes: Free public library with excellent WiFi, power sockets, climate control in historic palazzo. No phone booth; must use nearby coworking for sensitive calls. Popular with remote workers and students. Can get crowded late morning/early afternoon.

stays.name: Phi Hotel Bologna
stays.why: 4★ boutique, central location, clean/comfortable rooms, plentiful breakfast. €110/night × 10 = €1,100. Connected restaurant (Atipico). Professional staff. Leaves €100 for coworking (€15/day × 6–7 days) + food (€15–20/day). TripAdvisor #14 of 114 Bologna hotels (4/5★).

stays.name: Oroglio Hotel
stays.why: 4★ boutique, praised for service, staff warmth, breakfast quality, central location. €105/night × 10 = €1,050. Leaves €150 for coworking + meals. High cleanliness/comfort ratings (Feb 2025 review). Walking distance to Piazza Maggiore, coworking, cafes.

stays.name: Residence Panigale Apartment Hotel
stays.neighborhood: Borgo Panigale (2 km south of center, airport-adjacent)
stays.why: 1-bed apartment with full kitchen, fitness center, 24h security, parking. €75/night × 10 = €750. Leaves €450 for coworking (10 days), food, transit. Farther from center (bus ~15 min) but kitchen saves food costs. Airport shuttle nearby (saves €30 taxi). Budget-conscious option.

visa.details: Turkish citizen + Slovak Pobyt residence permit = visa-free travel in EU/EEA for up to 90 days in any 180-day rolling period (Schengen rule). 10-day stay poses zero visa concern. No restrictions on work for EU residents.

air_water_safety.air_quality_notes: AQI typically 25–45 (Moderate–Good). Worst months: Dec–Feb (heating, fog in Po Valley). Winter inversions can raise AQI to 50–80 occasionally. Summer/spring excellent. Tap water drinkable, regularly tested.
air_water_safety.safety_notes: Bologna is safe by European standards. Petty theft (pickpockets) in Piazza Maggiore/train station during crowded hours; use common sense. Solo night walking in historic center safe. University presence means active nightlife, well-lit streets. Avoid walking alone very late (after midnight) in outer neighborhoods. Scams: fake ticket helpers at train station, overpriced tourist restaurants on Piazza facing Maggiore.

ecommerce_events.name: Pitti Immagine (Florence, ~150 km; 2×/year)
ecommerce_events.relevance: Adjacent: fashion/retail (ecommerce adjacent); not directly Amazon, but supply-chain network event

ecommerce_events.name: Bologna Fiere Events (BolognaFiere Congress Center)
ecommerce_events.date: Year-round; major fairs typically Feb–May, Sept–Nov
ecommerce_events.relevance: Adjacent: hospitality, food tech, packaging fairs. Not ecommerce-focused but supplier/logistics events.

weather.conditions: Cool-to-mild autumn/spring; rainy Oct–Nov & Jan–Feb; sunny Sept, May. Mild winters (0–8°C rarely freezing). Hot, humid Jul–Aug (28–35°C).

options.name: Piscina Sterlino (Historic outdoor pool, Murri district)
options.quality: Historic, well-maintained
options.transit_from_center: Bus ~20 min from Piazza Maggiore; 2 km walk/bike

options.name: Piscina Cavina (Indoor lap pool, city center)
options.quality: Modern, lap lanes
options.transit_from_center: Walking distance from historic center; day pass €8–10

options.name: Piscina Paolo Gori (Pianoro, suburb)
options.quality: Public municipal pool

options.name: Fitness First Bologna
options.facilities: Weights, cardio machines, group classes (spinning, yoga, pilates), pool access
options.notes: Modern multinational. English-friendly staff. Central location. Day pass €15; good for short stay. Monthly ~€50 (no annual registration required if <30 days stay).

options.name: Palestra BodyPlanet
options.facilities: Weights, cardio, indoor basketball, volleyball courts
options.notes: Large facility, all-ages programs. Day pass €10–12. Less formal than Fitness First; good value. Bring own lock + towel/robe (Italian gym culture).

options.name: Centro Universitario Sportivo 'RECORD' (University Sports Center)
options.notes: Student-oriented, very affordable. €8 day pass. Open to public. Less English spoken; Italian sign-up simple. Best budget option.

gym.running_options: Parco Urbano (urban parks along medieval walls): Parco dei Giardini Margherita (~2 km), flat, scenic, popular with joggers. Pedestrian/bike paths around Po river (~10 km north). Historic center cobblestones walkable but hard on joints.
gym.yoga_pilates: Bologna has several small yoga/pilates studios (e.g., YogaLab Santo Stefano, Pilates Studio Bologna). Classes €15–20/drop-in; English offerings limited but introductory classes friendly to beginners. Fitness First also offers studio classes.

day_trips.hiking: Apennine trails ~45 km south: Porretta Terme area, Val di Sole. Regional trains (Trenitalia) from Bologna Centrale to Porretta (~1.5 hours, €5–8). Moderate trails: Madonna di San Luca sanctuary ~3.8 km porticoed walk from city edge. Car-free via bus/train; popular with locals.

sailing.notes: Bologna is landlocked, ~120 km south of Adriatic. No sailing infrastructure. Nearest marinas: Rimini/Ravenna coast (train ~1.5–2 hours). Not a realistic option for 10-day stay.

new_hobbies.venue: Bottega di Ceramica (Faenza, ~40 km; UNESCO ceramics town)
new_hobbies.neighborhood: Day trip via train to Faenza (1 hour from Bologna Central)
new_hobbies.language: Italian with gestures; some instructors speak English
new_hobbies.notes: Faenza is Italy's ceramic heart. Intro workshops (2–3 hours) €40–60. Can combine with pottery museum tour. Train ~€5 return. Aligns with Riza's maker/craft interest (n8n, AI building). Tangible creative output unique to 10-day trip.

new_hobbies.venue: Co-working maker spaces (e.g., Fablab Bologna, occasional workshops)
new_hobbies.notes: Bologna has small maker/DIY scene. Intro sessions in wood design ~€20–30. Not as strong as pottery/ceramics focus but aligns with indie-hacker mentality. Check local Meetup/Lu.ma for ad-hoc workshops.

new_hobbies.venue: Consorteria dell'Aceto Balsamico Tradizionale (Modena, 40 km)
new_hobbies.language: Italian; English tours available with advance notice
new_hobbies.notes: Modena is UNESCO balsamic vinegar capital. Intro tours + tasting ~€30–40. Train €7–10 return. Combines food culture (Riza enjoys black tea, appreciates craft) with regional culinary education. Pairs well with Bologna's food focus.

new_hobbies.venue: Local art schools (e.g., Accademia di Belle Arti, occasional open studios)
new_hobbies.notes: Bologna's art school scene offers experimental workshops. Italian calligraphy tradition strong. Intro sessions ~€15–25. Appealing to design-minded indie builders. Check Lu.ma/local galleries for popup classes.

new_hobbies.hobby: Swing dancing / Lindy hop (complementary to bachata/salsa interest)
new_hobbies.venue: Auditorium Manzoni, local dance studios (swing scene emerging)
new_hobbies.notes: Bologna has smaller swing/lindy hop community vs. salsa (which is strong). Intro group classes ~€12–15. Complements Riza's bachata/salsa interest; good cross-training in partner dance. Check local Meetup for 'swing' or 'lindy' events.

coworking_retreats.notes: No major curated coworking retreats (Feel.travel, Outsite, Coworkations, NomadX, Sun and Co, Mokrin House, Work Wanderers) operate in Bologna proper as of May 2026. Nearby retreats possible in Tuscany/Umbria (1–2 hours by train), but outside scope of 10-day Bologna stay. Lo Spazio and The Social Hub (see work_spots) offer daily/weekly coworking but not curated group retreats. For future longer stays, investigate Italian DNV (Digital Nomad Visa) and multi-week coworking passes.

car_free.walkability: Historic center is entirely walkable (~2 km across). Medieval streets, porticoed arcades throughout, pedestrian zones. Flat terrain. ~20 min to walk from Piazza Maggiore to any coworking space.
car_free.public_transport: Excellent city buses (ATC—Agenzia della Trasporto Urbano). Single ticket €2.50 (or €2.30 at tabacchi). 10-ticket carnet €14. Day pass €6. Monthly €35 (student/young person €27). Buses run 5:30am–midnight; some 24h lines. Never needed for 10-day stay if central hotel; buses cover airport (Aerobus BUS) €8 one-way from Marconi Airport, ~25 min.
car_free.airport_to_center: Marconi Airport (BLQ) ~4 km north. Aerobus direct to Bologna Centrale station (~25 min, €8 one-way). Taxi ~€20–25 flat rate. City buses (line 25, 35) also available (~€2.50, 40 min with stops). Trains from Centrale to city center free walk (10 min) or buses. Staying in historic center eliminates need for onward transport; walking only.

startup_community.scene_summary: Bologna's tech scene is real but modest compared to Milan. Anchored by University of Bologna's strong engineering/CS departments (~16k students; 10% international) and Emilia-Romagna's manufacturing heritage (IoT, robotics). Few breakout SaaS startups; more maker/DIY/university spinoffs. Coworking spaces host regular tech meetups, demo nights. Founders tend to be students or post-academic. Scene is inclusive but small—you'll recognize faces at multiple events.

key_venues.name: Lo Spazio Coworking
key_venues.what: Tech-forward coworking hub. Hosts regular meetups (Amazon events mentioned), demo nights, startup pitch sessions. Vibrant community board.

key_venues.name: The Social Hub Bologna
key_venues.what: International community space. Networking events, workshops, skill-share sessions. Many founders and remote workers. 'Dynamic and international atmosphere' per reviews.

key_venues.name: Working Capital Accelerator
key_venues.what: Semi-formal accelerator (EU-backed). Hosts demo days, founder meetups, office hours. Lean early-stage focus.

key_venues.name: Kilowatt (Giardini Margherita)
key_venues.what: Arts + tech crossover space in regenerated public park. Workshops, exhibitions, maker culture. Good for indie builders and hardware-curious developers.

startup_community.recurring_events: Check Lu.ma, Meetup.com for: 'Bologna Tech Meetup,' 'StartUp Emilia-Romagna,' university engineering seminars (open to public), coworking demo nights (typically 1st/3rd Thu of month). Frequency ~2–4 events/month.

bonus.flight_from_vie: Vienna (VIE) → Bologna (BLQ): ~1.5 hours direct (Ryanair, Wizz Air, etc.) or ~2.5 hours with 1 connect (Swiss, Lufthansa). Typical fares €30–80 one-way low-cost. Return trip from VIE ~€60–150 round-trip in shoulder season (Sept–Nov, Jan–Mar).

timezones.vs_dubai: −3 (Dubai GMT+4, Bologna CEST+2 in summer; −4 in winter CET/EDT overlap)

bonus.classical_music: Bologna has strong classical tradition. Teatro Comunale (city opera house, Piazza Verdi) hosts concerts, operas, ballet (Sept–June season; summer quiet). Philharmonic Orchestra Toscanini performs regularly (€10–40 tickets). Churches offer free/cheap classical vespers (e.g., Basilica di San Petronio). University often has student recitals (free). Season (fall/winter) richer; May offers outdoor concerts in parks. Book in advance for major performances.
bonus.dance_scene: Bachata/salsa very active. Multiple dance schools: ScuolaDiDanza.it lists 10+ salsa/bachata venues. Classes €10–15/drop-in; socials/milongas weekends in clubs (€8–12 entry). Clubs: Caffeine (salsa Wed–Fri), Centro Direzionale venues. Strong university student scene = vibrant nightlife. Thursday/Friday best nights.

concerns: No sailing (landlocked). Nearest sea ~120 km; unrealistic for 10-day sprint.
Coworking has limited phone booths in some spaces (BAAK, Le Serre lack them; Lo Spazio has them). Budget for 1–2 dedicated coworking days/week for sensitive client video calls.

Winter (Dec–Feb) temperatures drop to 0–5°C, raining frequently. Swimming indoor-only; outdoor pools closed. Classical music season peaks; cafes quieter mid-winter. Consider shoulder months (Sept–Nov, Mar–Apr) for better experience.
Tech startup scene is 'good' not 'excellent'—limited high-growth exits or venture presence vs. Milan. Indie-hacker + university spinoff vibe more prominent. If major VC/scale is priority, reconsider.
Coworking retreat scene absent in Bologna. If group cohort experience desired, extend trip to Tuscany or investigate Italian DNV for longer co-living stays elsewhere.
August (July–Aug) extremely hot (28–35°C), humid, touristy. Many restaurants/shops close mid-Aug. Avoid if possible.

summary: Corfu is a compelling choice for Riza: strong coworking infrastructure (Silicon Urban Ecosystem with 250 Mbps Starlink), affordable 7-day accommodation well within €2,000, and a growing digital nomad community. The island combines reliable work conditions in Corfu Town with exceptional sailing, swimming, and outdoor activities. Key concern: inconsistent internet outside urban areas, but central locations solve this.
remote_work_pitch: Corfu has emerged as a serious digital nomad hub with dedicated coworking (Silicon Urban Ecosystem, opened 2023), reliable fiber/Starlink in town, and laptop-friendly cafes like Fat Cat on the remote-work corridor. Unlike many Greek islands, Corfu offers year-round services, a tight-knit English-speaking community, and direct airport access. It's ideal for client video calls (phone booths available) and pairs work with unbeatable sailing conditions (May–October) and hiking.
best_months: May, June, September, October (shoulder season: warm, not crowded, ideal sailing). November–March quieter but wetter; July–August peak tourism.

internet.fixed_mbps: 250 (Starlink at Silicon), 20–50 (fiber in Corfu Town), variable outside
internet.notes: Fiber-optic in Corfu Town and Gouvia; Starlink at Silicon Urban Ecosystem reaches 250 Mbps. Cafes average 20 Mbps. Rural areas unreliable—stay central.

work_spots.name: Silicon Urban Ecosystem
work_spots.notes: Greece's first dedicated coworking space (opened Nov 2023). Starlink 250 Mbps, soundproof phone booths, 6–10 person meeting rooms, self-serve café, 24/7 private offices. Highly rated by digital nomads.

work_spots.name: Fat Cat Coffee n' More
work_spots.neighborhood: Corfu Town, Donatou Dimoulitsa (near Saroko Square)
work_spots.notes: Top-rated work cafe on remote-work directories. Abundant power outlets, big tables, U-Roast coffee, waffles, sandwiches, pizzas, vegan milk. Board games. Walking distance to Silicon.

work_spots.name: The Venetian Well
work_spots.notes: Picturesque square with reliable WiFi, charming Venetian architecture, waterfront vibes. Good for a few hours; not optimal for full workday, but high-quality atmosphere.

work_spots.name: Imabari Beachfront Cafe (Ypsos)
work_spots.neighborhood: Ypsos (15 min north of Corfu Town by bus)
work_spots.notes: Beachfront setting with sand-toes vibe. WiFi adequate but less reliable than town; best for breaks/inspiration rather than critical calls. Seasonal hours.

stays.name: Hotel Bretagne
stays.why: Central, reliable 3-star hotel well below budget. Good WiFi, clean rooms, walking distance to Silicon and work cafes. Allows 7-night stay comfortably within €2,000 total (stay + coworking + food).

stays.name: Eleals Boutique Hotel
stays.why: Highly rated boutique with design detail, excellent service. Central location. €1,015 for 7 nights + €150–200 coworking + €300–400 food = ~€1,500 total, well within budget.

stays.name: Albergo Botti
stays.why: 3-star boutique in the heart of town. Continental breakfast, free WiFi, concierge. Lowest-cost option; total 7 days ~€950–1,100 all-in. Excellent value.

visa.details: Turkish citizen with Slovak Pobyt (EU residence permit) is visa-free in Greece for 90 days under Schengen. No restrictions. Turkey-EU bilateral rules apply; can extend via visa residency if staying 30+ days, but short 7-day visits need no visa.

air_water_safety.air_quality_notes: Corfu benefits from Ionian Sea breezes. No major industrial pollution. AQI typically 30–50 (Good). Air quality best May–October; winter slightly elevated due to heating.
air_water_safety.safety_notes: Corfu Town is safe for solo night walks; petty theft rare. Avoid isolated areas at night. Tourist-friendly, low violent crime. Standard urban caution applies.

weather.conditions: Mediterranean; mild winters, warm summers, more rainfall than Aegean islands (lush greenery)

options.name: Paleokastritsa Beach (west coast)
options.quality: Crystal-clear, turquoise waters, dramatic cliffs. Popular, can be crowded in summer.
options.transit_from_center: 30 min bus from Corfu Town center (KTEL bus)

options.name: Glyfada Beach (west)
options.quality: Long sandy beach, calm waters, family-friendly. Less crowded than Paleokastritsa.

options.name: Kassiopi Beach (north coast)
options.quality: Pebble/sand mix, clear waters, charming village harbor. Quieter than southern beaches.

options.name: Angsana Corfu Resort & Spa (hotel pool, guest access available)
options.quality: 25m heated indoor pool, seasonal outdoor pools. 5-star facility.

options.name: Nikoll Fitness Center
options.notes: Central, affordable. Standard gym; no frills but functional for lifting + cardio.

options.name: InAction Healthstudio
options.facilities: Group classes, Pilates Reformer, pool, gymnastics
options.notes: Full-service with pool. Pilates strong. English-friendly staff. Good variety.

options.name: The Lifting Room
options.facilities: Strength (free weights, machines), cardio, 24/7 access to members
options.notes: Modern, members-only. Minimal supervision, self-service. Best for serious lifters. Day pass via email code.

gym.running_options: Corfu Trail (long-distance footpath across island, excellent scenic route). Mon-Repos park (26 min walk from town center) has beautiful tree-lined paths. Seafront Spianada promenade (town center, 1 km flat loop).
gym.yoga_pilates: Pilates Center (Mantzarou 13, Corfu Town) – The Original Pilates Center, reformer focus, English classes available. InAction offers group yoga classes. Pricing ~€15–20/class or €60–80/month for packages.

day_trips.hiking: Corfu Trail (56 km north–south; accessible by day sections via bus to trailheads). Mount Pantokrator (906m, highest point; 3–4 hr hike, scenic views to Italy/Albania). Angelokastro fortress walk (2 hr loop, northwest coast). All accessible by public transport or short taxi.

marinas.name: Gouvia Marina
marinas.location: East coast, 15 min north of Corfu Town
marinas.transit_from_center: Bus no. 7 from airport/town (30 min); 15 min by taxi (€15–20)

marinas.name: Corfu Sailing Club Marina
marinas.transit_from_center: Walking distance from town center (5 min)

schools.name: Seven Seas Sailing School (partnered with BorrowABoat)

sailing.best_season: May–October. Peak: July–Aug (crowded). Shoulder: May–Jun, Sep–Oct (ideal: warm, fewer boats, afternoon breezes 10–15 knots, sea temp 23–25°C).
sailing.notes: Ionian Sea calm waters, reliable Maestral afternoon winds. North-westerly summer winds. Corfu is gateway to Paxos, Antipaxos, Lefkas, Kefalonia. Charter companies (Istion Yachting, Sunsail, Dream Yacht Charter, Sailogy, Sailo, SamBoat) all based at Gouvia. Bareboat requires ICC or equivalent certification.

new_hobbies.venue: Local artisan studios in Corfu Town or Paleokastritsa (check local listings; not major scene)
new_hobbies.notes: Corfu has Venetian/Italian craft traditions. Pottery classes less prominent than major pottery hubs (e.g., Florence, Seville) but available via private instructors or cultural centers. Recommend asking Silicon coworking staff for local recommendations.

new_hobbies.venue: PADI/CMAS dive centers (Corfu Divers, Nautilus Diving) at Kassiopi/Paleokastritsa
new_hobbies.notes: Ionian Sea clear waters, shallow reefs. Freediving certification courses (SSI, CMAS) available. Calmer than Aegean. Summer peak; winter less busy. Great for Riza's swimming/water interests.

new_hobbies.venue: Private tutors or cultural institutes in Corfu Town (Italy Next Door, local language centers)
new_hobbies.notes: Corfu's Venetian heritage & proximity to Italy (40 km) make Italian exposure natural. Private lessons €20–30/hr. Café/street Italian common. Complements Riza's B1 German path.

new_hobbies.venue: Aghios Georgios beach or Ipsos Bay (water sports centers)
new_hobbies.notes: Summer afternoon winds ideal. Ipsos Bay shallow, safe for lessons. Equipment rental + instruction ~€60–100/session. Corfu less famous than Tarifa or Vassiliki but solid conditions May–Sep.

new_hobbies.venue: Paleokastritsa / Kassiopi kayak tours & rentals
new_hobbies.notes: Calm Ionian waters perfect for kayaking. Explore sea caves, secluded coves. Rental + half-day guided tour ~€50–80. Pairs well with swimming/sailing interests.

coworking_retreats.notes: No curated coworking retreat operators (Feel.travel, Coworkations, Outsite, Mokrin House, etc.) currently active in or based near Corfu as of May 2026. Silicon Urban Ecosystem is the primary dedicated space; private Airbnb + coworking membership is standard model here. Greece's retreat scene concentrates in Athens and Crete.

car_free.walkability: Corfu Town center is very walkable; Spianada, Liston, old quarter all pedestrian-friendly. Beach areas require bus/taxi. Silicon coworking is walking distance from many central hotels.
car_free.public_transport: KTEL bus system covers island well. Reliable, cheap (single trip ~€1.50, monthly pass ~€35). Buses run 6am–midnight. Taxis available; meter or agreed fare (~€15–30 in-town).
car_free.airport_to_center: Bus from Corfu International Airport (CFU) to downtown (20 min, ~€3) or taxi (€30–35, 15 min). Ferry from Italy (Ancona/Trieste) arrives in Corfu Town harbor (walkable).

startup_community.scene_summary: Corfu has a small but growing digital nomad community (Work From Greece platform, local Meetup groups). No major tech/venture scene—nothing like Athens. Startup activity minimal. Strength is remote workers and freelancers, not founders/investors. Community is tight-knit and welcoming to solo builders (Riza's indie-hacker/AI profile fits well).

key_venues.name: Silicon Urban Ecosystem
key_venues.what: Primary hub for digital nomads & remote workers. Networking events, seminars, workshops. Community-building focus.

key_venues.name: Work From Greece (Corfu chapter)
key_venues.what: Informal meetups for digital nomads, remote workers, expats. Online + offline events. Slack community.

key_venues.name: Fat Cat Coffee n' More
key_venues.what: Unofficial work hub; nomads congregate. Community vibe, not formal events.

startup_community.recurring_events: Work From Greece monthly meetups (check Lu.ma / Meetup). Silicon occasionally hosts workshops/seminars for members. No demo nights or formal startup events.

bonus.flight_from_vie: Vienna (VIE) → Corfu (CFU): direct flight ~2h 20m with Ryanair/Wizz Air, or 1 stop (via Athens/Munich) ~4–6h. ~€50–150 budget airline. Ferry from Ancona/Trieste (Italy) also option (~24–48h, scenic, €80–120). Landing: CFU airport, 15 min bus to town.
bonus.classical_music: Corfu Philharmonic Society (historic, founded 1850s). Occasional concerts in town center venues. Orthodox churches host Byzantine chant services. No world-class concert hall, but UNESCO old town creates ambient charm. Summer festivals occasionally feature classical performances.
bonus.dance_scene: Bachata/salsa: no dedicated scene. Nightlife in Kavos (south) and Corfu Town waterfront (bars, clubs) but mostly pop/electronic/Greek music. Latin dance classes rare; private tutors possible via inquiry. Not a salsa hub like major Spanish/Latin cities.

concerns: Internet outside Corfu Town & Gouvia unreliable; rural areas challenging for video calls. Mitigation: stay central (Saroko/Liston), use Silicon.

Co-working scene nascent (Silicon only dedicated space opened 2023); limited serendipitous startup/tech networking. Good for focus; weak for community. Mitigate: join Work From Greece meetups, use Fat Cat.
Summer (July–Aug) very crowded & pricey; accommodation harder to book. Recommendation: visit May–Jun or Sep–Oct (shoulder season).
Salsa/bachata scene minimal; not ideal for dance hobbyists (though general nightlife exists). Consider Lisbon/Barcelona/Madrid for this.

No Greek digital nomad visa boost (EU citizens don't need it; DNV tax incentive requires non-EU work). As Turkish citizen with Slovak residence, Riza gets standard 90-day visa-free, not special tax status.

sources.what: Community reviews, facilities, work environment feedback

sources.what: Fat Cat Coffee n' More specs, WiFi speed, hours, power outlets; 5 curated work cafes

sources.what: Corfu internet assessment, cost of living (€400–650/month rent), infrastructure critique, digital nomad visa context

sources.what: Nikoll Fitness Center, Pilates Center, Runner Gym, InAction addresses & contacts

summary: Lombok—specifically Kuta—is an emerging remote-work haven that checks Riza's boxes: reliable fiber internet (30–50 Mbps per device), dedicated coworking spaces with phone booths on the horizon, and strong video-call infrastructure. Unlike overcrowded Bali, it stays peaceful while offering swimming, sailing, and world-class hiking nearby. A 7-day stay fits comfortably within €2,000, leaving headroom for leisure and adventure. Turkey-to-Lombok visa runs through Indonesia's 30-day Visa on Arrival (extendable once)—no pre-approval needed. Best for April–May and September–October when weather stays dry and crowds thin.
remote_work_pitch: Kuta Lombok has leapfrogged Bali on infrastructure: coworking spaces now offer fiber connections, backup power, and Starlink redundancy. You get a professional work environment during business hours—critical for 5+ hour client calls spanning Europe, UK, and Middle East timezones—without sacrificing island lifestyle. The indie-hacker and digital-nomad scene is still small but tight, and the internet quality ranks in Southeast Asia's top tier as of 2026. After hours: sailing, Mount Rinjani hiking, and salsa at local venues keep burnout at bay.
best_months: May–June, September–October (dry season, minimal crowds, excellent weather)

internet.notes: Fiber coworking spaces (The Spot, South Lombok Cowork, KARIA) provide 1,200 Mbps aggregate (limited to 50 Mbps per device). Backup generators + UPS standard. Local SIM (Telkomsel/XL) 4G fallback. Storms can briefly interrupt service; plan offline work or use mobile hotspot.

work_spots.name: The Spot
work_spots.best_for: long focus, video calls, late night work (accommodates timezone spread)
work_spots.notes: Fiber + Starlink backup, 50 Mbps per device, calm garden setting, on-site restaurant. Network architect owner ensures enterprise-grade infrastructure. No dedicated phone booth yet but quiet focus rooms. AC, desks, power.

work_spots.name: South Lombok Cowork
work_spots.neighborhood: Kuta, Jalan Batu Riti (Mandalika Bay views)
work_spots.best_for: professional calls, beachfront focus, full-day sprints
work_spots.notes: Fast WiFi, AC, monitors on request, meeting room (printers, coffee). Ocean view terrace. Best for structured 9–5 work; weekend/evening access requires membership. Phone booth on roadmap.

work_spots.name: KARIA (TIKA Lombok)
work_spots.hours: 24/7 for members; day pass hours TBD
work_spots.best_for: budget nomads, 24/7 access for timezone juggling, all-levels community
work_spots.notes: Newest, most affordable dedicated coworking. Shared desks, ergonomic seating, unlimited tea/coffee. Digital-nomad focused. No phone booth; small desk dividers provide mild privacy. Part of TIKA Hotels complex (accommodation co-located).

work_spots.name: Loka Gym Café
work_spots.best_for: morning meetings, social work, post-gym sessions
work_spots.notes: Fast WiFi, power outlets, open-air relaxed vibe. Integrated with Loka Gym & Recovery facility (sauna, ice baths). Great for morning calls, social co-working; not ideal for solo 5+ hour focused sessions. Occasional noise from gym.

stays.name: Sikara Lombok Hotel
stays.why: Budget-friendly 3–4-star with modern rooms, AC, flat-screen TV. Mountain/sea views, pool. 9.6 rating (Booking). No kitchen, but central location near restaurants/cafes. Stays under €2k budget easily with headroom for coworking/food.

stays.name: El Tropico Boutique Hotel
stays.why: Boutique charm: outdoor pool, restaurant, terrace. Close to Kuta Beach for morning swims. Friendly staff. Good mid-range option balancing comfort & budget (still €1,566 remaining for coworking + food).

stays.name: Loka Village (Boutique Bungalows)
stays.neighborhood: Kuta, quiet area 800m from Loka Gym
stays.why: Bungalows with ensuite, AC, hot water, mini fridge. Kitchen on-site (cook your own meals = cost savings). Working desk, good WiFi. Integrated with Loka Gym (guest discount on day passes). Perfect for fitness-focused nomads. Quieter setting for focused work.

visa.details: Turkish citizens can obtain Visa on Arrival (VOA) at Lombok International Airport: 30 days for ~$35 USD (IDR 500k), extendable once for another 30 days (60 days total, ~$70 USD). No pre-approval needed. Slovak Pobyt residence permit does not grant visa-free entry to Indonesia—Turkish passport controls. VOA is tourism-only (no profit-making); remote work for foreign employer is tolerated in practice. For 7-day trip, single VOA sufficient. If extending beyond 30 days later, apply B211A (Tourist Visa) in advance via Indonesia eVisa portal (USD 100–250, extendable to 180 days).

air_water_safety.air_quality_notes: AQI typically 25–75 (Moderate–Good). Coastal breeze. Occasional haze during dry-season farm burns (July–Sep); interior/highland valleys are worse. No major pollution. Monitor iQAIR/WAQI if sensitive.
air_water_safety.safety_notes: Kuta is safe for solo night walks, well-lit main areas. Petty theft/pickpocketing rare but possible in crowds (Gili ferry, markets). Scams uncommon in coworking/restaurant zones. Avoid isolated beaches at night. Respect local Islamic customs (modest dress away from beach). Drug laws strict (zero tolerance). Overall safer than Bali's party zones. Women travelers report feeling comfortable.

ecommerce_events.name: Lombok Marketplace & Digital Nomad Meetups
ecommerce_events.relevance: Adjacent—indie hackers, remote workers, founders. Small but active. Hosted at coworking spaces (The Spot, KARIA).

weather.conditions: Tropical; dry season (May–Oct) sunny, <20mm rain/month; wet season (Nov–Mar) 100+ mm/month, brief afternoon downpours

options.name: Tanjung Aan Beach
options.quality: Crystal clear, white sand, calm (south coast). Best dry season.

options.name: Mawun Beach
options.quality: Quieter bay, soft white sand, protected, stunning. 15 min west of Kuta.

options.name: Hotel pools (Sikara, El Tropico, Loka)
options.quality: Well-maintained, chlorinated, safe. On-site at accommodations.

options.name: Xeno Fit
options.facilities: Free weights, machines, air-conditioned cardio room, boxing bags, climbing wall, stretching areas, on-site café, coworking space, CrossFit/HYROX classes
options.notes: Newest, state-of-art. High energy. Affiliated with CrossFit & HYROX. Excellent post-work recovery environment. English-speaking staff. Day pass €14, month €85 (great value for Riza's lifting+cardio routine).

options.name: Loka Gym & Loka Recovery
options.neighborhood: Kuta, main road (integrated with Loka café)
options.facilities: Free weights, machines, Muay Thai classes (4x/week, 3 trainers), Surf Strength, Booty Builder, Olympic Weightlifting, Metcon, HIIT, sauna, 3 ice baths, magnesium pool, sparring area
options.notes: Recovery-focused (sauna, ice baths unique in Lombok). Strong community. Drop-in sessions for non-members on recovery facilities. English-friendly classes. Loka Sunday BBQ (7pm, 70k IDR ~€4.50). Great vibe for long-term builders.

options.name: Loka Village Gym (mini-gym on-site)
options.facilities: Basic weights, cardio, complementary for Loka Village guests; discount on main Loka Gym membership
options.notes: If staying at Loka Village, basic gym on-site. Partner discount at main Loka facility (€10–12/day guest rate).

gym.running_options: Beach runs (Tanjung Aan, Mawun) at sunrise/sunset. Quiet main road in Kuta early morning. Town to beach ~2km flat. Mount Rinjani base trails (30km north) for serious distance.
gym.yoga_pilates: Loka offers functional fitness classes (Booty Builder, etc.). Kenza (restaurant/yoga retreat, Gili Air satellite) offers yoga classes; limited English. Yoga studios scattered in Kuta (check Google Maps). Market is underdeveloped vs. Bali.

day_trips.hiking: Mount Rinjani (3,726m volcano, 2–3 day trek from Senaru village trailhead, ~50km north; public transport via bemo or taxi). One-day crater rim hike possible. Tetebatu Monkey Forest (lush jungle, waterfall, 1–2 hours north by scooter). Sendang Gile & Tiu Kelep waterfalls (north coast, swimming pools, 2 hour drive). All doable as day excursions or overnight treks.

sailing.notes: Lombok is a major sailing hub. Sunset cruises (2–3 hours, €50–80 pp) widely available. Multi-day liveaboard charters common (€350–500/day full boat). Sea temps 27–28°C year-round. Gili Islands (10–20 min speedboat from Kuta) popular day trip. Komodo liveaboards depart Lombok. Small local sailing scene (not Bali-scale). Wind W-E winter (May–Sep) good for sailing; monsoon currents strong Dec–Jan.

new_hobbies.venue: Gerupuk (right-hand breaks, beginner-friendly), Selong Belanak (mellow, sandy), Mawi Beach (advanced power waves)
new_hobbies.neighborhood: South Lombok coast, 10–25 min from Kuta
new_hobbies.notes: Lombok is a world-class surf destination. Intro lessons €40–60. Spot-sharing less crowded than Bali. Consistent swells May–Sep. Riza can combine gym strength training with surf (Loka offers Surf Strength classes). Water temp 27–28°C year-round, minimal gear needed.

new_hobbies.venue: Gili Islands (Gili Trawangan, Gili Air, Gili Meno), Senggigi Reef, Belongas Bay
new_hobbies.neighborhood: Gili Islands 20–30 min by speedboat; Senggigi 45 min north; Belongas 1 hour south
new_hobbies.notes: PADI centers abundant (Divine Divers, Scuba Froggy, Lutwala Dive). Intro dive €90–120. 20–30m visibility dry season. Coral gardens, turtles, sharks. Day-trip snorkeling €35–50. Spearfishing/freediving also popular locally.

new_hobbies.venue: Sasak Villages (Rambitan, Sade Traditional Weaving Village; Tetebateau area)
new_hobbies.neighborhood: Central/inland Lombok, 30–60 min by scooter from Kuta
new_hobbies.notes: Lombok's indigenous Sasak culture centers on hand-weaving and pottery. Half-day village tours €25–40 include workshop time. Unlike pottery in Florence or Seville, this is living village craft (less touristy than Bali). Great for afternoon cultural immersion away from Kuta hustle.

new_hobbies.venue: Mount Rinjani foothills (Kali Kode Bunut area) or south coast cliffs (Mawun)
new_hobbies.notes: Nascent paragliding scene in Lombok. Introductory tandem flights €120–180 from scenic highlands. Dry season (May–Sep) best. Not as established as Bali or Interlaken, but unique experience. Growing community post-2024.

new_hobbies.venue: Loka Gym (on-site classes), standalone gyms
new_hobbies.notes: Loka Gym offers 4x/week Muay Thai classes (beginner, intermediate) with 3 specialized trainers—unique draw. Drop-in €15/class. Strong local boxing/Muay Thai culture (common in Indonesia). Great complement to Riza's gym routine and bachelor-level fitness goals.

coworking_retreats.notes: Lombok lacks curated co-working retreat operators (Feel.travel, Coworkations, Outsite, Sun and Co, NomadX, Mokrin House, Work Wanderers do not run dedicated programs here as of May 2026). The island is too emerging for such structured retreats. TIKA Lombok and The Spot offer co-living/hybrid setups but are permanent properties, not time-limited retreats. Bali, Chiang Mai, and Bangkok dominate the retreat market. Lombok remains a DIY nomad base: book a coworking membership + hotel/villa directly.

car_free.walkability: Kuta town center (main drag) is walkable for cafes, restaurants, beaches (5–10 min loop). Coworking spaces clustered within 1–2km, reachable on foot or 5-min scooter. Beyond ~2km (Senggigi, Mawun, Tanjung Aan), scooter/taxi required. No uber, but official taxis, informal bike taxis, and scooter rentals cheap (€2–5/day scooter, €10–15 taxi short trip).
car_free.public_transport: Bemo (minibus) system covers inter-town routes (Kuta ↔ Mataram ↔ Senggigi ↔ airport) for €0.50–2. Infrequent (30–60 min headways), crowded, but very cheap. No fixed schedules; flag down or ask locals. Day pass: ~€5 for all-day bemo hopping. Modern buses planned but limited as of 2026. Hiring a driver/scooter more practical for nomads.
car_free.airport_to_center: Lombok International Airport (LOP) ~25km south of Kuta. Official taxis €16–20 (~25 min drive). Ride-hailing (Gojek Indonesia app) cheaper (~€8–12) but variable. Bemo from airport area slower but €2–3 option. Pre-arrange hotel pickup (often free or €10 fee).

startup_community.scene_summary: Lombok's tech/startup scene is nascent. No major VC presence, no unicorns, no monthly demo nights. The digital-nomad community is tighter than the local startup ecosystem—most 'founders' are indie hackers, SaaS solopreneurs, and remote consultants. Co-working spaces host informal meetups (1st Wed, Nomad List). Expat/founder density is *low* vs. Bali or Chiang Mai. However, the indie-hacker vibe (Claude API, n8n builders) does find home here; quieter, lower cost of living attracts builders who value focus over networking.

key_venues.name: The Spot Coworking
key_venues.what: Hub for digital nomads, founders, freelancers. Monthly meetups, informal networking. Owner (network architect) well-connected locally.

key_venues.name: KARIA Coworking (TIKA)
key_venues.what: Newer, nomad-focused. Casual community events, daily co-working vibe. Growing DIY startup hangout.

key_venues.name: Loka Gym Sunday BBQ
key_venues.what: Weekly (Sunday 7pm) expat/nomad gathering (€4.50 all-you-can-eat). Casual networking. Mix of builders, remote workers, digital nomads.

startup_community.recurring_events: 1st Wednesday monthly: Nomad List / meetup.com digital nomad meetup (venue rotates: The Spot, KARIA, Loka). Loka Sunday BBQ (weekly 7pm, social + networking). No formal demo nights, pitch events, or founder circles.

bonus.flight_from_vie: Direct: Vienna (VIE) → Lombok (LOP) not available; usually 1–2 stops (e.g., via Jakarta/Denpasar). ~16–20 hours total (incl. layover). Example: VIE → Doha → Denpasar → Lombok (Qatar Airways/garuda) ~€420–650 return mid-season, €300–400 low-season (Nov–March). Bali (DPS) is closer (15 hours), then ferry/flight to Lombok (+2–3 hours, €15–30). Book 6–8 weeks ahead for best fares.
bonus.classical_music: Classical music venues are absent in Lombok. No concert halls, symphony orchestras, or dedicated classical spaces (vs. Vienna Staatsoper, Musikverein). Occasional expat-organized chamber performances at resorts or private venues; word-of-mouth only. Bali (45 min ferry) has Neka Museum (occasional classical events) and resort recitals. Riza would need to embrace local gamelan (Indonesian orchestral music) or accept a 7-day break from live classical. Streaming via coworking WiFi is reliable fallback.
bonus.dance_scene: Bachata/Salsa: Limited formal scene. No dedicated dance studios or weekly salsa nights (unlike Bali's Club Med or Seminyak bars). However, expat/nomad community occasionally organizes informal salsa gatherings (check Facebook groups: 'Lombok Expats,' 'Lombok Digital Nomads'). Some bars (Kuta main drag) play Latin music on weekends and tolerate informal dancing. Tetebatu area has occasional cultural dance performances (Sasak traditional). Riza would rely on self-practice or organizing peer sessions at Loka/coworking. Less ideal than dedicated salsa cities (e.g., Cali, Havana), but doable for hobbyist.

concerns: No dedicated phone booths in coworking spaces yet (roadmap at South Lombok Cowork). For video calls requiring isolation, book private meeting room or use hotel room.
Classical music venues absent; Riza may need to accept 7-day break or rely on streaming.
Bachata/salsa scene underdeveloped; no weekly classes or formal clubs. Expat organizing required.
Scooter rentals encouraged by nomads, but Riza is car-free. Walkability limited beyond 2km; relies on taxis/bemos (cheap, safe, but slow/infrequent).
Wet season (Nov–Mar) brings tropical downpours, high humidity, and rare equipment issues (rare blackouts). Plan work offline or backup mobile hotspot.
Startup/founder ecosystem very weak vs. Bali/Chiang Mai; limited professional networking for Amazon ecommerce peers. Indie hacker scene is strong, not B2B ecommerce.
German proficiency: Staff in coworking/hotels speak functional English, minimal German. Not an issue for Riza (B1 German), but no German-speaking community.
