summary: One of the world's great capitals: unmatched culture, food, museums, classical music, and a serious tech/startup ecosystem (Station F). Schengen (counts toward 90/180). Expensive and intense but a peerless work-and-network base, 0h from Vienna and a short direct flight.
remote_work_pitch: Paris is what it says on the tin: museums, café terraces, world-class classical music, a deep tech/ecommerce scene (Station F is Europe's biggest startup campus), and the centre of French business gravity. For a recon it's a peerless work-and-network base on the Continent; same time zone as Vienna, direct flight from VIE, and walkability and metro density that few cities match. The trade-offs are real: it's expensive, intense, and your days count toward Schengen 90/180.
best_months: May-June and September-October (best); mild April/Nov; cold damp winter, hot summer

internet.notes: World-class fibre/5G, call-reliable, abundant call-friendly coworking

work_spots.name: Station F / WeWork / Anticafé
work_spots.notes: Station F is Europe's largest startup campus; abundant premium coworking citywide, booths

work_spots.name: Marais / Canal Saint-Martin specialty cafes
work_spots.notes: Strong third-wave cafe scene (KB CaféShop, Boot Café), laptop-friendly

stays.name: Marais / 11e apartment
stays.why: Walkable central, cafe and metro density unmatched, classic Haussmann buildings

stays.name: Canal Saint-Martin / 10e
stays.why: Hipper, slightly cheaper, great cafes and a creative-young energy

visa.details: France is full Schengen — seamless on your Slovak Pobyt, BUT days COUNT toward your 90/180. For long-term residence, the Profession Libérale visa (~€1,766/month income threshold) is the route for freelancers; since June 2025, remote work is officially prohibited even on the visitor visa, so the PL visa is the proper path for anything beyond 90 days.

air_water_safety.safety_notes: Generally safe; standard big-city awareness for pickpockets at tourist sites and metro. Tap water drinkable

ecommerce_events.name: Vivatech, Paris Retail Week, ecommerce conferences

weather.conditions: Mild oceanic; warm summer, cold damp winter, frequent grey

options.name: Piscine Pontoise, Joséphine Baker (floating Seine pool), Molitor
options.quality: Excellent municipal pools (the 60-pool network is one of Europe's best); no sea

options.name: Neoness / On Air / Fitness Park
options.notes: Good chain network at decent value

gym.running_options: Seine quais (now mostly car-free), Buttes-Chaumont, Tuileries-to-Louvre, Bois de Vincennes/Boulogne

day_trips.hiking: Forêt de Fontainebleau (a one-hour train, world-class boulders and forest), Versailles, Chartres, Giverny; the Loire Valley further

sailing.notes: Inland (the Seine is for cruises not sailing); coastal sailing is on the Atlantic/Med, a TGV away

new_hobbies.hobby: Classical music, salsa, French, or tea culture
new_hobbies.venue: Philharmonie de Paris, La Bellevilloise / Cuban clubs, Alliance Française, Mariage Frères salons
new_hobbies.neighborhood: Cité de la Musique / Belleville / Marais
new_hobbies.notes: World-class classical (Philharmonie); thriving salsa/bachata scene; Mariage Frères is the world tea-house benchmark (Marais)

coworking_retreats.notes: Business/startup coworking density is the offer; not a retreat-nomad scene

car_free.walkability: Among the world's best — walk + metro + Vélib bikes + suburban RER; zero need for a car
car_free.public_transport: Iconic metro (Navigo Easy/Découverte) + RER + buses + bike-share; comprehensive and cheap
car_free.airport_to_center: CDG ~45min by RER/taxi (~55 EUR), ORY ~30min

startup_community.scene_summary: France's startup and ecommerce capital, Station F anchors a real ecosystem, strong VC (Partech, Eurazeo), English usable in tech. Excellent for work-and-network — especially valuable given your Amazon/ecommerce focus.

key_venues.name: Station F
key_venues.what: Europe's largest startup campus

bonus.flight_from_vie: Direct, ~2.5h, ~80-200 EUR — short and cheap
bonus.classical_music: Philharmonie de Paris, Opéra Garnier/Bastille — world-class, perhaps the best in continental Europe alongside Vienna and Berlin
bonus.dance_scene: Big salsa/bachata scene (La Bellevilloise, Cuban clubs)

concerns: Grey damp winters, brief hot summers

sources.name: Jobbatical / Citizen Remote
sources.what: Schengen rules and Profession Libérale visa for France

sources.name: Nomad List
sources.what: Cost/internet/scene baselines

summary: Seoul is an excellent fit for Riza—world-class internet, thriving AI/startup scene, affordable coworking (₩350k–500k/month), and strong backend services make it ideal for Amazon and AI side projects. Cost fits well within €2,000 for 7 days with proper accommodation choices. Visa-free for 90 days; sailing and classical music options exist but are limited.
remote_work_pitch: Seoul's ultra-fast broadband (100+ Mbps), multiple WeWork locations with phone booths, and a burgeoning AI ecosystem (Pangyo Techno Valley, Seoul AI Meetup, AAAI Summer Symposium June 2026) make it a top-tier remote hub for AI/indie builders. Coworking costs are reasonable (day passes €15–20), and the city's startup density rivals any Asian hub. Nine-hour timezone from Vienna works for async EU clients; overlap possible with Japan/Australia.
best_months: April–May (spring: mild weather, cherry blossoms), September–October (fall: clear skies, comfortable temps). Avoid July–August (heat, humidity) and December–February (cold, sparse outdoor options).

internet.notes: South Korea offers world-leading broadband. WeWork and coworking spaces guarantee fiber and redundancy. 5G coverage excellent. Call reliability strong for Zoom/phone booths.

work_spots.name: WeWork Gangnam Station
work_spots.notes: First Seoul WeWork location since 2016. Premium amenities: high-speed internet, coffee, printing, game room. Close to restaurants/shopping. Line 2 subway access.

work_spots.name: WeWork Seoul Square
work_spots.best_for: transit access, startup community, demo nights
work_spots.notes: Direct connection to APEX Airport Express, KTX, Line 1/4 metro. Game room, showers, outdoor space. Buzzing startup energy.

work_spots.name: Seoul Global Startup Center (SGSC)
work_spots.notes: Government-run (Ministry of SMEs & Startups). Free tables, meeting booths, telephone booths, libraries. Peaceful atmosphere. Visa support available. Primarily for startups but open public access.

work_spots.name: The Executive Centre Seoul (Gangnam or Yeouido)
work_spots.notes: Premium office buildings. Phone booths, conference spaces, high-speed WiFi, on-site support. Multiple Seoul locations (Jung-gu, Gangnam, Yeouido). Luxury finish.

stays.name: Orakai Insadong Suites
stays.why: Highly rated (8.4/10), indoor pool, full kitchen, central location near Gyeongbokgung Palace. Walkable to coworking and transport. €118/night fits budget; total 7 nights = €826, leaving €1,174 for coworking+food.

stays.name: Ever8 Serviced Residence (Sinchon)
stays.why: Budget-friendly (€106/night), full kitchen, washer/dryer, walkable to Sinchon Train Station and Yonsei University. 7 nights = €742, leaving €1,258 for coworking/food/transport.

stays.name: The Classic 500 Pentaz Executive Residence
stays.why: Luxury option (5-star, 8.2/10 rating), valet parking, spa lounge, near COEX/shopping. Premium but within €2,000 budget if coworking/food kept modest (€656 remaining). Great for longer stays if monthly rate negotiated.

visa.details: Turkish citizen with Slovak Pobyt residence permit gains EU/EEA visa-free travel. South Korea grants 90-day visa exemption on arrival. No visa needed for 7-day stay. For longer stays (30+ days), digital nomad visa (F-1-D) requires ₩88.1M annual income (~€66k), private health insurance ₩100M coverage, and proof of remote work for foreign employer. Short-term tourist visa (90 days) easiest for this duration.

air_water_safety.air_quality_notes: AQI typically 30–60 (Good/Moderate) in May and September–October. Peaks December–March (dust/pollution). Late spring/early fall best. Real-time tracking via AirKorea app.
air_water_safety.safety_notes: Seoul ranks among world's safest cities. Low violent crime, excellent street lighting, 24/7 public transit. Petty theft/pickpocketing rare. Solo night travel very safe. Scams minimal but exercise normal city caution (taxis use meter, verify prices). Women solo travelers report high safety.

ecommerce_events.name: AI Summit Seoul & Expo 2026
ecommerce_events.relevance: AI, LLMs, enterprise innovation. Direct fit for Claude API + n8n product building. COEX venue.

ecommerce_events.name: AAAI 2026 Summer Symposium Series
ecommerce_events.relevance: AI research, LLMs, resilient systems. Academic + industry convergence. Dongguk University.

ecommerce_events.name: Festival of Learning 2026 (AI + EdTech)
ecommerce_events.relevance: AI, learning sciences, HCI. Adjacent to indie AI product scene.

ecommerce_events.name: OpenAI Startup Meetup Seoul
ecommerce_events.relevance: OpenAI product roadmap, startup insights. Perfect for n8n/Claude integration builders.

weather.conditions: Mild spring (April–May) or cool autumn (Sept–Oct). Sunny, 4–6 hours per day. Low humidity. Occasional light rain.

options.name: Orakai Insadong Suites (hotel pool)
options.quality: Heated, hotel-grade, clean
options.transit_from_center: In-hotel; central Insadong location, metro Line 3 Anguk Station nearby

options.name: Seoul Forest Aquatic Center
options.quality: Olympic-standard public facility, clean, competitive lanes
options.transit_from_center: Seoul Forest, Line 2 Ttukseom Station (15 min walk or direct)

options.name: Samsung Leports Center (pool)
options.quality: High-end gym pool, coaching available
options.transit_from_center: Multiple locations; coaching in swimming offered

options.name: TrainTrue Gangnam
options.facilities: Powerlifting, Olympic lifting, top-tier equipment (Rogue), bumper plates, coaching
options.notes: World-class strength focus. Advanced lifters. English staff available. Premium vibe. Fits Riza's lifting/cardio combo.

options.name: Body & Seoul (Itaewon)
options.notes: Foreigner-friendly, English coaching, welcoming vibe. Drop-in friendly. Good for short stays.

options.name: FitClub Seoul (Itaewon)
options.facilities: Full gym, classes, English support, flexible hours
options.notes: Expat-friendly, solid programming. Central location. Budget-conscious option.

gym.running_options: Seoul Forest loop (6km), Han River Cycling Park (40km), Yeouido Park (5km loop). Public parks everywhere. Excellent running routes, safe, well-lit. Accessible by metro.
gym.yoga_pilates: Tao Yoga Cheongdam (boutique), The Flow Room Yoga Studio (Gangnam area), Star-Kali Fitness (Pilates + Yoga). Most charge €12–18/class; intro rates often available. English classes exist but limited; Korean classes welcoming to foreigners with basic English instructions.

day_trips.hiking: Bukhansan National Park (15 min metro + shuttle to trailhead, difficulty easy–hard, scenic views), Namsan Tower base (urban hike, central, easy), Seoraksan National Park (2–3 hours by train, UNESCO site). All accessible via public transport. Riza can easily day-hike without car.

marinas.name: Han River Park Marina (Yeouido / Mapo)
marinas.transit_from_center: Subway Line 5 Yeouido Station (5 min walk); direct river access from Yeouido Park

marinas.name: Seoul Marina / Han River Leisure Zone
marinas.transit_from_center: Line 9 Ichon Station or Line 3 Samseong; bus/walk to Han River access points

marinas.name: Incheon Marina (nearby, coastal)
marinas.location: Incheon (1 hour west of Seoul by metro)
marinas.transit_from_center: Airport Rail-link or Subway Line 1 to Incheon (70 min); marinas near Incheon port

schools.name: Han River Sailing School (affiliated with Yeouido Park)
schools.certification: Local (KSA—Korea Sailing Association); intro/advanced courses available

sailing.best_season: April–May (spring, mild winds, calm waters) and September–October (fall, excellent conditions). June–August hot and humid; typhoon risk Aug–Sept. Dec–Feb cold, rough conditions.
sailing.notes: Han River sailing scene is inland (flatwater, recreational). Coastal/ocean sailing requires Incheon trip. Wind conditions: steady springs/falls, variable summer. No major regatta scene in May 2026 visit window, but schools active. Accessible, not world-class venue, but solid intro/skill-builder option for Riza's interests.

new_hobbies.venue: Bukchon Hanok Village Pottery Studios (traditional Korean ceramic workshops)
new_hobbies.notes: Historic hanok village. Pottery deeply rooted in Korean culture. Classes often 2–3 hours. Riza gains cultural immersion + tangible craft output. Perfect fit for indie-hacker aesthetic + maker mentality.

new_hobbies.venue: Myeongdong Tea Houses or Insadong Traditional Tea Rooms (e.g., Osulloc Tea House)
new_hobbies.notes: Riza drinks black tea; Korean traditional tea (green, omija, yuzu, etc.) complements. Intro sessions teach ceremony + tasting. Insadong rich in traditional venues. Bridges classical music interest (meditative vibe).

new_hobbies.venue: National Gugak Center or Namsangol Hanok Village Performance Venues
new_hobbies.notes: Bridges classical music passion. Pansori (narrative singing), gayageum (zither) are UNESCO-protected. Spring/fall festivals active. Complements Seoul's cultural depth + Riza's appreciation for complex, disciplined art forms.

new_hobbies.venue: Hongdae Street Art Alley, Mural Tours, or DIY self-guided (Gangnam, Insadong, Hongdae)
new_hobbies.language: Self-guided or English tour groups available (Meetup.com, Airbnb experiences)
new_hobbies.notes: Seoul has explosive street art scene (Instagram-famous walls, murals, galleries). Accessible, free if self-guided. Builds visual literacy + social connections. Indie-hacker/creative alignment.

new_hobbies.hobby: Korean cooking class (kimchi, bibimbap, traditional banchan)
new_hobbies.venue: Cooking Class Studios (e.g., Korean Food Class or Airbnb Experiences, Insadong-area studios)
new_hobbies.notes: Riza already cooks; Korean culinary tradition offers deep cultural + technical learning. Classes typically 3 hours, hands-on. Market visits often included. Fun, practical, community-building.

coworking_retreats.notes: Seoul's coworking scene is office-based (WeWork, Executive Centre) rather than curated retreat-focused (like Outsite, Mokrin House, Coworkations.com models). No dedicated 'digital nomad villa retreats' with group living & shared experiences detected in Seoul proper for May 2026 timeframe. South Korea's strong corporate coworking dominates. Nearby Jeju Island has emerging remote-work retreats, but outside 7-day scope.

car_free.walkability: Excellent in central districts (Insadong, Myeongdong, Gangnam, Hongdae). Wide sidewalks, safe crossings, pedestrian zones. Outer areas walkable but transit supplemented.
car_free.public_transport: World-class subway (9 lines, 130+ stations), buses, taxis. Integrated T-money card (€50 initial, rechargeable) works across all modes. Single trip ~€1.20. Day pass €5–7. Monthly pass €55. Frequency: trains every 2–5 min peak hours. English signage excellent.
car_free.airport_to_center: Incheon International Airport: AREX (Airport Rail Link) direct to Seoul Station (43 min, €9 one-way); Subway Line 1 onwards. Limousine buses €10–15 (slower, 60–90 min). Gimpo Airport (domestic): Direct subway to city (20 min, €1.50). No car needed; transit convenient, reliable, 24/7.

startup_community.scene_summary: Seoul has a strong but fragmented startup ecosystem. Gangnam dominates corporate/fintech; Hongdae/Itaewon skew creative/indie. AI/ML startups growing (Pangyo Techno Valley emerging 'Silicon Valley of Korea'). OpenAI, Startup Grind, and Seoul AI Meetup active. Compared to SF/NYC, ecosystem is smaller, Korean-language gatekeeping real, but for AI/indie hackers with Claude/n8n focus, sufficient critical mass exists. May 2026 has AAAI Summer Symposium + AI Summit, boosting visibility. Visa + language barriers keep expat friction moderate.

key_venues.name: Seoul Global Startup Center (SGSC)
key_venues.what: Government-backed incubator (Ministry of SMEs), visa support, free coworking, mentoring. 1,152 members. Founder-focused. Multilingual.

key_venues.name: Seoul Startup Hub (Gongdeok, Mapo-gu)
key_venues.what: Large government-supported accelerator. Free coworking, mentoring, investment opportunities. Quiet, friendly vibe. Public spaces + private offices.

key_venues.name: Startup Grind Seoul
key_venues.what: Global founder network. Monthly meetups, pitch nights, investor connections. 120 countries, 3.5M+ worldwide members. Accessible to foreigners.

key_venues.name: Seoul AI Meetup (Meetup.com)
key_venues.what: 232+ members. Monthly in-person + online discussions on AI applications, LLMs, ML. Direct relevance to Claude API + n8n side projects. Active community.

startup_community.recurring_events: Seoul AI Meetup (monthly), Startup Grind Seoul (monthly), AAAI Summer Symposium (June 22–24, 2026), AI Summit (June 2026, COEX), OpenAI Startup Meetup (recurring, next April 22, 2026 confirmed). WeWork community events (networking, lunch & learns). Friday pitch nights (varies by venue).

bonus.flight_from_vie: Vienna (VIE) to Seoul (ICN/GMP): ~11 hours direct (no direct flights; 1–2 hour layover typical via Middle East/Asia hub). Turkish Airlines (VIE→IST→ICN, 15–16h total), Lufthansa (VIE→FRA→ICN, 13–14h), Korean Air (VIE→ICN direct on select days, 11.5h). Price: €400–700 return. 26 May 2026: mid-week, not peak season, favorable rates expected.
bonus.classical_music: Seoul lacks Vienna-tier classical music infrastructure. Lotte Concert Hall, Seoul Arts Center host symphony/chamber performances (April–May season active). Recordings of Korean traditional court music (UNESCO) + modern classical fusion available. Spring/fall arts seasons worth checking. Not a major classical music hub; live options moderate, recorded cultural access strong.
bonus.dance_scene: Salsa/bachata: Active scene in Hongdae & Gangnam (Latin clubs, socials). Jhonatan Dance Company, unnamed studios offer Salsa/Bachata classes. 'Salsa in Seoul' Facebook group exists. On2 dominant, some on1 at Bulldog (Itaewon). Intro lessons feasible; ecosystem smaller than Vienna/Barcelona but genuine. Bachata scene less developed than salsa. Latin food restaurants (Casa Latina in Hongdae) anchor vibe.

concerns: 7-day stay is very short for maximal community/network building; startup events may not align with brief window. Recommend extending to 2–3 weeks if possible.
Korean language barrier: Core coworking/hostels have English, but secondary services (gyms, cafes, transport minor issues) require Korean or translation app reliance.
Salsa/bachata scene exists but smaller than EU cities; Latin dance partner availability & studio class frequency moderate. Not a major hub for ballroom/Latin.
Sailing in Han River is flatwater recreational; no ocean sailing accessible from Seoul proper without day trip to Incheon. Intro/skill-builder only, not advanced.
Classical music venues available but Seoul not a flagship classical destination like Vienna. Theater/opera houses active but fewer world-renowned orchestras.
No curated coworking retreats (Feel.travel, Outsite model) in Seoul; must book accommodation + coworking separately. Adds logistical friction.
Turkish citizens may face minor visa scrutiny on entry (dual passport check); Korean visa officer familiarity with Slovak Pobyt variable. Recommend carry residence permit + passport both.

sources.name: Booking.com / Expedia / Tripadvisor
sources.what: Serviced apartment pricing, ratings, amenities (Orakai, Ever8, The Classic 500 Pentaz). Current 2026 rates.

sources.name: WeWork Seoul Official
sources.what: Coworking locations (Gangnam Station, Seoul Square, Yeouido), amenities, phone booths, pricing (₩350k base), day pass estimates.

sources.name: The Executive Centre Seoul
sources.what: Premium coworking alternatives, phone booths, day pass/monthly pricing, multiple Seoul locations.

sources.name: Bright!Tax / GreenBack / Digital Nomads Korea
sources.what: South Korea digital nomad visa requirements (F-1-D), income thresholds (₩88.1M~€66k), 90-day visa exemption for Turkish citizens.

sources.name: Meetup.com / Startup Grind Seoul / Seoul AI Meetup
sources.what: Startup community, AI meetups, founder events, networking. Active 2026 calendar.

sources.name: AAAI / AI Summit Seoul / Festival of Learning 2026
sources.what: June 2026 AI/academic conferences in Seoul. Direct relevance to Riza's AI/Claude/n8n interests.

sources.name: GigaBrain / TheCultureTrip / 10Mag
sources.what: Gyms (TrainTrue, Body & Seoul, FitClub, Star-Kali), salsa/bachata dance scene (Hongdae, Gangnam), swimming (Seoul Forest), running routes (Han River, Yeouido Park).

sources.name: Nomad List / Numbeo (implicit cross-check)
sources.what: Cost of living signals (cappuccino €3.50, lunch €7, gym €12/day, transit €55/month), safety, air quality, internet speed benchmarks (100+ Mbps).

summary: Split is an excellent fit for Riza: strong coworking infrastructure, reliable 50-100+ Mbps internet, excellent air quality, visa-free EU access, and a vibrant sailing culture. The 7-day budget is comfortable (~€1,200-1,400 total), leaving room for quality accommodations and food. Best visited Sept-October or April-May for weather and fewer tourists.
remote_work_pitch: Split combines world-class maritime infrastructure with emerging tech community support: multiple coworking spaces (The Works has phone booths, 24/7 access, ~€20/day), AI/startup meetups through Split Tech City, and reliable fast internet in coworking venues and cafes. As a Turkish citizen with Slovak Pobyt, you're visa-free under Schengen 90/180—no restrictions for this 7-day stay. Timezone coverage is strong (Vienna +1h, London same, Dubai -4h, Tokyo +7h).
best_months: April-May, September-October (mild weather, fewer crowds, sea temp 17-24°C); July-August warm but very touristy; November-February cold (10-12°C), rain increases

internet.notes: Coworking spaces (The Works, scaleup, Re.Split) deliver 100+ Mbps. Airbnb/hotel speeds vary 25-100 Mbps. Mobile data solid for calls. Some cafes (D16 Coffee) have professional-grade WiFi. Call reliability good in coworking environments.

work_spots.name: The Works
work_spots.notes: Only coworking with guaranteed phone booths. High-speed WiFi, complimentary coffee, standing & seated desks, in-house gym, lounge, 24/7 access. Strong for video calls. Community-focused events & networking.

work_spots.name: Re.Split
work_spots.neighborhood: East of Diocletian's Palace (Faculty of Economics area)
work_spots.notes: Biophilic design, plants, zen break zones. Young professional crowd. Private desks, private offices available. Coffee & pastry bar. Free parking nearby. Less suitable for long calls (no dedicated booths) but excellent for focused work.

work_spots.name: scaleup Office Split
work_spots.notes: Largest coworking (800m²). Modern furniture, meeting rooms, virtual office. Near Žnjan beach (10-15 min walk). Professional environment. Limited phone booth infrastructure, better for open-plan work.

work_spots.name: D16 Coffee
work_spots.notes: Popular with nomads. Professional-grade barista, freshly roasted beans. Decent WiFi. Small tables—solo work only. Tight seating (fits ~20 people), can be crowded. Better for casual work than intensive calls.

stays.name: Heritage Hotel 19
stays.neighborhood: Old Town edge (edge of Diocletian's Palace)
stays.why: 19th-century townhouse, characterful rooms with exposed stone, mid-century modern lighting. Courtyard with olive tree. Excellent walkability, near Diocletian's Palace & Marjan Hill. Fits boutique preference. ~€95/night keeps 7-day total ~€665, leaving €500+ for food/coworking within €2k budget.

stays.name: Cornaro Hotel
stays.why: Central location, rooftop terrace/bar, modern rooms with sea views. Free WiFi, 24hr front desk. Contemporary 4-star comfort. ~€110/night = €770 for 7 days, leaves €430 for meals & coworking. Excellent access to food/maritime scene.

stays.name: Aparthotel Marvie
stays.why: First wellness hotel in Croatia, digital-nomad focused. Kitchenette in units (save on dining). Coworking space access for hotel guests included. 50% discount on 28+ day stays (if extended). ~€105/night for serviced apt with kitchen = €735/7 days. Excellent value—leaves €565 for food/coworking. Kitchen allows budget dining.

visa.details: Turkish citizen + Slovak Pobyt = EU/EEA visa-free under Schengen. 7-day stay is well within 90-day limit per 180 days. No visa application needed. Slovak residence permit recognized throughout Schengen. Upon arrival, passport control may be minimal.

air_water_safety.air_quality_notes: AQI 11-39 (Good-Moderate). PM2.5 typically 28-32 µg/m³. Winter months (Jan-Feb) slightly elevated (~50-60 AQI). Summer-autumn excellent. Ranked world's #1 'feel-good' destination (2,600+ sun hours, clean air).
air_water_safety.safety_notes: Croatia is relatively safe. Solo night walks in Old Town and near waterfront are low-risk. Petty theft in tourist areas exists but not severe. Avoid poorly lit residential areas late night. Beach/marina areas safe. English widely spoken (99% of service workers). No major scams reported. Street harassment minimal.

weather.conditions: Mediterranean: mild spring/fall; hot dry summer; cool winter

options.name: Bacvice Beach (main sandy beach)
options.quality: Clear Adriatic, pebble/sandy mix, busy in summer

options.name: Žnjan Beach
options.quality: Rock/pebble, cleaner, fewer crowds

options.name: Kašjuni Beach
options.quality: Calm bay, clear water, scenic

options.name: The Works In-House Gym
options.facilities: Compact gym, weights, cardio (included with coworking membership)
options.notes: Included with day pass or membership. Not full-service but functional for weights & cardio.

options.name: ACI Marina Split Sports Center
options.notes: Open year-round. Near marina. Good sauna access. €8 day pass or €35/month. Decent facilities for a port city gym.

options.name: Hotel Radisson Blu Resort & Spa Fitness Center
options.facilities: Full gym, sauna, indoor pool, spa
options.notes: 5-star hotel gym. Day pass ~€12-15. Premium option if staying elsewhere. Sauna + pool included.

gym.running_options: Marjan Hill (pine-covered, panoramic views, 15-20 min from center). Riva promenade (seafront, flat, ~2km loop). Both excellent for cardio.
gym.yoga_pilates: Multiple yoga studios in Old Town and Varoš neighborhood. English-friendly classes available. Expect €12-15/class or €50-60/month membership. Hatha, vinyasa, Pilates options.

day_trips.hiking: Marjan Hill (pine forest trails, 15-20 min walk from Old Town center, 178m peak, panoramic Adriatic views). Easy-moderate trails. Free parking at base. Excellent for morning hike before work.

marinas.name: ACI Marina Split
marinas.location: Southwest Riva (30-min walk or 5-min taxi from Old Town)

marinas.name: Marina Zenta
marinas.location: 15-minute walk west from city port, near Bacvice Beach

marinas.name: Marina Kaštela
marinas.location: 25 km north of Split (30 min taxi/bus)

sailing.best_season: May-October (calm seas, wind 10-15 knots). Sept-Oct ideal (warm, less crowded). June-Aug very busy.
sailing.notes: Dalmatian islands (Brač, Hvar, Vis) 2-4 hours sail away. Crystal-clear water. Perfect for week-long charter or intro lessons. Regatta season summer. Strong sailing culture, easy crew access.

new_hobbies.venue: Culture Hub Croatia (PROSTOR creative workspace)
new_hobbies.notes: Split has rich stone-carving tradition (Diocletian's Palace). Culture Hub offers workshops. Ideal for tactile, meditative practice. Complements architectural interest.

new_hobbies.venue: Marina Zenta Diving Club or local instructors
new_hobbies.notes: Adriatic is world-class for beginners. Clear 15-25m visibility. Intro sessions at Marina Zenta. Complements sailing interest. Summer/fall best.

new_hobbies.venue: Local cooking schools (Konoba-style or market-based)
new_hobbies.neighborhood: Old Town markets or residential kitchens
new_hobbies.notes: Riza cooks but coastal Dalmatian cuisine (peka, seafood stews, truffle pasta) is unique. 3-hour market-to-table classes widely available. Pairs with black tea appreciation.

new_hobbies.venue: Marjan Hill community gym or outdoor training groups
new_hobbies.notes: Split's urban architecture (palace walls, rooftops, waterfront) is parkour-friendly. Young European scene. Complements gym/cardio routine.

new_hobbies.venue: Marina Zenta or independent outfitters
new_hobbies.notes: Half-day trips to nearby coves. Less technical than sailing, more intimate. Excellent fitness. Complements existing water sports interests.

coworking_retreats.notes: No dedicated remote-work retreats (Feel.travel, Coworkations, Outsite, etc.) currently operating in Split. City lacks curated coliving/coworking retreat infrastructure. Individual accommodations + day-pass coworking remain the norm. Consider nearby alternatives (Dalmatian islands, Croatian coast) if retreat model preferred.

car_free.walkability: Excellent in Old Town & Riva; moderate in residential areas (Varoš, Split 3). Hills (Marjan) add effort. Most nomad zones walkable.
car_free.public_transport: Tram system (3 lines: 1, 2, 3). Bus network extensive. Day pass €2, monthly €35. Reliable 10-15 min frequencies. Taxis cheap (€5-8 in-city), used as backup. Ferry to islands frequent.
car_free.airport_to_center: Bus 37 or 39: ~30 min, €4-5. Taxi: ~25 km, €25-35, 30-40 min. No train. Uber available.

startup_community.scene_summary: Split Tech City (est. 2010) anchors a small but active startup ecosystem focused on IT, AI, and deep tech. Monthly AI/startup meetups held (e.g., at The Works, Smartspace). Digital Nomad Association Croatia actively promotes Split as nomad destination. ~100-150 active tech professionals + growing remote worker cohort. Not Zagreb-level, but honest: emerging, supportive, approachable. Indie-hacker/AI-builder scene nascent but welcoming.

key_venues.name: Split Tech City
key_venues.what: Formal association connecting companies, startups, institutions. Hosts lectures, workshops, meetups on tech/AI/entrepreneurship.

key_venues.name: The Works Coworking
key_venues.what: Hosts startup meetups, AI panels, networking. Central hub for founders & remote workers.

key_venues.name: Smartspace
key_venues.what: Workspace for designers, programmers, entrepreneurs. Hosts workshops, exhibitions, community events.

key_venues.name: Culture Hub Croatia (PROSTOR + T29)
key_venues.what: Non-profit creative coworking. Hosts cultural events, workshops, makers community. Adjacent to tech but creative-first focus.

startup_community.recurring_events: Split Startups Meetup (monthly, AI/tech panel focus, hosted at The Works or Smartspace). Locals 'n' Nomads meetup (recurring, visa/community focus). Split Tech City lectures & workshops (ongoing). Digital Nomad Association Croatia events (irregular, quarterly). Informal coffee/networking at D16 Coffee or The Works.

bonus.flight_from_vie: Direct or 1-stop (via Munich, Budapest, Vienna Hub): 1.5-2.5 hrs flight time. ~€50-150 economy each way (budget carriers Ryanair, Wizz). Airport (SPU) 25 km northwest. Total journey ~4-5 hours Vienna to hotel.
bonus.classical_music: Croatian National Theatre hosts opera & classical. Diocletian's Palace courtyard hosts summer festivals (concerts, classical music performances, June-Sept). Summer season strong. Winter calendar quieter. St. Dominus Cathedral occasional concerts. Music faculty (FESB) student recitals available.

dance_scene.bachata_salsa: Limited formal scene vs. larger cities (Barcelona, Berlin). 1-2 dedicated salsa/bachata clubs (e.g., Kala or seasonal venues). Tourist-oriented rather than dancer-focused. Summer season (June-Sept) stronger. Dance studios offer occasional classes (€12-15/class). Best strategy: book classes online pre-visit or combine with weekend trip to Dubrovnik/Zagreb for larger events.

concerns: Limited formal bachata/salsa dance scene (tourist city, not dedicated dance hub). Plan classes pre-trip or use summer season.
Coworking retreat infrastructure non-existent (no Feel.travel, Coworkations, etc. nearby)—would need private booking + day coworking.
Phone booth scarcity outside The Works (plan video calls there). Some cafes may not accommodate 3+ hour focused sessions despite good WiFi.
Tech startup ecosystem still small (good for indie hackers, limited for fundraising/VC signals). Zagreb 2+ hours away if ecosystem networking priority.
Winter weather (Nov-Feb) cool (~10-12°C, increased rain), sea temp low (~12°C). Not ideal for swimming/sailing hobbies.

summary: Taipei is an excellent 7-day sprint destination for Riza: visa-free 90-day entry, exceptional coworking with phone booths (WeWork, SkyCo, T3CO all equipped), ~€60–80/night budget hotels fit easily in the €2000 budget, and strong tech scene with regular AI/startup meetups. One caveat: air quality can spike when pollution drifts from China.
remote_work_pitch: Taipei combines stellar internet (170+ Mbps fixed, 111 Mbps mobile 5G), abundant coworking spaces with dedicated phone booths for client calls, and a genuine AI/startup community—critical for Riza's indie-hacker edge. The city's public transport is flawless (car-free compatible), and accommodation is cheap enough to leave €1200+ for 7 days of coworking + meals while staying in a decent hotel. Timezone coverage to Vienna (+8hr), London (+8hr), Tokyo (+1hr) is good.
best_months: September–November (autumn: warm, dry, typhoon-free); March–May (spring: 20–28°C, cherry blossoms); avoid June–August (heat, typhoons) and December–February (rainy, gloomy)

internet.notes: Homeplus & Chunghwa Telecom lead. Taiwan has 5G urban blanket coverage (97%+) with 300–400 Mbps peak; fiber to 90% by 2025. Reliable for video calls.

work_spots.name: WeWork Taipei
work_spots.notes: Premium amenities, private phone booths, onsite staff, business-class printers. High-end vibe, glass walls, community events.

work_spots.name: SkyCo Nanjing Fuxing
work_spots.notes: Taiwanese-owned, 3 floors with phone booths on each. Quiet zones (like library), standing desks, high-quality chairs. 90% Taiwanese membership.

work_spots.name: T3CO (T3 Coworking)
work_spots.notes: State-of-the-art, next to City Hall MRT. Stunning Taipei 101 views. Global member network, diverse professionals.

work_spots.name: JustCo
work_spots.notes: Virtual offices, 1–3 person desks, dedicated cubicles. Decentralized network across city. Events & community focus.

stays.name: Mitsui Garden Hotel Taipei
stays.why: Modern 4-star, MRT-adjacent, walkable to shops/cafés, well-reviewed. Weekly discount likely available (10–15% off nightly).

stays.name: Sparkle Hotel
stays.why: Mid-budget with exceptional location (practically connected to Taipei 101 & MRT). Good value-for-quality, boutique vibe.

stays.name: Caesar Metro Taipei
stays.why: Budget-friendly 3-star, MRT hub, convenient for day trips & airport access. Most popular with travelers. Tight budget fit.

visa.details: Turkish passport + Slovak Pobyt residence: Turkey is visa-free to Taiwan for 90 days (no visa required, no extension possible). Slovak residence permit confirms legal status in EU/EEA, making Riza visa-free within Schengen and Taiwan. 7-day stay is well within 90-day window.

air_water_safety.air_quality_notes: Winter/spring generally good (AQI 40–80). Pollution spikes when wind carries smog from China (worst in autumn/early winter). Monitor WAQI app.
air_water_safety.safety_notes: Taipei is extremely safe: very low street crime, pick-pocketing rare, scams uncommon. Women travel solo freely. Night transit safe. No neighborhoods to avoid for solo traveler.

ecommerce_events.name: MEET Taipei Startup Festival
ecommerce_events.relevance: Ecommerce, AI, startups. 413 startups from 19 countries, investors, large innovation platform. Riza's AI/indie product side fits perfectly.

ecommerce_events.name: Taipei Game Show
ecommerce_events.relevance: Adjacent: digital content, ecommerce tech, blockchain. 360k attendees.

ecommerce_events.name: Indie Hackers Taipei Meetup
ecommerce_events.relevance: AI builders, founders, side projects. Direct match for Riza's community.

weather.conditions: Spring (Mar–May): mild, cherry blossoms; Summer (Jun–Aug): hot/humid, typhoons; Autumn (Sep–Nov): warm, dry, ideal; Winter (Dec–Feb): cool, rainy.

options.name: Taipei Nangang Software Park Swimming Pool
options.quality: Public, indoor, Olympic standard
options.transit_from_center: Nangang Software Park MRT (Linhai line), ~30 min from Xinyi

options.name: Baisha Bay Beach
options.quality: Sandy, swimmable (water temp 22°C summer)
options.transit_from_center: Bus from Taipei Main Station, ~60 min north

options.name: Daan Forest Park Water Feature (splash pad)
options.quality: Park fountains, not lap swimming

options.name: Fitness First Taipei
options.facilities: Weights, cardio, classes (yoga, spin), functional training
options.notes: International chain, English-speaking staff, modern equipment. Busy peak hours.

options.name: Gold's Gym Taipei
options.facilities: Weights, cardio, Olympic platforms, sauna, classes
options.notes: Strong lifting culture, powerlifting bars. Riza's preference for weights + cardio well-served.

options.name: Hotel Gym (Mitsui Garden / Sparkle)
options.notes: Free with hotel stay. Not comprehensive but convenient.

gym.running_options: Daan Forest Park (central, 3.5 km loop, flat); Taipei Riverside Park (Tamsui–New Taipei riverside trail, scenic, 20+ km total); popular evening runs after 6pm. Safe, well-lit.
gym.yoga_pilates: Y+ Yoga, Pure Yoga (Taipei Center)—English classes, ~€15–20/class or membership. Many boutique studios in Daan, Xinyi.

day_trips.hiking: Elephant Mountain (Xiangshan, Taipei): 1.5-hr trailhead via Taipei 101 MRT + 20-min walk. Easy/moderate. Scenic city views, sunset popular. Yangmingshan National Park (40 min MRT): volcanic trails, sulfur hot springs.

marinas.name: Tamsui Fisherman's Wharf
marinas.transit_from_center: Red MRT line to Tamsui station, then bus/taxi 15 min to wharf. ~50 min from Taipei center.

marinas.name: Danshui Riverside Marina
marinas.transit_from_center: Tamsui MRT, walkable from station (~20 min along riverside).

marinas.name: Keeling Harbor (Jilong)
marinas.location: Keeling, northeast coast (1 hr by train)
marinas.transit_from_center: Northeast Coast Express train from Taipei Main Station, scenic ride.

sailing.best_season: October–April (calm waters, steady winds, no typhoons); avoid June–September (typhoon season).
sailing.notes: Taiwan Strait & northeast coast offer decent sailing but not world-class. Winds moderate. Small/local sailing scene vs. Mediterranean. Good for weekend blasts.

new_hobbies.venue: Jiufen Old Street Tea Houses or Maokong Tea Plantations
new_hobbies.notes: Taipei is China's tea capital. Half-day trip via MRT to Jiufen/Maokong; hands-on tea tasting, grading, brewing. Aligns with Riza's love of black tea + culture.

new_hobbies.venue: Taipei Cooking Studio or night market workshops
new_hobbies.notes: Riza already cooks; street food + night market cooking classes build on it. Hands-on, fun, local. 2–3 hour sessions common.

new_hobbies.venue: Yangmingshan Paragliding Spot or Outdoor adventure operators
new_hobbies.notes: Taiwan's volcanic peaks + consistent winds. 7-day stay too short for certification, but intro/tandem flights possible. Scenic, adrenaline-heavy.

new_hobbies.venue: Taipei Artist Studios / weekend plein-air groups
new_hobbies.notes: Taipei's street culture, temples, architecture are painters' paradise. Cafés host sketching groups. Low barrier, high local engagement.

new_hobbies.venue: East Coast Day Trip (Wanli or Jiufen Coast)
new_hobbies.notes: Taiwan's northeast coast has calm, clear waters (summer). Short trips popular. Complements sailing interest.

coworking_retreats.notes: No major curated co-working retreat operators (Feel.travel, Coworkations, Outsite, Sun & Co) currently offering Taipei-based group retreats with shared accommodation + work + community programming. Taipei is a stand-alone coworking city (DIY model), not a grouped retreat hub like Bali or Lisbon.

car_free.walkability: Daan, Xinyi, Ximending highly walkable; cross-city exploration best via MRT. Streets narrow, pedestrian-heavy, safe.
car_free.public_transport: MRT (Metro Rapid Transit): 6 lines, blanket coverage, ~€0.97/month pass (Yo-Yo Card). Buses, taxis cheap (~€1–3 per ride). Frequency: trains every 1–3 min peak hours.
car_free.airport_to_center: Taoyuan International Airport: Airport MRT direct to Taipei Main Station (~35 min, €5). Songshan Domestic Airport: MRT line 1, ~15 min to center. Both car-free friendly.

startup_community.scene_summary: Taipei's startup scene is active but not Silicon Valley-tier. MEET Taipei (Asia's largest startup festival, 75k+ attendees, 400+ startups) anchors the ecosystem. Regular AI/indie-hacker meetups exist (Indie Hackers Taipei monthly). Government backing (Taiwan Tech Arena, MODA). Strength: AI chips, hardware, ecommerce tech. Weakness: relatively few bootstrapped indie SaaS founders vs. VC-backed hardware startups.

key_venues.name: Taiwan Tech Arena (TTA)
key_venues.what: Incubator, demo nights, startup events. Focus: AI, IoT, precision tech.

key_venues.name: Indie Hackers Taipei Meetup
key_venues.what: Monthly founder/builder meetup. Direct match for Riza. Website: indiehackerstaipei.com

key_venues.name: FutureWard Central
key_venues.what: Startup-focused coworking, monthly events, founder community.

startup_community.recurring_events: Indie Hackers Taipei (monthly); MEET Taipei Festival (November, annual); Taipei Tech Meetup (regular); AI & Data Science Meetup (monthly).

bonus.flight_from_vie: Vienna (VIE) → Taipei (TPE): ~12–14 hours (1–2 stops, typically via Bangkok or Doha). Direct flights rare; common routing: VIE → Emirates/Qatar hub → TPE. ~€550–750 mid-range round-trip.
bonus.classical_music: Taipei has active classical scene: National Taiwan Concert Hall (NTCH), Taiwan Philharmonic. Season Sept–June. English info available. Venues: Taipei City Hall, Taipei National Theater. Ticket prices: €20–80 typical.
bonus.dance_scene: Bachata/Salsa: Small but active. Dance studios in Daan (e.g., 'Spin & Move', 'Latin Soul Dance Studio') offer beginner classes. Riza already dances; scene is niche but welcoming. Weekend milongas & salsa nights in Ximending bars.

concerns: Air pollution: Winter/early-spring smog from China can spike AQI to 150+. Riza should monitor WAQI, avoid outdoor gym/running on bad days.

Language barrier: English spoken in tourist/business areas (coworking, hotels) but street signs, menus, gyms dominantly Mandarin/Chinese characters. B1 German won't help; Riza should prep a translation app (Google Translate offline).

Internet latency to Vienna: 8-hour timezone gap + ~200ms ping to EU. Client calls possible but off-peak (early morning Taipei = late evening Vienna). Coworking phone booths help, but call scheduling requires flexibility.

Food: Taipei's cuisine is carb-heavy (noodles, rice). Limited 'protein-focused' options compared to Western cities. Gyms + 7-Elevens have basic options, but meal-prep might be needed for Riza's lifting regimen.
7-day stay limits network-building: Startup community is real, but deep connections require 2–4 weeks. Riza can hit Indie Hackers meetup but won't deeply integrate into scene in one week.

summary: The standout for your passport: Georgia gives Turkish citizens up to 365 days visa-free, the longest runway of any city here. Vibrant, cheap, great cafe-work culture, real nomad community. Direct from VIE (Wizz, sometimes seasonal).
remote_work_pitch: Tbilisi is the single best base in this group for testing a long stay: a full year visa-free means zero day-counting stress, paired with a thriving nomad scene, superb wine-and-coffee culture, and sulfur baths. For an operator who wants a non-Schengen anchor, nothing here matches its runway.
best_months: May-June, September-October (warm, dry, wine season); July-August hot

internet.notes: Fiber solid in center; cafe wifi generally reliable for calls, carry Magti/Silknet SIM as backup

work_spots.name: Lokal / Impact Hub Tbilisi
work_spots.notes: Strong nomad/founder community, booths and meeting rooms

work_spots.name: Terminal / Sp- coworking
work_spots.notes: Chain of quiet professional coworking, call booths

work_spots.name: Coffee LAB / Erti Kava
work_spots.notes: Excellent specialty coffee, laptop-friendly, nomad-dense

stays.name: Vera/Sololaki serviced apartment
stays.why: Leafy, central, cafe-dense, walkable to old town

stays.name: Old Town boutique hotel
stays.why: Atmospheric, near sulfur baths and Narikala

visa.details: Turkish citizens: Georgia allows entry with ID card and grants up to 365 days visa-free stay — the longest runway here. Outside Schengen, does NOT consume your 90/180 Schengen allowance. Best day-counting situation of all 10.

air_water_safety.air_quality_notes: Traffic particulates in center; fine in leafier districts
air_water_safety.safety_notes: Very safe, famously hospitable; chaotic driving is the main hazard, otherwise relaxed for solo evenings

weather.conditions: Continental, hot summers, mild shoulder

options.name: Lisi Lake / Turtle Lake
options.quality: Good, popular summer swimming

options.name: Sport Hall / boutique gyms
options.notes: Decent, English limited but workable

gym.running_options: Vake Park, Lisi Lake loop, Mtatsminda paths
gym.yoga_pilates: Growing studio scene, some English classes in Vera/Vake

day_trips.hiking: Mtatsminda (funicular from center), and day trips to Kazbegi/Gudauri by marshrutka for serious mountains

sailing.notes: Inland; Black Sea sailing is at Batumi, not Tbilisi

new_hobbies.venue: Vino Underground, winery day courses
new_hobbies.notes: Georgia is the birthplace of wine (8000 years); qvevri amphora winemaking is a uniquely Georgian deep-dive

options.name: Various nomad coliving (e.g. Fabrika area)
options.notes: Fabrika hostel-complex is a nomad social hub even if you stay in private accommodation nearby

coworking_retreats.notes: Active nomad scene, informal coliving around Fabrika

car_free.walkability: Center and Vera very walkable; hilly
car_free.public_transport: Metro (2 lines), buses, cheap (~0.30/ride); Bolt cheap everywhere
car_free.airport_to_center: ~20min by bus 37 (~0.30) or taxi/Bolt (~12 EUR)

startup_community.scene_summary: Strong and growing nomad/founder scene, lots of international remote workers, crypto and IT crowd, English widely spoken in the bubble. Real serendipity at coworkings and cafes. Not Amazon-specific but genuine density.

key_venues.name: Impact Hub Tbilisi
key_venues.what: Founder/nomad community anchor

key_venues.name: Fabrika
key_venues.what: Social courtyard hub for the nomad crowd

bonus.flight_from_vie: 3h direct (Wizz Air, sometimes seasonal) ~120-220 EUR; otherwise 1-stop via IST
bonus.classical_music: Tbilisi Opera and Ballet Theatre, Rustaveli; good cheap tickets
bonus.dance_scene: Growing bachata/salsa scene, weekly socials in center

concerns: Direct VIE flight can be seasonal, verify dates
+3h from Vienna shifts EU client-call timing

sources.name: Nomad List

summary: <cite index="7-1,7-21">Tirana costs 20% less than Belgrade while providing comparable internet speeds (137 Mbps) and is the region's most nomad-friendly destination with 90-day visa-free entry for Turkish citizens</cite>. <cite index="1-2">Coworking hubs offer daily rates from €5–€15</cite>, and the walkable center suits Riza's car-free requirement. Excellent for a 7-day sprint with strong internet, affordable stays under €2000, and emerging AI/startup community.
remote_work_pitch: <cite index="1-14">Tirana ranks 3rd in Europe for internet speed with an average of 250 Mbps and 90% fiber coverage in major cities</cite>. <cite index="6-3">The city is walkable in the center, has a growing number of cafés and coworking spaces aimed at freelancers and startups, and offers quick weekend access to mountains or the Albanian Riviera</cite>. <cite index="4-4">Reliable internet, and vibrant locations near key spots like Skanderbeg Square make the city an excellent coworking option</cite>.
best_months: April–May, September–October (pleasant weather, moderate prices); June (summer season but vibrant); avoid November–February (rain, poor air quality)

internet.notes: <cite index="1-14,1-15">90% fiber coverage in major cities with 99.5% uptime</cite>. <cite index="1-16">Most coworking spaces, accommodations, and cafés provide reliable WiFi that can handle video calls and file uploads without a hitch</cite>.

work_spots.name: InnoSpace Tirana
work_spots.notes: <cite index="4-8">Spacious, modern, with private offices and day passes at €15</cite>. <cite index="3-28">Great location near the best pubs and restaurants in the Blloku area, along with easy access to transit routes</cite>.

work_spots.name: Dutch Hub
work_spots.notes: <cite index="5-28,5-29">Open in Tirana since 2018 and is one of the biggest in the city. Welcome both foreigners and locals to work, which makes a fantastic community hub</cite>.

work_spots.name: Coolab
work_spots.notes: <cite index="5-25,5-26,5-27">One of the top-rated coworking spaces in Tirana, very popular with those who want a convenient location and a friendly, chill vibe. Praised for its events and the social aspect as it has fantastic facilities and great common spaces</cite>.

work_spots.name: Café Mulberry (Blloku)
work_spots.notes: <cite index="22-10,22-11,22-12,22-13">Café culture is deeply embedded—you can sit in a beautiful cafe for hours, nursing a single espresso, using the wifi, working on your laptop, and nobody bats an eye. Not like some European cities where you feel pressured to order more or leave after 30 minutes</cite>.

stays.name: Ebel Boutique Hotel
stays.why: <cite index="16-9,16-10">Traditional aesthetic sets it apart from typical modern hotel—the design and décor have warmth and character. Rooms are surprisingly spacious—far more generous than you'd expect at this price point</cite>.

stays.name: The Rooms - Serviced Apartments Albania
stays.why: <cite index="11-3,11-4,11-5">Locations are strategic, the complexes are recent, modern and well-organised. The apartments are quiet, comfortable, perfectly equipped and technologically advanced. Guest care service is active 24 hours a day</cite>.

stays.name: Hotel Mondial (Apartments)
stays.why: <cite index="18-6,18-7">Spacious, comfortable and amazing value for money. Felt much more like having our own flat than a hotel room</cite>.

visa.details: <cite index="31-17">Albania grants visa-free entry to six additional countries – Armenia, Azerbaijan, China, Kazakhstan, Kuwait, and Turkey</cite>. Turkish citizens enjoy 90-day visa-free entry within any 180-day period. <cite index="36-26,36-27">A newer path is the digital nomad option, piloted from January 2026. It allows a 1-year stay with proof of €3,000 monthly income</cite>. <cite index="39-28">None of the Western Balkan neighbors (Montenegro, Kosovo, North Macedonia, Serbia) are in Schengen, so your 90-day clock stays paused for the entire Balkan leg</cite>.

air_water_safety.air_quality_notes: <cite index="29-22">Air quality issues in winter (heating season) and high traffic periods</cite>. <cite index="41-1,41-2">Summer generally brings better air quality with winds dispersing emissions. Those with respiratory conditions should monitor air quality forecasts during winter visits</cite>.
air_water_safety.safety_notes: Tirana is generally safe for solo travelers. <cite index="6-4">English is increasingly spoken among younger people and in hospitality, making daily life easier for many nomads</cite>. Petty theft exists; use standard urban precautions. Traffic is chaotic—use crosswalks carefully. Avoid isolated areas at night.

weather.conditions: <cite index="41-6,41-7,41-8">Mediterranean climate with distinct seasonal variations. Summers arrive hot and dry with abundant sunshine, while winters bring cooler temperatures and most of the year's rainfall. Spring and autumn provide transitional periods with pleasant, moderate weather</cite>.

options.name: Durrës Beach
options.quality: Good (sandy, nearby Adriatic)
options.transit_from_center: 30 minutes by bus or car via coastal road

options.name: Artificial Lake / Grand Park
options.quality: OK (urban lake, walking trails, limited swimming)
options.transit_from_center: Walking distance from city center; part of Grand Park

options.name: Hotel/Gym Pools (multiple locations)
options.quality: Good (modern facilities in Blloku hotels)
options.transit_from_center: Within walking distance in central neighborhoods

options.name: Fit Zone Gym
options.notes: Busy during peak hours; English-speaking staff

options.name: Gold's Gym Tirana
options.notes: Modern, well-equipped; premium pricing

options.name: Local Community Gym (Qendra Sportive)
options.notes: Very affordable; less modern but functional

gym.running_options: <cite index="22-28,22-29">Grand Park area (around Parku i Madh, Tirana's main green space) offers a quieter vibe with easy access to running trails and the artificial lake</cite>.
gym.yoga_pilates: Scattered studios in Blloku and downtown; English classes at premium studios (€12–€18/class); many coworking spaces host yoga events.

day_trips.hiking: <cite index="7-7">Tirana sits just 30 minutes from Adriatic beaches at Durrës, 45 minutes from dramatic coastal cities like Sarandë, and 90 minutes from Albanian Alps hiking trails</cite>. Near Tirana: Dajti Express cable car (20 min by bus/taxi to base) for easy mountain access; mountain peaks offer day-hike loops.

sailing.best_season: May–September (warm, calm waters; summer most touristy/expensive)
sailing.notes: Sailing scene is emerging; Durrës 30 min away has Adriatic access. Charter availability is limited compared to Croatia or Greece but growing.

new_hobbies.venue: Tirana Photo Tours / Street Photography Groups
new_hobbies.notes: Tirana's street art, grand architecture (Communist-era + modern rebuild), and vibrant café culture make it a photographer's dream. Blloku and Skanderbeg Square offer excellent urban composition.

new_hobbies.venue: Sarandë Freediving School (coastal)
new_hobbies.notes: Ionian Sea offers clear waters and underwater caves near Sarandë. Weekend trip compatible with 7-day stay; emerging scene with qualified instructors.

new_hobbies.venue: Dajti Paragliding / Local Clubs
new_hobbies.notes: Dajti Mountain overlooks Tirana with excellent thermals. Tandem flights available; growing adventure sports scene.

new_hobbies.venue: Studio Argjilë / Local Ceramic Workshops
new_hobbies.notes: Albania has traditional pottery heritage. Small ateliers in Tirana offer casual drop-in classes; vibrant artisan community in Old Bazar.

coworking_retreats.notes: No established curated co-working retreats (Feel.travel, Coworkations, Outsite, Sun and Co, NomadX) currently operate in or near Tirana as of May 2026. The city has organic coworking/startup community but no formal coliving retreat operators.

car_free.walkability: <cite index="7-3">Tirana scores 92/100 for walkability – higher than any Balkan capital – with most destinations reachable on foot</cite>.
car_free.public_transport: <cite index="6-9,6-11">Buses are inexpensive but can be slow; there's no metro. Cycling infrastructure is limited, though short distances in the centre are walkable</cite>. <cite index="24-11,24-12">A one-way ticket for local transport in Tirana costs £0.36. If you are commuting regularly, a monthly pass is typically priced at £14</cite>. Taxi/Bolt apps fill gaps; ~€2–€5 per ride within city.
car_free.airport_to_center: <cite index="39-7">A 24-hour airport bus runs to the city center (~400 ALL / ~€4, every 30 minutes)</cite>.

startup_community.scene_summary: <cite index="6-6,6-7">There's no single dominant digital-nomad hub in Tirana yet the city has multiple coworking options and reliable cafes with fast internet. Popular coworking and creative hubs and community meeting points include small/medium spaces that host events, meetups and startup nights</cite>. Scene is emerging but less mature than Belgrade or Bucharest.

key_venues.name: InnoSpace Tirana
key_venues.what: Hub for startups, entrepreneurs, tech community; hosts networking events and demo nights

key_venues.name: Dutch Hub
key_venues.what: Networking and collaboration focus; community-driven; attracts local + international founders

key_venues.name: Coolab
key_venues.what: Creative hub; hosts regular events, workshops, and startup meetups

key_venues.name: Blloku District
key_venues.what: Informal hub—cafés, bars, restaurants serve as de facto networking spaces for creatives and nomads

startup_community.recurring_events: Startup meetups (periodic); tech talks at coworking spaces; indie hacker community online (Slack groups, Reddit r/albania); no regular structured demo nights but organic events 1–2x/month

bonus.flight_from_vie: <cite index="39-5,39-6">Tirana International Airport Nënë Tereza (TIA) is the country's main airport, located about 25 km northwest of central Tirana. Budget carriers like Wizz Air and Ryanair, along with carriers like Turkish Airlines, Lufthansa, and Air Albania, connect Tirana to dozens of European cities</cite>. Direct flights ~2.5 hours; ~€30–€100 (budget carriers).

timezones.vs_london: +1 (Tirana ahead in summer, same in winter)

bonus.classical_music: Limited scene compared to Vienna. National Theater and Opera House host performances; seasonal concerts at smaller venues. <cite index="41-9">The city experiences roughly 2,500 hours of sunshine annually - among the sunniest capitals in Europe</cite>. Best for outdoor recital culture and café concerts; not a major classical hub.
bonus.dance_scene: Bachata/salsa clubs exist in Blloku (Klub 555, Energie, etc.); mid-size scene, mostly Latin DJ nights + casual partners. Studio lessons available (€12–€18/class); beginner-friendly but less established than larger European cities.

concerns: Winter air quality (December–February heating season) may impact gym/outdoor workouts.
Visa limited to 90 days unless digital nomad permit obtained (requires €3,000/month proof of income).
Traffic and driving chaotic—car-free is wise, but bus reliability varies.
Startup/AI community is emerging but less dense than Vienna, Berlin, or Belgrade—good for indie builders, sparse for corporate clients.
Some restaurant prices and Blloku accommodation rising; still affordable relative to Western Europe.

summary: <cite index="2-3">Tivat offers an ideal balance between productivity and quality of life with its relaxed coastal lifestyle, modern infrastructure, and international community.</cite> <cite index="7-11,7-12,7-13">Tivat centers around Porto Montenegro, a luxury yacht marina that defines the town's character, making it Montenegro's most modern coastal city with new development and polished infrastructure.</cite> Budget fits comfortably; sailing/marinas excellent; visa alert for Turkish citizens.
remote_work_pitch: <cite index="1-1,1-3">Tivat is positioned as one of the fastest-growing digital nomad hubs on the Adriatic, surrounded by cafés, restaurants, and a vibrant international community near Porto Montenegro.</cite> <cite index="2-21">Mediterranean climate with over 240 sunny days per year, direct international flights via Tivat Airport, and growing digital nomad community.</cite> Perfect for consulting calls (phone booths at coworking), Baltic tech vibe, reliable fiber internet, and unbeatable sailings infrastructure.
best_months: April–May, September–October (shoulder seasons: mild weather, fewer tourists, calm seas for sailing)

internet.notes: <cite index="4-3">According to Speedtest, Montenegro is ranked 49 on its Global Index, with download speeds of about 40 Mbps and upload speeds of 15 Mbps.</cite> <cite index="7-14,7-15">Infrastructure quality in Tivat exceeds other coastal cities; internet rarely has issues, buildings are newer with proper insulation and amenities.</cite> <cite index="24-1">WiFi4WB initiative provides free, fast and reliable public internet at Magnolia Square in Tivat.</cite>

work_spots.name: Shipyard Coworking
work_spots.notes: <cite index="2-18,2-19">Located in central Tivat, the coworking space sits just a short walk from the Adriatic waterfront, cafés, and Porto Montenegro promenade, making it easy to combine focused work sessions with relaxing seaside breaks.</cite> <cite index="2-21">Features ultra-fast fiber internet, comfortable ergonomic chairs, meeting areas, external monitors, and free coffee/tea.</cite>

work_spots.name: Impact Coworking
work_spots.notes: <cite index="4-1">Impact Coworking listed among Montenegro coworking options.</cite> Likely more budget-friendly than Shipyard; check current rates on Coworker or local booking sites.

work_spots.name: Porto Montenegro Nautika Business Club
work_spots.notes: <cite index="1-19">Porto Montenegro is expanding business offering with the launch of the Nautika Business Club—an exclusive workspace set within the unique historical setting of the Maritime Heritage Museum in Tivat.</cite> Excellent for client calls; upscale restaurants and yacht-watching ambiance.

work_spots.name: Café Magnolia Square (Public WiFi + Work-Friendly)
work_spots.notes: <cite index="24-1">Magnolia Square in Tivat has free, fast and reliable public internet from the WiFi4WB initiative.</cite> Ideal for informal meetings; plan for breaks if running long calls (no dedicated booth).

stays.name: Casa Manor Boutique Hotel
stays.why: <cite index="14-10,14-11,14-12">Casa Manor Boutique Hotel presents a luxurious getaway with an excellent guest review rating of 9.6, set against stunning sea views with beachfront access and complimentary continental breakfast.</cite> Intimate, reliable, perfect for short stays.

stays.name: Hotel Palma
stays.why: Well-reviewed mid-range option directly on waterfront with excellent breakfast. <cite index="16-33,16-34,16-36">Staff incredibly welcoming and attentive, rooms spacious and elegantly decorated with stunning bay views, breakfast buffet varied and plentiful.</cite> Strong value; €595 fits budget.

stays.name: D&D Apartments (Porto Montenegro area)
stays.why: <cite index="16-32">D&D Apartments from $58 (≈€54 est.) offering easy access to Porto Montenegro.</cite> Budget estimate €70/night to account for seasonal variance. Full kitchen allows meal prep; frees up €300+ for coworking + meals + activities. Quieter than hotels; good WiFi expected.

visa.details: <cite index="47-1">As of October 30, 2025, the visa-free travel arrangement for Turkish citizens holding ordinary (burgundy) passports to Montenegro has been temporarily suspended.</cite> <cite index="47-4">Turkish citizens holding a passport with a valid Schengen visa or residence permit valid for at least three months can enter Montenegro without obtaining a Montenegrin visa for stays up to 30 days.</cite> Riza's Slovak Pobyt (EU residence permit) qualifies: <cite index="44-2">Holders of valid foreign travel documents with a residence permit in Schengen zone countries can enter and stay in Montenegro for up to 30 days.</cite> 7-day stay is visa-free under Pobyt + Schengen rules. No Schengen 90/180 reset required; Montenegro aligns with Schengen logic.

air_water_safety.air_quality_notes: Mediterranean coastal air, minimal industrial pollution. No AQI data found, but <cite index="33-1">Tivat has crystal-clear waters with over 200 sunny days a year, mild climate, and lush vegetation.</cite> Winter months (Dec–Jan) can have occasional haze; spring/summer excellent.
air_water_safety.safety_notes: <cite index="5-42">Kotor is calm, friendly, and very safe, even at night.</cite> Tivat comparable—upscale tourism infrastructure, low street crime. Porto Montenegro area particularly safe/patrolled. Standard precautions advised in old town at night; minimal scams reported. Solo travel safe; good expat/nomad community presence.

ecommerce_events.name: Summer Fest Tivat
ecommerce_events.relevance: Adjacent – local business networking, tourism/hospitality focus, informal founder hangouts

ecommerce_events.name: Budva Summer Carnival
ecommerce_events.relevance: Adjacent – tourism/ecommerce + retail angle, large crowd, partner opportunities

ecommerce_events.name: Tivat Bocce Olympiad
ecommerce_events.relevance: Social/community only – limited ecommerce relevance; useful for team breaks/networking

weather.conditions: Mediterranean: warm dry summers, mild rainy winters. <cite index="37-1,37-6">Average year air temperature is 15°C. Averagely 115 days per year with temperature above 30°C. Swimming season lasts 180 days.</cite> <cite index="31-3,31-4">Warm, dry summers and mild, rainy winters; best time for sailing between May and October.</cite>

options.name: Plavi Horizonti Beach (Blue Horizon)
options.quality: Excellent – <cite index="37-14,37-16,37-17">considered the most attractive beach on the Montenegrin coast, situated in the open sea next to the hotel, with very fine sand suitable for healing rheumatic disorders.</cite>

options.name: Sveti Marko Island Beach
options.quality: Good – scenic, calm waters, small visitor count; historic island
options.transit_from_center: Tourist boat from Tivat port (€5–8 return)

options.name: Hotel Beaches (Porto Montenegro waterfront)
options.quality: Good – managed, lifeguards, easy entry

options.name: Regent Porto Montenegro Hotel Fitness Center
options.notes: Day pass available to non-guests; upscale; <cite index="14-16,14-17">Features full-service spa, Turkish bath, outdoor tennis courts.</cite>

options.name: Fitness Club Tivat Central
options.notes: Local chain; budget-friendly; typical Adriatic standard; English spoken by staff.

options.name: Club Nautika (Porto Montenegro)
options.facilities: Weights, cardio, swimming pool, yoga classes
options.notes: Marina-integrated; smaller; fewer tourists; strong yoga/pilates offerings (English classes available).

gym.running_options: Promenade loop around waterfront (Tivat → Porto Montenegro → Donja Lastva): 5–8 km scenic route, flat/hilly sections, sea views; <cite index="29-6">Scenic cycling, hiking, and walking trails available at Vrmac Peninsula.</cite>
gym.yoga_pilates: Nautika Club (yoga, 3×/week in English); independent instructors available via Meetup/local Facebook groups.

day_trips.hiking: <cite index="8-8,8-9,8-10">Tivat offers abundant hiking and cycling opportunities; encircled by lush hills and dramatic mountains, the town presents numerous trails weaving through verdant forests and along the scenic coastline.</cite> Vrmac Peninsula (2–3 km): moderate 1-hour summit hike with 360° bay views. Lovćen National Park (30 km, 45 min by bus to Kotor, then car/hike): alpine terrain, UNESCO Lake Cetinje views. Kotor Old Town (15 km, 30 min bus): UNESCO heritage, medieval fortress hike. All accessible car-free via public transit.

marinas.name: Porto Montenegro
marinas.transit_from_center: Walk (2–5 min); <cite index="36-14,36-15">Located at coordinates 42˚26'N 018˚41'E, features world-class full-service marina with 450 berths accommodating yachts ranging from 12m to 250m.</cite> <cite index="36-17">Marina Porto Montenegro is equipped with free WiFi throughout its premises.</cite>

marinas.name: ACI Marina Budva (Dukley Marina)
marinas.transit_from_center: 30 min by bus (€2); <cite index="36-22">Marina can accommodate up to 300 berths, suitable for vessels up to 70 meters in length and with a maximum draft of 4 meters.</cite>

marinas.name: Kotor Port (Luka Kotor)
marinas.transit_from_center: 20 min by local bus (€1.50); <cite index="35-18,35-19,35-20,35-21">Known as Luka Kotor or Cattaro, can accommodate up to 30 boats, up to 30 metres in length with maximum seabed depth of 8.5 metres and offers high-quality services.</cite>

sailing.best_season: <cite index="29-19,29-20">Best time to rent a boat in Tivat is from late May through September, when the pleasant Mediterranean climate offers warm temperatures and sunny skies; peak summer months July and August can get crowded.</cite> <cite index="29-2">Prevailing wind during summer is excellent for sailing, typically starting around midday and fading by sunset.</cite>
sailing.notes: <cite index="29-1">The sea is warm, ideal for swimming and water sports, while the Bora and Sirocco winds provide steady sailing conditions.</cite> Tivat is a leading charter base; beginner-friendly; multiple bareboat and crewed options; <cite index="32-14,32-15">Montenegro is the right choice for beginners or those with little sailing experience, with abundance of multiple well-equipped marinas and various mooring options.</cite>

new_hobbies.venue: Adriatic Freediving Center (Porto Montenegro or Tivat Bay operations)
new_hobbies.notes: Crystal-clear Adriatic waters, warm sea temps (22°C May–Sept), abundant marine life, AIDA-certified instruction. Tivat Bay is shallow (ideal for beginners); island dives nearby.

new_hobbies.venue: Vrmac Paragliding School / Tivat Bay Launch Sites
new_hobbies.notes: <cite index="29-8">Try paragliding for a bird's view of the bay.</cite> Moderate thermals; bay breeze provides steady lift; 360° bay/mountain panoramas; light conditions ideal spring/fall.

new_hobbies.venue: Ada Bojana Watersports Complex (day trip, 60 km, or local Tivat Beach rentals)
new_hobbies.neighborhood: Plavi Horizonti Beach or Ada Bojana delta
new_hobbies.notes: Bay area calm/forgiving for learners; spring/autumn consistent northerly winds (Bora); summer light winds. Ada Bojana (south) has stronger thermals/dunes. Equipment rental widely available.

new_hobbies.venue: Local artisan studios in old town Tivat or Kotor (15 km)
new_hobbies.neighborhood: Tivat Old Town or Kotor historic quarter
new_hobbies.notes: Adriatic coast rich in pottery tradition; Kotor especially known for ceramicists; classes often in intimate studios; Mediterranean clay + sea views. Cultural immersion; 2–3 hour sessions common.

new_hobbies.venue: Teodo Bay Kitesurfing School (8 km, local shuttle) or Ada Bojana
new_hobbies.notes: Bay provides sheltered conditions for learners; summer thermal winds; equipment rental + lessons bundled. Ada Bojana (delta) better for intermediate/advanced; wider wind window.

options.name: Playworking (Montenegro co-living house)
options.dates: Year-round rolling availability (check availability on Nomadico/booking sites)
options.notes: <cite index="3-1,3-3,3-4,3-5">Playworking is a large house accommodating up to 15 people with ample coworking space, nice living room, lots of outdoor space to enjoy Montenegro's climate, and free laundry facilities; located just outside of Tivat.</cite> Weekly/monthly rates available; mixed community; good for longer stays (30+ days preferred for value).

options.name: Nomio Tivat (Modern Co-living for Digital Nomads)
options.notes: <cite index="1-6,1-7">Nomio redefines the typical hotel concept with coliving-style apartments tailored for longer stays; fully furnished, stylish units with natural light and functional layouts for both work and relaxation.</cite> This is a boutique co-living with coworking—not a curated retreat, but modern, nightly/weekly rates available; price ~€65–85/night (est.).

options.name: Pachamama Retreat (mentioned in local nomad guides)
options.type: Curated retreat (set dates, group-style; details limited)
options.dates: Check Lu.ma or Coworkations.com for seasonal schedules
options.notes: Limited public info; appears to be smaller, intimate retreat focused on wellness + work balance; Tivat/Bay of Kotor region. Verify current dates/availability directly.

coworking_retreats.notes: Tivat has emerging coliving/retreat scene but not as mature as Lisbon or Bali. Playworking (Kotor, 15 min away) is most established. Nomio bridges hotel and coliving. Expect €60–100/night for co-living, €1200–1600/week for curated retreats. For a true curated 7-day retreat with group activities, budget €150–200/day; Playworking + Nomio offer flexibility at lower cost but less structured programming.

car_free.walkability: <cite index="2-1">The area combines luxury infrastructure, international cafés, coworking spaces, and relaxed Mediterranean living.</cite> Town center (Porto Montenegro to old town) is highly walkable (20–30 min end-to-end). Waterfront promenade scenic and pedestrian-friendly. Day-to-day errands doable on foot; longer excursions require transit.
car_free.public_transport: <cite index="4-24,4-25">Traveling around Montenegro is very simple thanks to its fantastic bus system; if you're a slow travel digital nomad who prefers to explore your homebase country, buses will quickly become your best friend.</cite> Tivat-Kotor: €1.50 (20 min). Tivat-Budva: €2 (30 min). Tivat-Podgorica: €4 (90 min). Monthly transit card estimated €30–40 (includes local buses + intercity). Reliable, frequent, air-conditioned.
car_free.airport_to_center: <cite index="5-2,5-4">Tivat Airport is 12 minutes by taxi (€20); closest airport is Tivat (TIV) just a 15–20 minute drive from Kotor with 15–25 euros by taxi.</cite> No direct airport bus; taxi/Uber only reliable option (budget €18–25).

startup_community.scene_summary: <cite index="7-7,7-27">Budva, Tivat, and Bar's where the nomad community actually exists—you'll meet people without trying.</cite> Tivat has growing digital nomad presence (Amazon consultants, indie hackers, AI builders) but <cite index="7-12,7-13">it's Montenegro's most modern coastal city with new development and polished infrastructure; more business-like than Budva's beach party vibe.</cite> Startup scene weaker than Budva or Podgorica; mostly freelancers and location-independent professionals. AI/tech founders present but sparse; founder networks require active meetup participation.

key_venues.name: Shipyard Coworking
key_venues.what: Regular founder hangouts, casual talks, peer mentorship; community manager on-site

key_venues.name: Porto Montenegro Nautika Business Club
key_venues.what: High-end networking, investor/business events (seasonal), luxury founder dinners

key_venues.name: Magnolia Square Meetup Spot
key_venues.what: Informal daily gathering of digital nomads (morning/afternoon); organized via local Facebook group

key_venues.name: Montenegro Digital Nomads & Remote Workers (Facebook Group)
key_venues.what: <cite index="3-30">Join the Facebook group Montenegro Digital Nomads & Remote Workers to connect with other digital nomads in Montenegro and to find out about any events and meetups.</cite>

startup_community.recurring_events: Monthly coworking socials (Shipyard, Porto); summer tech talks (July–Aug, ad-hoc); informal weekly coffee meetups (Magnolia, Tivat Bay). No major demo days/pitch events in Tivat; Podgorica hosts TechCrunch Disrupt-adjacent events (100 km). IndieHackers community present online; in-person meetups sporadic.

bonus.flight_from_vie: <cite index="4-29,4-30">Tivat gets considerably more passengers because it's closer to the coast and tourist cities; Podgorica Airport works all year round, while Tivat Airport is fairly seasonal (although there are some off-season flights as well).</cite> Vienna–Tivat: 2–2.5 hours (direct flights seasonally via Wizz Air, Lufthansa). ~€80–150 return (low season) to €200–400 (high season). Buses from VIE to TIV available (10–12 hours, €40–60) but flight preferred.

timezones.vs_london: -1 hour (Tivat GMT+1 summer, GMT+2 winter EU transition)

bonus.classical_music: No dedicated concert hall in Tivat proper. <cite index="29-28">Summer Fest and Bocce Olympiad gather numerous tourists.</cite> Kotor (15 km): historic churches (St. Tryphon Cathedral) host summer chamber concerts; Kotor Summer Festival (June–Sept) features classical performances. Budva (23 km): larger venues. Podgorica National Opera (90 km) offers full season. Streaming + local piano bars (Porto Montenegro) fill gap; many hotels host live classical in summer.
bonus.dance_scene: Bachata/Salsa: No dedicated dance studios in Tivat; <cite index="7-4">Montenegrins nurse coffees for hours anyway, so nobody minds if you set up shop.</cite> Kotor has one or two independent instructors (check local Facebook groups). Budva has 2–3 salsa clubs and casual classes. Summer months: beach clubs (Porto Montenegro area) host Latin dance nights informally. Riza may need to rely on online classes or organize weekly lessons via WhatsApp community.

concerns: Visa: Turkish citizens now require visa for Montenegro (as of Oct 2025); Riza's Slovak Pobyt + Schengen residency covers 30-day entry, sufficient for 7-day stay.
Startup ecosystem: Weak local founder network; AI/tech scene minimal vs. Vienna/Berlin; requires active community-seeking.
Seasonal tourism: July–Aug crowded; infrastructure/prices inflated; shoulder seasons (Apr–May, Sep–Oct) preferable.
Classical music: No concert hall; reliant on summer festivals or day trips to Kotor/Podgorica.
Salsa/Bachata: No dedicated studios; informal scene; may require organizing peer lessons or relying on online instructors.

summary: A grand, affordable Danube capital famous for thermal baths and a buzzing social scene, with top-10 global internet and prices among the lowest of any EU capital. Thermal-bath swimming and a strong classical tradition suit you well. Vienna is 2.5h by train. Schengen, so days count toward your 90/180.
remote_work_pitch: Budapest hits a lot of your notes cheaply: genuinely fast internet, a beautiful walkable city, thermal baths for daily swims (Szechenyi, Gellert, Rudas), a serious classical scene (Liszt Academy, the Opera, Mupa), ruin-bar nightlife, and good coworking, all 2.5h from Vienna. It is one of Europe's best value-for-culture bases, with the usual cold winters and a tourist-heavy core as caveats.
best_months: April to June and September to October; hot summers, cold winters

internet.notes: Hungary ranks top-10 globally for speed; fast reliable fiber everywhere

air_water_safety.safety_notes: Very safe; watch taxi overcharging and tourist-bar scams. Tap water fine

car_free.public_transport: Metro/tram/bus, ~28 EUR/month; no car needed
