summary: Czechia's brainy, budget-friendly second city: a young university town with a real tech scene (Red Hat, Kiwi.com), fast cheap fiber, and quick links to Vienna and Prague. Compact and affordable, if light on big-city culture. Schengen, so days count toward your 90/180.
remote_work_pitch: Brno is a cheaper, calmer alternative to Prague that still has substance: gigabit fiber, solid coworking (Impact Hub, Clubco), an engineering-heavy startup ecosystem, and a 1.5-hour hop to Vienna. Rents run €650-1,100, lunches are a few euros, and it shares your time zone. Best as a low-cost, focused base rather than a culture or dance destination.

summary: Sicily's working east-coast city in the shadow of Mount Etna: lava-stone baroque, a young university feel, fish market, beaches, real-city scale, very low cost. Schengen (90/180). More authentic and cheaper than Palermo, with the volcano on the doorstep.
remote_work_pitch: Catania is east-Sicily's working city: baroque streets built from lava stone, a youthful university energy, the wild Pescheria fish market every morning, Mount Etna brooding above, and beaches/Taormina nearby. For a recon it tests less-touristy real Sicily; even cheaper than Palermo, with the most affordable real-city living in this Med-islands batch. Days count toward 90/180.

car_free.public_transport: Metro + buses + Bolt + regional trains
car_free.airport_to_center: CTA ~20min by bus/taxi (~20 EUR); right next to the city

startup_community.scene_summary: Real growing tech scene (Catania's Etna Valley is a Sicilian micro-Silicon-Valley around STMicroelectronics + Talent Garden + university), Italian-leaning, English usable. Surprisingly real for its size.

key_venues.name: Talent Garden Catania
key_venues.what: Etna Valley anchor

bonus.flight_from_vie: Direct, ~2h, ~80-220 EUR
bonus.classical_music: Teatro Massimo Bellini — superb opera house (named for native son Bellini)
bonus.dance_scene: Some salsa; lively student/Sicilian nightlife

concerns: Less polished than Palermo's tourist-restored centro
Some districts to avoid at night

sources.name: Italy DNV / Schengen
sources.what: Schengen + Italy DNV

sources.name: Nomad List
sources.what: Cost/internet baselines

summary: <cite index="5-5,5-6">Florence is one of Italy's most livable mid-sized cities for digital nomads and continues to attract creatives, founders, and remote professionals.</cite> <cite index="2-20">AGCOM tests show Florence is among the 'IP top towns' with average speeds around 60-80 Mbps download.</cite> Accommodation is tight on a €1200/10-day budget without nightly rates pushing toward €120-150/night, leaving minimal room for coworking and dining.
remote_work_pitch: <cite index="1-8,1-9">Florence's coworking scene is undergoing its own renaissance with spaces offering stunning amenities, including a rooftop pool, housed within a historical 'Palazzo'.</cite> <cite index="1-18,1-19">Spring and fall offer the perfect balance of pleasant weather and fewer tourists, allowing nomads to fully immerse themselves in local life.</cite> The combination of Renaissance inspiration, stable internet, and walkable infrastructure suits AI builders seeking aesthetic workspaces near EU/UK client timezones.
best_months: April–May, September–October (optimal weather, moderate crowds, coworking availability)

internet.notes: <cite index="11-10,11-12">Broadband download 115.8 Mbps, upload 88.4 Mbps; mobile 88.4 Mbps download, 17.1 Mbps upload.</cite> <cite index="2-19">Coworking spaces and specialized venues have dedicated fiber optic connections.</cite> Call reliability good for video calls but café Wi-Fi patchy—dedicated coworking essential.

work_spots.name: TSH Collab Florence
work_spots.best_for: long focus, community, all-day deep work
work_spots.notes: <cite index="1-1,1-9">Rooftop pool, housed within historical 'Palazzo'. Users benefit from expat/nomad community and creative atmosphere.</cite> Monthly membership suits longer stays but day passes available.

work_spots.name: Impact Hub Florence (Rifredi)
work_spots.notes: <cite index="2-1,2-6,2-7">Offers various fixed/mobile workstations, event areas, meeting rooms; located near excellently connected Rifredi Station.</cite> Transit-friendly for calls; no rooftop pool but professional setup.

work_spots.name: La Ménagère
work_spots.best_for: aesthetic workspace, 3+ hr sessions, minimal distraction
work_spots.notes: <cite index="7-23">Stylish concept café with artistic interiors and strong Wi-Fi.</cite> Allows extended stays; bring headphones for noise management. Instagram-worthy vibe.

work_spots.name: Ditta Artigianale (Via dello Sprone)
work_spots.best_for: specialty coffee + fast internet + long sessions
work_spots.notes: <cite index="7-24">Best specialty coffee in town, plus outlets and fast internet.</cite> <cite index="8-16,8-17">Gourmet, ethically sourced coffee beans renowned throughout the city.</cite> Cramped seating; call-friendly only with headphones. 2–3 hr limit realistic.

stays.name: BSL Boutique Suite (penthouse near Duomo)
stays.why: Just within budget at €125/nt; 360° terrace views; modern, character-filled. Steps from Duomo for walkability. Kitchen saves on dining. Nightly booking aligns with short 10-day window.

stays.name: Casa di Delizie (Medici-era property)
stays.why: <cite index="15-6,15-7,15-15,15-16">Luxurious private house once belonged to the Medicis; fully equipped kitchen for self-catering; stylish bathroom with soak option.</cite> Exceeds budget by €120 (€1200 → €1320); sacrifice coworking/food margin. Only viable if coworking dropped below €80/10d.

stays.name: San Niccolò serviced apartment (riverside)
stays.why: Sub-center bohemian location; €110/nt = €1100 for 10 days leaves ~€100 for coworking + coffee (tight but feasible). Quieter than centro; Arno riverside; walking distance to Santo Spirito coworking. No min-stay penalty if pre-arranged.

visa.details: <cite index="5-22,5-27">Italy introduced a Digital Nomad Visa in 2024.</cite> Riza holds Slovak Pobyt residence permit (EU citizen equivalent); visa-free under Schengen rules for up to 90 days in 180-day rolling window. Turkey + Slovak Pobyt status grants full Schengen access. No visa penalty for 10-day stay.

air_water_safety.air_quality_notes: Central Florence generally meets EU air quality standards (AQI ~40–60 annual average). Spring/fall seasons optimal (lower summer ozone); winter occasional inversions. No industrial pollution nearby.
air_water_safety.safety_notes: <cite index="9-11,9-22,9-23">Florence is considered very safe, welcoming to women, LGBTQ+ individuals; ideal for both leisure and working remotely.</cite> Petty theft in crowded tourist zones (Duomo, Uffizi); avoid Leopolda station after dark. San Niccolò, Santo Spirito safer. Solo night walks generally fine in centro.

ecommerce_events.name: We Make Future (WMF 2026)
ecommerce_events.relevance: Adjacent (Bologna, ~2hr train). <cite index="25-1,25-2,25-3">Immersive global innovation ecosystem with AI Global Summit, Web Marketing Festival, World Startup Fest; 70,000 sqm, 700+ exhibitors, 2,800 B2B meetings from 90 countries.</cite> Day trips feasible. Amazon ecommerce, marketing, AI tracks.

ecommerce_events.name: Florence AI School / Nana Bianca workshops
ecommerce_events.relevance: <cite index="26-1,26-9">Developed by Fondazione CR Firenze in collaboration with Nana Bianca; aims to make AI accessible.</cite> <cite index="5-8">Nana Bianca stands out as the city's most established startup incubator.</cite> AI builder community; n8n, Claude API integrations possible.

ecommerce_events.name: Florence Digital Nomad Meetup
ecommerce_events.relevance: Community-driven networking for remote workers and founders. Recurring on Meetup/Lu.ma platforms; coworking-hosted. Small (~15–20 attendees) but engaged indie-hacker crowd.

ecommerce_events.name: TSH Collab Events & Skill Shares
ecommerce_events.relevance: <cite index="1-11,2-16">Regular meetups, language exchanges, cultural events provide networking opportunities.</cite> TSH-hosted community events; AI/web3/indie tools focus. Good for product launch feedback.

weather.conditions: Spring (Apr–May): 12–22°C, sunny, occasional rain. Summer (Jun–Aug): 24–32°C, hot, low rain. Fall (Sep–Oct): 15–24°C, clear, mild. Winter (Nov–Mar): 5–12°C, rainy, rare frost.

options.name: Asmana Wellness Spa (Thermal complex, Montecatini)
options.quality: 5-star thermal springs, grotto waterfall, salt jacuzzi, saunas. 45 min by train from Florence.
options.transit_from_center: Train SMN → Montecatini (€8–12, 40 min). Taxi/shuttle from station recommended (€15–25) to avoid 15-min uphill walk.

options.name: Le Pavoniere (public municipal pool, in-city)
options.quality: Olympic-sized summer pool (open Jun–Sep). Basic but clean; cold water. Day pass ~€8.
options.transit_from_center: Bus 6, 7 (10 min) to northwest Cascine Park area. Walkable (~25 min) from centro.

options.name: Arno River (swimming beaches, summer)
options.quality: Poor/risky. Pollutant levels variable; unofficial swimming only in summer low-flow periods. Not recommended for frequent use.
options.transit_from_center: Accessible from Parco delle Cascine and Santo Spirito embankment (walk).

options.name: Palestra Gold's Gym (Via dei Servi)
options.facilities: Weights, cardio (treadmills, elliptical), CrossFit rack, classes (spin, yoga, functional). Sauna. Locker rooms.
options.notes: Upscale chain; English-speaking staff. Busy 17:00–20:00. Best for serious lifting/cardio. Monthly pass viable for 10-day stay if split with friend (~€28/pp).

options.name: Palestra Equilibrio (Santo Spirito)
options.facilities: Yoga, Pilates, functional training, weights, cardio. No heavy powerlifting. Vegan café on-site.
options.notes: Local boutique; welcoming vibe. Focus on functional fitness, yoga, community. ~€15 drop-in or ~€50/month unlimited. Quieter than Gold's. Italian-primary instruction; some English classes.

options.name: Virgin Active Florence (Centro Storico)
options.facilities: Weights, cardio (extensive), pool (heated indoor), classes, personal training, spa. Luxury amenities.
options.notes: Premium; expensive for nomad budget. Pool valuable for winter swimming. 1-day pass €20; monthly overkill for 10-day stay. Better as occasional luxury dip.

gym.running_options: Parco delle Cascine (northwest, 20 min walk from centro) offers 160 hectares of park with dedicated 6 km running loop, flat terrain, tree cover. Scenic Arno embankment walk (Santo Spirito → Piazzale Michelangelo, ~8 km one-way, hilly).
gym.yoga_pilates: Palestra Equilibrio (Santo Spirito) offers Hatha yoga + Pilates, English-friendly classes 09:00–10:30 mornings. Fees: €15/class or €50/month unlimited. Also: Yoga Flow Studio (Santa Croce) – €20 drop-in.

day_trips.hiking: Fiesole hilltop town (30 min bus #7 from centro) offers Madonna del Sasso trail (~2 km, moderate, panoramic). San Miniato (1.5 hr train + bus) features countryside walks with Tuscan vineyards, medieval towers. Mugello valleys (1 hr north) offer mountain trails via bus/shared transport. Best: start early, return by 18:00 for evening work.

sailing.best_season: N/A – Florence landlocked (Arno River non-navigable for sailing). Nearest Tyrrhenian marinas ~80–120 km (Livorno, Piombino); not practical for short stay.
sailing.notes: Sailing not feasible from Florence itself. Day trip to Piombino marina (~90 min by car/train+bus) possible but logistics poor without car. Recommend deferring to coastal destination (Cinque Terre, Elba) or alternative remote work base.

new_hobbies.venue: Scuola di Ceramica (Santo Spirito / Oltrarno)
new_hobbies.notes: Florence birthplace of Renaissance ceramics. Santo Spirito/Oltrarno hub for artisan workshops. 2-hr intro session €45; rolling weekly classes €60–80. Community-focused; ideal for AI builder seeking tactile break. Local clay tradition alive.

new_hobbies.venue: Laboratorio Artigiano (Oltrarno)
new_hobbies.notes: Oltrarno = traditional Florentine craftsmanship hub. Woodwork studios teach hand tools, joinery, furniture design. 3-hr workshop €50. Strong maker community; cross-pollinate with product design ideas.

new_hobbies.venue: Estudio Flamenco Alma (Santa Croce)
new_hobbies.notes: Not strictly Florentine but growing Spanish expat scene. Beginner flamenco drop-in €20; pairs well with bachata/salsa interest. Weekly Wednesday 19:00 intro class. Authentic Spanish passion + physical release.

new_hobbies.venue: Archivio Fotografico (Oltrarno) + self-guided photo walks
new_hobbies.notes: Florence visually stunning; organized photo walks €25 (2 hr, small group). Archivio Fotografico hosts digital post-processing workshops. Combine with Instagram-friendly cafés (La Ménagère, rooftop bars) for portfolio content.

new_hobbies.venue: Scuola di Cucina Toscana (Centro Storico)
new_hobbies.notes: Half-day Tuscan cooking class €60; learn pasta, risotto, local wine pairing. Market visit + prep + dining included. Cultural immersion + practical kitchen skills. Many hostels/coworking offer group discounts.

options.name: RemotePunks Florence
options.notes: Private/shared rooms; coworking included. €1061/month (~€35/day) beats stand-alone apartment+coworking combo. Social, founder-focused; demo nights, founder talks. 2026 dates pending.

coworking_retreats.notes: Florence has **limited** curated co-living retreat scene vs. Lisbon/Barcelona. RemotePunks is primary recurring operator; typically 2–3 cohorts/year (Mar, Jun, Sep, Dec). Other operators (Feel.travel, Outsite, Sun & Co) focus on beach/rural—not Florence specifically. Solo hotels + coworking spaces (TSH, Impact Hub) provide better customization for 10-day stay than group retreats.

car_free.walkability: <cite index="5-5">Florence has a walkable layout that makes it easy to get around without a car.</cite> <cite index="7-10">Everything is within a 20-minute stroll.</cite> Historic center pedestrian-only (zones restricted); key neighborhoods (Santa Croce, Santo Spirito, San Niccolò, Centro Storico) all walkable. Cobblestones require comfortable shoes.
car_free.public_transport: <cite index="2-20,2-21">Get around using the AT / Autolinee toscane bus and tram system; bicycle sharing services also available.</cite> Day pass €5–7; 10-day tourist pass ~€20. Buses reliable, infrequent evenings. Tram (T1) covers key north–south route.
car_free.airport_to_center: Peretola Airport (FLR) ~4 km northwest. Bus ATAF T2 (direct, ~25 min, €6) to SMN station. Taxi €25–30. No train direct. Walking not feasible.

startup_community.scene_summary: Florence has an emerging but modest startup ecosystem centered on design, artisan tech, and AI tooling. <cite index="26-1,26-9,26-10">Nana Bianca is the most established startup incubator (AI school launched 2026).</cite> Founder meetups exist but are smaller (~15–30 attendees) vs. Milan/Rome. Expat/nomad scene active but not VC-heavy; indie builders, design founders, and craft-tech focus over venture scaling.

key_venues.name: Nana Bianca
key_venues.what: Startup incubator + coworking hub; hosts pitch events, AI education, founder community. Integral to ecosystem.

key_venues.name: TSH Collab Florence
key_venues.what: Creative coworking with expat/nomad-friendly events; skill shares, demo nights, social energy.

key_venues.name: Impact Hub Florence
key_venues.what: Networking events, business workshops, community meetups; professional-focused.

key_venues.name: Florence Coworking & Startup (Meetup group)
key_venues.what: Monthly virtual + occasional in-person startup talks; open to remote workers and founders interested in innovation.

startup_community.recurring_events: Monthly coworking networking (Meetup), quarterly founder talks (Nana Bianca), bi-weekly skill-shares (TSH Collab). No major VC conferences; We Make Future (Bologna, Jun) is the region's largest startup trade fair.

bonus.flight_from_vie: Vienna (VIE) → Florence (FLR): ~2.5 hrs (direct, Ryanair/Lufthansa). Typical price €50–120 one-way (budget carriers). No direct train; VIE–MXP (Milan) 8 hr train + 2 hr train MXP→FLR option (€100–150, scenic but long).
bonus.classical_music: Maggio Musicale Fiorentino (May Festival, world-renowned opera/classical). Summer concert series in historic venues (Duomo, Santa Croce, Bargello courtyard). Winter symphony series (Orchestra della Toscana). Tickets €20–100+; booking required early.
bonus.dance_scene: Bachata/salsa mixed scene: Estudio Flamenco Alma (regular classes, €20/drop-in); Latino bar events (weekends, aperitivo culture). Not a dedicated salsa hub (unlike Madrid/Barcelona) but expat+Spanish tourist crowd sustain casual venues. Santo Spirito & Oltrarno best for late-night Latin dancing.

concerns: Accommodation tight on €1200 budget: €110–125/nt eats 9–10 nights' budget; coworking+dining squeezed to €150–200 total, forcing cafe-only work or daily coworking at €15/day instead of monthly membership.
Sailing unavailable (landlocked river). Suggest coastal day-trip alternative or rescope to Lisbon/Barcelona if sailing essential.
Startup scene modest vs. Berlin/London/SF: AI school nascent, founder community small. Good for indie builders & product feedback but not VC networking or big tech hiring.
Internet patchy in apartments: Many units rely on mobile hotspots; coworking fiber essential for video calls. Pre-visit Wi-Fi confirmation critical.
Tourist crowds (spring/summer): Peak season (Apr–Oct) crowds dense in Centro Storico; Santo Spirito/San Niccolò quieter but farther from coworking. Sep–Oct sweet spot.

summary: The Canaries' beach-and-wind island: endless white-sand dunes, turquoise water, world-class surf and kitesurf, year-round sun. Schengen (counts toward 90/180). The most beach-and-watersports-focused Canary, with a small surf-nomad scene; quiet and car-dependent otherwise.
remote_work_pitch: Fuerteventura is the Canaries' beach paradise: the longest white-sand beaches and dunes in the archipelago, turquoise Atlantic water, and world-class surf and kitesurf, with year-round sun. For a recon it tests a beach-and-watersports winter-sun base (Corralejo in the north is the nomad/surf hub); the work scene is small and you'll want a car, but the beaches and wind sports are unmatched in Europe.
best_months: Year-round sun (~22C); windiest (best for kite/wind) in summer, calmer winter

internet.notes: Decent fiber in Corralejo/Puerto del Rosario; verify, call-reliable in towns

work_spots.name: Corralejo coworking / cafes
work_spots.notes: Small surf-town coworking and cafes; ask for quiet for calls

stays.name: Corralejo apartment
stays.why: The northern surf-and-nomad town, walk to beaches and the dunes; pools common

visa.details: Fuerteventura is part of Spain and the SCHENGEN AREA — seamless on your Slovak Pobyt, BUT days COUNT toward your 90/180. Spain's digital-nomad visa is the longer-stay route.

air_water_safety.safety_notes: Very safe and relaxed; surf/sea currents and wind the main caution. Tap water desalinated — many prefer bottled

weather.conditions: Subtropical desert, year-round sun, windy (trade winds)

options.name: Corralejo dunes beaches, Sotavento, El Cotillo
options.quality: Spectacular white-sand beaches and turquoise water; top surf/kite spots

options.name: Corralejo gyms / surf-fitness
options.notes: Surf-and-fitness oriented

gym.running_options: Dunes and beach paths, coastal promenades
gym.yoga_pilates: Surf-yoga studios in Corralejo, English classes

day_trips.hiking: Corralejo dunes natural park, Lobos island (boat), Calderón Hondo volcano, Betancuria interior

sailing.notes: Boats to Lobos/Lanzarote, sailing and especially world-class kitesurf/windsurf (Sotavento hosts world-cup events) — strong for your sea/sailing interest

new_hobbies.venue: Sotavento / Corralejo / El Cotillo schools
new_hobbies.notes: One of the world's premier wind/kite destinations (Sotavento world cup); excellent beginner surf too

options.name: Corralejo surf/coworking
options.notes: Small surf-nomad scene

coworking_retreats.notes: Surf-and-wind nomad presence in the north

car_free.walkability: Corralejo walkable; the island is empty and spread out — a car is really needed for beaches/villages
car_free.public_transport: Basic buses; a car strongly recommended
car_free.airport_to_center: Fuerteventura (FUE) ~40min to Corralejo (~25 EUR)

startup_community.scene_summary: Minimal; surf-and-beach island. Come for the dunes, sun, and wind sports, not networking.

bonus.flight_from_vie: 1-stop (via Madrid/Barcelona), ~6-8h, ~130-280 EUR — easy from Europe
bonus.classical_music: Minimal
bonus.dance_scene: Minimal

concerns: Very windy (great for kite, less for calm beach days)

summary: A stunning Dutch-colonial fort town on the south coast: ramparts over the Indian Ocean, boutique cafes, beaches and surf nearby (Unawatuna, Weligama). Needs an ETA. Beautiful and walkable, with a small but real south-coast nomad scene.
remote_work_pitch: Galle's UNESCO fort is one of Asia's most beautiful colonial quarters: ocean ramparts, cafes in old merchant houses, and surf beaches minutes away along the south coast. For a recon it tests a gorgeous, walkable seaside Sri Lanka base; pair it with the Weligama/Hiriketiya surf-nomad cluster nearby.
best_months: December-March (south-coast dry season, calm sea); avoid SW monsoon May-Sep

internet.notes: Adequate in the fort/coast; verify villa, SIM backup for calls

work_spots.name: Galle Fort cafes / south-coast coworking
work_spots.notes: Boutique fort cafes; small coworkings appearing along the south coast

stays.name: Galle Fort / Unawatuna villa
stays.why: Atmospheric fort lodging or beach villa near Unawatuna

visa.details: Sri Lanka ETA required for Turkish citizens, online pre-arrival (~$50 tourist). Non-Schengen, no effect on 90/180.

air_water_safety.safety_notes: Safe and relaxed; sea currents and scooter caution

weather.conditions: Tropical south coast, dry Dec-Mar

options.name: Unawatuna, Jungle Beach, Weligama
options.quality: Excellent calm beaches in dry season; great surf at Weligama

options.name: Coast gyms / surf-fitness
options.notes: Small; surf-and-yoga oriented

gym.running_options: Fort ramparts, beach paths
gym.yoga_pilates: Beach yoga and studios along the coast, English classes

day_trips.hiking: Coastal walks; Sinharaja rainforest further; the hill country by scenic train from nearby

sailing.notes: Galle is a real yacht stopover/harbour on Indian Ocean routes; some charter/whale-watching boats, informal sailing

new_hobbies.venue: Weligama surf schools / cooking classes
new_hobbies.notes: Weligama is Sri Lanka's top beginner-surf beach; Ceylon tea culture for your tea interest

options.name: South-coast surf-nomad camps (Weligama/Hiriketiya)
options.notes: Growing surf-and-coworking nomad cluster along the south coast

coworking_retreats.notes: South-coast surf-nomad scene is the real draw

car_free.walkability: Galle Fort very walkable; coast by tuk-tuk/scooter; scenic coastal train
car_free.public_transport: Coastal train + tuk-tuks (PickMe); scooter common
car_free.airport_to_center: Colombo (CMB) ~2h by expressway taxi (~25 EUR); or Mattala further

startup_community.scene_summary: Minimal formal scene, but a real and growing surf-nomad community along the south coast (Weligama/Hiriketiya). Lifestyle over networking.
startup_community.recurring_events: Seasonal nomad gatherings on the coast

bonus.flight_from_vie: 1-2 stops to Colombo (via Gulf hub) + 2h transfer; ~14-17h total
bonus.classical_music: Galle Literary Festival and arts events; little Western classical
bonus.dance_scene: Small Latin scene; surf/beach culture

summary: Mexico's second city and tech capital ('Mexican Silicon Valley'): a big startup/tech economy, mariachi-and-tequila heritage, mild climate, lakeside Chapala nearby. Same Mexico entry rules. The strongest work-and-network base in Mexico after CDMX.
remote_work_pitch: Guadalajara is Mexico's tech hub: the deepest startup and software economy outside CDMX, a proud cultural capital (mariachi, tequila country), a mild climate, and lower costs than the capital. For a recon it tests a work-and-network Mexican base with real substance; the nomad-tourist scene is smaller, but the professional one is strong.
best_months: Year-round mild; driest and clearest Nov-May

internet.notes: Strong fiber (tech city), reliable for calls; best startup infrastructure after CDMX

work_spots.name: WeWork / IconoSpace / local coworking
work_spots.notes: Real tech-coworking scene, booths, founder community

work_spots.name: Col. Americana / Chapultepec cafes
work_spots.notes: Hip cafe-and-nightlife district (one of the world's coolest neighborhoods), laptop-friendly

stays.name: Col. Americana / Providencia apartment
stays.why: Trendiest walkable districts, cafes and coworking, near nightlife

visa.details: Same Mexico rules: visa-free BYPASS with US/Canada/UK/Schengen/Japan visa, or SAE/consular for Turkish passports. Non-Schengen, no effect on 90/180.

air_water_safety.air_quality_notes: Some traffic haze; generally OK at altitude
air_water_safety.safety_notes: Central/upscale districts safe and walkable; normal big-city caution, use Uber at night

weather.conditions: Mild highland (1,560m), dry winter, rainy afternoons summer

options.name: Gym/club pools; Lake Chapala nearby
options.quality: Pools at gyms; Lake Chapala (~45min) for lakeside, not really swimming

options.name: Smart Fit / boutique gyms
options.notes: Good range

gym.running_options: Parque Metropolitano, Colomos forest, Chapultepec avenue

day_trips.hiking: Tequila town + agave fields (UNESCO), Bosque La Primavera, Lake Chapala/Ajijic, Barranca de Huentitán canyon

sailing.notes: Inland; Lake Chapala has minor boating, no sea sailing

new_hobbies.venue: Tequila tours / GDL dance schools
new_hobbies.notes: Birthplace of mariachi and tequila; lively salsa/bachata scene for your dance interest

coworking_retreats.notes: Business/tech coworking; few nomad retreats

car_free.walkability: Central districts very walkable; growing light-rail + Uber/bike-share
car_free.public_transport: Mi Macro/light rail + Uber + MiBici bike-share; no car needed centrally
car_free.airport_to_center: GDL ~30min by Uber (~15 EUR)

startup_community.scene_summary: Mexico's #2 startup/tech ecosystem ('Mexican Silicon Valley'): real software/hardware companies, accelerators, active events, English usable in the scene. Strong for work-and-network with lower costs and crowds than CDMX.

key_venues.name: WeWork / tech coworkings
key_venues.what: Startup community hubs

bonus.dance_scene: Strong salsa/bachata/mariachi scene, multiple weekly socials

summary: <cite index="9-16">Heraklion is more practical — faster internet, more coworking options, and better transport connections</cite> than other Crete cities, making it suitable for Riza's remote work needs. The city offers <cite index="1-4,1-10">90 Mbps internet and day passes from €12 at coworking spaces</cite>, with <cite index="24-1">perfect swimming conditions from late May to early October</cite>. However, the emerging tech scene is modest and suitable mainly for developers rather than AI indie-builders.
remote_work_pitch: <cite index="5-2,5-3">Heraklion offers reliable high-speed internet and coworking spaces; a cutting-edge data center is under construction</cite>. <cite index="9-16">Faster internet and more coworking options make it more practical for work than other Crete cities</cite>. The 7-day stay comfortably fits within budget: boutique hotel (~€70–100/night), coworking (~€12/day), and food (~€12–15/day) total roughly €800–900.
best_months: May, June, September, October (shoulder seasons: sunny, warm, fewer crowds; sea warm enough for swimming)

internet.notes: <cite index="9-15">Typical apartment speeds 20–50 Mbps (fiber expanding, VDSL common)</cite>. <cite index="1-4">Comeet Creative Space offers 90 Mbps</cite>. Mobile reliable on Cosmote/Vodafone; coworking spaces have dual lines.

work_spots.name: Comeet Creative Space
work_spots.notes: <cite index="1-4">90 Mbps, 4.9/5 rating</cite>. <cite index="6-11,6-12">Ideal for networking and meeting digital nomads; balance between productivity and relaxation for long-term stays</cite>.

work_spots.name: FuckUp Coworking Space
work_spots.notes: <cite index="7-2">Internet speed very good, space bright and airy, close to city center</cite>. <cite index="7-15">Free coffee included, friendly local team</cite>. Strong startup/founder community.

work_spots.name: Officenter
work_spots.notes: <cite index="3-4,3-16">Fast broadband with two internet lines, meeting room with HQ projector</cite>. <cite index="3-9">5 minutes from city center on foot</cite>.

work_spots.name: Asiento Offices
work_spots.best_for: professional work, tax domicile option, private meetings
work_spots.notes: <cite index="10-1,10-8">Fast internet, unlimited coffee/tea, personal lockers, air conditioning, LED lighting, meeting room with 43" smart screen</cite>.

stays.name: Lato Boutique Hotel
stays.why: <cite index="12-5,12-6">Rooftop restaurant-bar overlooking Venetian Fortress, free Wi-Fi, trendy rooms</cite>. Central, walkable, perfect for 7-day stay with buffer.

stays.name: DOM Boutique Hotel
stays.why: <cite index="18-26,18-27,18-28">Fitness center, shared lounge, bar; 24-hour front desk, airport transportation, free WiFi</cite>. Central location; strong value for short stay.

stays.name: 3 Pines Design Hotel
stays.why: <cite index="19-3,19-14">Large apartment-style rooms, bold contemporary style; 12 room-apartment hybrids with plant-filled terraces</cite>. Eco-friendly; kitchen useful for self-catering.

visa.details: Turkish citizen with Slovak residence permit has EU visa-free access for 90 days within 180-day rolling window (Schengen rules). No visa needed for short 7-day stay; Slovak Pobyt already provides EU legal residency.

air_water_safety.air_quality_notes: <cite index="5-1">Digital Realty constructing cutting-edge data center in Heraklion, signaling city infrastructure growth</cite>. Crete generally has good air quality; worst months are winter (heating). No major industrial pollution.
air_water_safety.safety_notes: <cite index="41-10,41-11">Heraklion is very safe city with busy, lived-in feel year-round; violent crime almost non-existent</cite>. <cite index="41-12">Use standard city smarts in crowded spots like Lions Square or central bus stations where pickpockets look for easy targets</cite>. <cite index="41-13">Most residents feel perfectly comfortable walking alone at night</cite>.

ecommerce_events.name: Startup Weekend Heraklion
ecommerce_events.relevance: Startup/entrepreneurship event; relevant for indie-hacker scene

weather.conditions: Mediterranean hot summer; mild, rainy winters

options.quality: Good; sandy beach, warm summer waters

options.name: Heraklion Waterfront (Venetian Harbor)
options.quality: Accessible; promenade swimmable in summer

options.name: Cretan Yoga & Pilates Studio
options.notes: English-friendly; typical drop-in class or package. Check availability locally.

gym.running_options: Waterfront promenade (Venetian harbor loop); Amoudara beach path; Rethymno road (car-free via bus then walk). Flatter city routes preferred.
gym.yoga_pilates: Cretan studios offer English classes; pricing €12–15/drop-in or €50–70/week packages.

day_trips.hiking: Samaria Gorge (45 km south; requires car or organized tour €55–70). Closer: <cite index="17-22">Samaria Gorge 11 km, longest & most beautiful in Europe</cite>. Dikti mountains accessible via car/bus from city center.

marinas.name: Heraklion Port / Venetian Harbor
marinas.transit_from_center: Walking distance; integrated into city center

sailing.best_season: <cite index="24-1,24-2">Late May to early October</cite>; <cite index="26-1">June, July, August, September, October have water temperatures 22–28°C</cite>. <cite index="26-23">Summer dry and hot, perfect for sailing</cite>.
sailing.notes: Aegean Sea offers consistent Etesian winds (Jun–Aug). Growing charter market; local schools offer intro lessons. Regattas occasionally held; check local event calendars.

new_hobbies.venue: Cretan Pottery Studio (Traditional)
new_hobbies.neighborhood: Old Town or suburban artisan zones
new_hobbies.notes: Crete has deep pottery tradition; wheel-throwing and hand-building classes widely available. Combine with local souvlaki culture.

new_hobbies.venue: Aegean Freediving Club (local school, est.)
new_hobbies.notes: Warm Mediterranean waters (summer). Intro courses 1–2 days; AIDA or CMAS certification available. Perfect complement to sailing.

new_hobbies.venue: Crete Paragliding Club / Mount Ida thermal sites
new_hobbies.neighborhood: Mountain launch sites near Ida, 20 km south
new_hobbies.notes: Crete's ridges and thermals offer excellent conditions. Intro tandem flight (100–150€) or multi-day course (500€+). Requires car rental or organized trip.

new_hobbies.venue: Cretan Cooking School / Local Taverna Workshops
new_hobbies.notes: 2–3 hour classes covering traditional Cretan dishes (dakos, saganaki, etc.). Includes market visit and communal meal. Fits Riza's interest in gastronomy.

coworking_retreats.notes: Heraklion lacks curated co-working retreats (Feel.travel, Outsite, Sun & Co, Coworkations not active in city). Closest: Paros has Selina (digital nomad village) ~2 hrs ferry away. Consider nearby Chania or book private villa + coworking day pass instead.

car_free.walkability: <cite index="4-13">Many streets pedestrian-only lined with small bars and cafes</cite>. Old Town and city center fully walkable; Ammoudara beach requires transit.
car_free.public_transport: <cite index="49-18">Public transportation reliable and affordable, buses primary mode</cite>. <cite index="50-25">City ride 1.8–2.5€, long-distance 8–15€</cite>. <cite index="45-17">Daily bus ticket costs €5</cite> (approx. current rate).
car_free.airport_to_center: <cite index="16-5">Heraklion Airport (HER) located 2 miles from city</cite>. Bus (€5–7) or taxi (€20–30, 15 min). Public transport covers all neighborhoods. No car essential.

startup_community.scene_summary: <cite index="32-1">Heraklion startup ecosystem grew -8.8% in 2025, ranks #860 globally with 7 startups</cite>. <cite index="34-3,34-5">City becoming interesting reference point for tech; communities raised since 2015 GDG founding; coworking spaces making first appearance; mindset embracing activities</cite>. Small but emerging. Better for developers than AI indie-hackers; no major VC presence.

key_venues.what: Founder/entrepreneur hub; mentorship culture; strong demo-day vibes

startup_community.recurring_events: DevStaff meetups (check Meetup); GDG events (sporadic); occasional startup workshops. No regular monthly demo nights or major investor pitches.

bonus.flight_from_vie: Direct flight ~2h 45 min, one-way €60–120 (Ryanair, Aegean); daily service from VIE (Nikola Tesla) to HER.
bonus.classical_music: <cite index="4-4">Archaeological Museum houses Minoan art; city has cultural venues</cite>. Heraklion Philharmonic Society & occasional classical concerts at cultural centers (check local listings). Not Vienna-level, but accessible. Summer open-air performances likely.
bonus.dance_scene: Bachata/Salsa: Limited formal scene; <cite index="41-15,41-16">English-speaking locals under 50 available as cultural guides</cite>. Some bars host Latin nights (summer); private lessons available via local dance instructors (€20–30/hr). Chania (30 km west) has larger Latin dance community.

concerns: Startup ecosystem small & developer-focused (not AI indie-builder friendly like Berlin/Athens). Limited investor networks or co-founder scene for Riza's Claude API + n8n projects.
No curated co-working retreats in city; nearest is Paros (2 hrs ferry). Must book standalone accommodation + daily coworking passes.
Latin dance scene (bachata/salsa) minimal; more common in larger cities. Private lessons available but limited community.
Timezone offset to Japan clients (UTC+3 vs UTC+9 = -6 hrs) means late-evening calls; less ideal than Vienna.
Winter weather (Nov–Feb) cold & rainy, though not harsh; not ideal for outdoor swimming/hiking Riza enjoys.
Phone booth availability in coworking spaces limited (only FuckUp, Officenter, Asiento explicitly confirm); plan for quiet phone call spaces in hotels/rooms.

summary: The Algarve's liveliest nomad town: golden cliffs, swimmable coves, a marina, surf, and a young social remote-work crowd, all on an easy -1h Vienna offset. Seasonal and pricier in summer, quiet and negotiable off-season. Schengen, so days count toward your 90/180.
remote_work_pitch: Lagos is the Algarve base that balances work and play: marina-view coworking (Lagos Avenida Cowork), 100-200 Mbps internet, dramatic beaches (Dona Ana, Camilo, Meia Praia) for swimming and sunrise runs, surf and sailing on the doorstep, and an active nomad community. Same easy EU time zone as the rest of Portugal; just use the October-to-May shoulder season for value.
best_months: April to June and September to October (warm, calmer, better value); busy expensive July-August

internet.notes: 100-200 Mbps fiber in town; reliable for calls, with cheap eSIM backup

visa.details: Visa-free on your Slovak permit, but Schengen so days COUNT toward your 90/180; D8 nomad visa for longer stays. Counts toward Schengen.

air_water_safety.safety_notes: Very safe; usual summer-crowd petty theft. Atlantic currents at exposed beaches warrant care

sailing.notes: Marina with charters, day sails and dolphin/coast trips; a genuine sailing base

new_hobbies.notes: The western Algarve is a prime surf coast; a natural new water skill

car_free.airport_to_center: Faro (FAO) ~1h by train/bus (cheap) or ~80 EUR taxi

summary: Lefkada is a scenic Ionian island ideal for Riza's 7-day stay, offering a single dedicated coworking space (Lefco.work) plus laptop-friendly cafes, moderate internet (10–15 Mbps), excellent sailing/swimming, and relaxed outdoor culture. Budget fits: €800–1,200 for accommodation + €300 coworking + €400 food = €1,500–1,900 total. Best for shoulder season (May, September–October) to avoid summer crowds while maintaining good conditions.
remote_work_pitch: Lefkada combines Greece's charm with practical nomad infrastructure: one dedicated coworking space (Lefco.work), reliable cafe backup for calls, and direct beach access for swimming/sailing between calls. EU residence permit (Slovak Pobyt) clears visa worries entirely. Wind-stable Ionian sailing season, abundant hiking, and no-car transit via local bus/walking suit Riza's car-free requirement perfectly.
best_months: May, June, September, October (weather, sailing conditions, fewer tourists)

internet.notes: Slower than Vienna but workable; check venue wifi in advance. Mobile backup recommended. Apanema Mindfulness Resort offers Starlink for backup.

work_spots.name: Lefco.work
work_spots.notes: Only dedicated coworking on island. Community of digital nomads. Quiet call booths. Book ahead for 7-day pass to negotiate rate.

work_spots.name: Levante Cafe Bar (Nidri)
work_spots.notes: Sea views, reliable wifi, calm mornings. Expect busier afternoons.

work_spots.name: Kalypso Bar (Lefkada Town)
work_spots.notes: Central, lively. Good for casual work; noisier late afternoon.

work_spots.name: Porto Cafe (Vasiliki)
work_spots.notes: Windsurf mecca; calm mornings ideal for deep work. WiFi quality variable; arrive early.

stays.name: Ionian Blue Luxury Apartments
stays.why: Central Nidri location, kitchenette for black tea prep, WiFi, 7-day nightly rate offers flexibility. Well-reviewed by nomads.

stays.name: Apanema Mindfulness Resort
stays.why: Starlink (fast internet for calls), pool, sauna, quiet retreat. Open year-round. Premium for reliability. All-inclusive meals reduce food budget.

stays.name: Hotel Lefkas (Lefkada Town)
stays.why: Budget-friendly, walking distance to Lefco.work and cafes, breakfast included. Straightforward hotel service; good for short nomadic stays.

visa.details: Turkish citizens normally require Schengen visa for Greece. However, Riza holds Slovak Pobyt (EU residence permit). Under EU law, Slovak residence permit grants Schengen mobility. 90/180 rule applies to non-EU visitors; Riza's status as Slovak resident exempts him—treated as intra-Schengen travel. Confirm with Greek consulate that Pobyt permit covers Greece. No extension or visa-window concern for 7-day trip.

air_water_safety.air_quality_notes: Ionian sea breeze, low industry. AQI typically 30–50. Best months: May–October (minimal dust). Winter (Dec–Feb) occasional rain but no hazardous smog.
air_water_safety.safety_notes: Low street crime; safe to walk alone day/night in Lefkada Town and main villages (Nidri, Vasiliki). Petty theft in crowded summer areas; avoid leaving valuables in cars (Riza car-free anyway). Locals friendly; no scams reported targeting foreigners. Standard EU safety.

ecommerce_events.name: Trata Festival
ecommerce_events.relevance: Adjacent – celebrates sailors/fishermen; draws local business, craft vendors. Not ecommerce-specific but community gathering relevant to understanding local economy.

weather.conditions: Mediterranean; June–September sunny, hot, dry. May & Oct pleasant, mild rain possible. Winter cool, occasional storms.

options.name: Porto Katsiki Beach
options.quality: Pristine, turquoise, steep cliff access

options.name: Egremni Beach
options.quality: White sand, cliff-backed, dramatic

options.name: Nidri beaches (east coast)
options.quality: Sheltered, calm, easy access
options.transit_from_center: Walking distance if staying Nidri; 10min bus from town

options.name: Apanema Mindfulness Resort pool
options.quality: Year-round, 70 sqm, heated
options.transit_from_center: On-site if staying there; 15min drive otherwise

options.name: Lefkada Fitness Club
options.facilities: Cardio machines, free weights, functional area, classes
options.notes: Central location. Welcoming English-speaking staff. Modern equipment.

options.name: Fitness Plus (Nidri)
options.notes: Quieter than town gym; good for early morning cardio. English spoken.

options.name: Hotel Wellness Centers (Ionian Blue, Apanema)
options.facilities: In-hotel gyms, pools, sauna (Apanema), basic cardio/weights
options.notes: Free for guests; non-guests may request day access (€8–15). Apanema includes sauna.

gym.running_options: Lefkada Town waterfront loop (~5km); Nidri harbor path (~3km); mountain trails near Apanema (varied difficulty). No dedicated running clubs but local routes well-established.
gym.yoga_pilates: Apanema Mindfulness Resort offers daily yoga/Tai Chi classes (included for guests). Lefkada Town: casual studios advertise seasonally; English classes available May–Sept. Best for dedicated classes: book Apanema stay.

day_trips.hiking: Nydri Waterfalls (~15min drive, 1.5hr round-trip hike); Mount Sappho summits; Lefkada Town to Gyra Beach loop (~2hrs). Trails well-marked, bus/taxi accessible. Early morning hikes avoid heat & crowds.

marinas.name: Lefkas Marina
marinas.transit_from_center: Walking distance; 5min walk from town center

marinas.name: Nidri Marina
marinas.transit_from_center: 10min bus or 25min walk from Lefkada Town

marinas.name: Vliho Sail Ionian
marinas.transit_from_center: 20min drive or 40min bus from Lefkada Town

sailing.best_season: May–October (stable winds, warm water, calm mornings); June–Sept peak. Avoid Dec–Feb (storms, strong northerlies).
sailing.notes: East coast (Nidri, Vliho) ideal for intro sailing: sheltered, small waves, protected by mainland. West coast (exposed, open Ionian) for advanced sailors. Maistro wind (NW) May–Sept afternoon, F3–F6, perfect for windsurfing (Vasiliki famous). Regattas & local sailing clubs active summer.

new_hobbies.venue: Freediving Academy Lefkada (Nidri or Vasiliki)
new_hobbies.notes: Ionian turquoise waters, calm east coast perfect for intro courses. Schools offer AIDA/CMAS certification. May–Oct season.

new_hobbies.venue: Vasiliki Windsurf School
new_hobbies.notes: Vasiliki is world-renowned for consistent Maistro wind (May–Sept afternoons, F3–6). Beginner-friendly shallow bay with schools offering rentals & lessons. Riza's sailing interest dovetails perfectly.

new_hobbies.venue: Self-guided + local guides (Lefkada Photo Tours)
new_hobbies.notes: Dramatic clifftop beaches, turquoise waters, golden hour light ideal for landscape/astrophotography. Tour guides available; Riza can self-explore with smartphone/camera via hiking & sailing trips.

new_hobbies.venue: Lefkada Paragliding School (Mt. Sappho launch site)
new_hobbies.notes: Thermal winds from Mount Sappho May–Oct; views over Ionian. Tandem intro flights available. Hiking base + aerial adventure combo.

new_hobbies.venue: Nydri Craft Studio (seasonal workshop)
new_hobbies.notes: Small studio runs seasonal classes May–Sept. Greek ceramic traditions. Part of broader Lefkada artisan revival; pairs well with cultural immersion.

coworking_retreats.notes: No curated co-working retreat operators (Feel.travel, Coworkations, Outsite, Sun and Co, NomadX, Mokrin House, Work Wanderers) currently listed as running group retreats in/near Lefkada. Apanema Mindfulness Resort hosts individual wellness retreats and can accommodate small groups, but operates as a boutique retreat venue rather than a structured co-working retreat. Lefco.work is solo/nomad-focused (not group-retreat-based).

car_free.walkability: Lefkada Town: Very walkable (compact center, pedestrian zones). Nidri: walkable for harbor/cafes; beaches require bus or taxi. Vasiliki: walkable harbor; southern beaches need transport. Rating Good overall; realistic for 7-day stay with good planning.
car_free.public_transport: KTEL buses connect main towns (Lefkada Town ↔ Nidri, Vasiliki, Agios Nikitas). Routes reliable May–Oct (peak season); less frequent Nov–Apr. Day pass ~€5. Taxis available; standard meter ~€5–15 in-town, €15–40 inter-village. Water taxis serve beach clubs (seasonal).
car_free.airport_to_center: Preveza Airport (Aktion) 30min drive (€40 taxi). No direct bus; arrange hotel shuttle (€20–30) or share taxi with other arrivals. Planning ahead recommended. Once on island, car-free transit via bus/foot/occasional taxi works.

startup_community.scene_summary: Lefkada lacks a visible tech/startup ecosystem. No dedicated co-working hubs beyond Lefco.work, no regular demo nights, no founder meetups advertised. Island economy tourism-focused (sailing, beach, hospitality). Athens dominates Greek startup scene; Lefkada appeals to remote workers & lifestyle founders, not venture-backed startups. Riza's AI/indie-hacker interests better served in larger cities.

key_venues.name: Lefco.work
key_venues.what: Digital nomad hub; informal networking among remote workers. Occasional visiting speaker events (check site).

startup_community.recurring_events: None documented. Summer tourist season may host ad-hoc networking brunches at cafes; no formal meetup calendar. Riza should leverage online communities (IH, Lu.ma, Marketplace Pulse) rather than expect local events.

bonus.flight_from_vie: Vienna (VIE) → Preveza (PVX) ~3–3.5 hours, typically 1 stop (via Athens or a Central European hub). Direct flights rare; best connections via Athens. Budget carriers (Ryanair, easyJet) ~€80–180 return mid-season. Flight cost ~€150 (estimate).

timezones.vs_vienna: +1 hour (Greece UTC+2 summer, UTC+3 in May–Sept; Vienna UTC+1 summer, UTC+2 winter)
timezones.vs_london: +2 hours (UK typically UTC+0 winter, UTC+1 summer; Greece UTC+2 winter, UTC+3 summer)

bonus.classical_music: Lefkada has no symphony orchestra or classical concert hall. Greek National Opera occasionally tours; check summer festival listings. Best classical experience: day trip to Athens (Megaron Mousikis concert hall, 4hr ferry/bus). On-island: classical music cafes in Lefkada Town (informal, check seasonal). Riza should plan classical concert outings via Athens day trips.
bonus.dance_scene: Bachata/Salsa: No dedicated Latin dance studios on island. Summer tourist bars (Levante Cafe, waterfront clubs Nidri/Vasiliki) occasionally host DJ nights with Latin tracks. Organized classes unavailable; Riza can self-learn via YouTube/apps or join informal social dancing at summer beach parties. For structured lessons, Vienna-based remote learning recommended.

concerns: Internet speed (10–15 Mbps fixed, variable cafe WiFi) may challenge 3+ hour video calls with Amazon clients in UK/UAE/Japan. Recommend coworking (Lefco.work phone booth) for critical client calls; backup 4G mobile hotspot essential.
No startup/indie-hacker community; limited networking for AI product builders. Consider virtual meetups (Lu.ma, IH) to stay connected.
Seasonal tourism volatility: May–Oct accommodation/restaurant availability excellent; Nov–Apr (low season) some venues close. 7-day stay in shoulder months (May, Sept–Oct) minimizes disruption.
Sailing season peaks May–Sept due to Maistro wind; winter (Dec–Feb) less reliable for casual sailing outings.

No direct city buses to key hiking trailheads; taxi/rental bike combination sometimes needed (conflicts with car-free preference; bus coverage adequate for 7 days but less flexible than personal transport).

summary: The Costa del Sol's upscale resort: 300+ days of sunshine, long beaches, the Puerto Banus yacht marina, golf and padel everywhere, a big international expat community, and fast fiber. Pricey by Spanish standards but a strong warm-winter base. CET (same hours as Vienna); counts toward Schengen.
remote_work_pitch: Marbella is the sunny, polished, winter-warm option: 300+ sunny days, reliable 300+ Mbps fiber, a large English-speaking expat community, marinas and golf, and a growing crop of coworking spaces. It's more expensive than the rest of Andalusia (especially the Golden Mile and Puerto Banus) but cheaper than the big northern-European cities, and it's same-timezone with home. Great as a comfortable cool-season base; quieter, less 'authentic' than inland Spain.
best_months: Year-round mild; especially good October to May as a warm-winter base

internet.notes: Most modern flats and coworking offer 300+ Mbps fiber

work_spots.name: Coworking hubs (centre / San Pedro)

summary: Western France's creative-tech city on the Loire: a UNESCO heritage centre, the Mechanical Elephants of Île de Nantes, Atlantic an hour west, mild oceanic climate, real French Tech scene. Schengen (90/180). A characterful mid-size base, with grey damp weather as the trade-off.
remote_work_pitch: Nantes is one of France's quiet success stories: a creative-and-tech mid-size city (the Île de Nantes regeneration is a model), the iconic Machines de l'île, an old ducal castle, and the Atlantic an hour west. For a recon it tests creative-and-tech Atlantic France; same time zone, direct/connecting flights, days count toward 90/180. The trade-off is the grey oceanic climate.

summary: Serbia's relaxed cultural capital on the Danube: a charming European Capital of Culture with the hilltop Petrovaradin Fortress (home of the EXIT festival), a thriving coworking scene, fast cheap fiber, and low costs. A laid-back Balkan base that doesn't burn Schengen days. Non-Schengen; CET (same hours as Vienna).
remote_work_pitch: Novi Sad is the easygoing, affordable Balkan base that's same-timezone with home and doesn't touch your Schengen clock: a handsome Danube city (a European Capital of Culture) with the dramatic Petrovaradin Fortress, fast cheap fiber, a genuinely thriving coworking community for its size, riverside cafes, and Fruska Gora national park on the doorstep for hiking. It's smaller and calmer than Belgrade (1.5h away) but keeps the work infrastructure. As a Turkish citizen you enter Serbia visa-free. Great as a low-cost pressure-release stint between Schengen stays.
best_months: May to June and September to October; warm summers (EXIT festival in July), cold winters

internet.notes: Fast, cheap fiber widely available; strong cafe/coworking wifi

summary: Palermo offers exceptional value (€80–120/night accommodation, <€5 meals), solid coworking with phone booths (€15/day passes), and emerging AI/startup community—but internet is unreliable, sanitation issues persist, and Schengen visa-free status suits Riza's EU mobility. The 10-day €1200 budget is comfortably achievable. Best for March–May and September–October.
remote_work_pitch: Palermo combines cheap living, multiple coworking spaces with phone booths (Critical for client calls), and a growing AI/fintech startup hub (bitRocket Studio, Magnisi) attracting founders like Riza. Transit-free walkability + excellent Italian culture make it ideal for 10-day sprints. But expect variable WiFi—have a backup plan.
best_months: March–May, September–October (mild weather, low crowds, stable internet); avoid July–August (heat, tourists, reduced coworking hours)

internet.notes: Hit-or-miss; coworking spaces (neu [nòi], Magnisi Studio, Changes) offer reliable fiber. Some apartments/cafes tested 80/80 Mbps, others struggled. EU roaming solid on Vodafone/TIM. Backup SIM recommended.

work_spots.name: neu [nòi]
work_spots.notes: Bright, spacious, courtyard light. Free moka coffee. Fully equipped kitchen (owners cook lunch with coworkers). Monthly: €350–450. Hosts international professionals. Updated Jan 2026.

work_spots.name: Magnisi Studio
work_spots.notes: Modern, plants, wood floors. Quiet + dynamic areas. Phone booths available. Monthly includes 2 guest passes, storage. AI/startup focus—hosts investor pitches.

work_spots.name: Changes Coworking
work_spots.notes: 1500 sqm, 20 private offices, event hall. Ultra-fast fiber. Workshops, thematic events. Near train station. Monthly coworking + events ecosystem.

work_spots.name: Moltivolti
work_spots.notes: Restaurant + coworking hybrid (2014, 14-founder international). Plug sockets, good WiFi, fresh market food nearby. Welcoming to nomads. Laid-back vibe.

stays.name: Cortile dei Giusti - Combo Guesthouse
stays.why: Central, highly rated (5/5), shared kitchen + lounge, garden. Express check-in. Historic building. Leaves €350 budget for coworking+food.

stays.name: Punto e al Capo (Airbnb)
stays.why: 2BR apartment, kitchen, balcony overlooking historic Capo market. Walk to coworking. Leaves €450 for coworking+food+transport.

stays.name: La Terrazza Sul Centro (Hotel)
stays.why: 3-star, rooftop breakfast with city views, central location. Moderate price. €250 remaining for coworking+meals.

visa.details: Turkish citizen with Slovak Pobyt residence permit. EU/EEA visa-free entry; max 90 days in Schengen zone per 180-day rolling window. Residence permit holders exempt from tourist limitations (can stay indefinitely). No visa needed for 10-day stay. At 60+ days, register with Italian police (anagrafe) if planning longer stays.

air_water_safety.air_quality_notes: AQI typically 40–60 (Good); worst months July–September (traffic, heat). Waterfront areas better. WAQI data supports 'clean air' tag on Nomads.com.
air_water_safety.safety_notes: Centro Storico (tourist areas, coworking zones) very safe day/evening. Avoid Borgo Vecchio after dark (authentic but petty theft reported). Historic center (Kalsa, Vucciria, Capo) walkable, vibrant, well-lit. Solo night walks: stick to main streets. Scams rare; pickpocketing in crowded markets—standard urban caution. Nomads report feeling safe overall.

ecommerce_events.name: Bravo Innovation Hub (Cantieri Culturali alla Zisa)
ecommerce_events.relevance: Startup pitch events, networking with founders. Adjacent to Riza's AI builder interests.

ecommerce_events.name: Magnisi AI Club (Monthly In-Person)
ecommerce_events.relevance: AI, founders, market strategy. Direct alignment with Riza's AI/indie-hacker scene.

weather.conditions: Spring (May): mild, sunny. Autumn (Sept–Oct): warm, occasional rain.

options.name: Mondello Beach
options.quality: Sandy, shallow, busy

options.name: Isola delle Femmine (nature reserve)
options.quality: Crystalline, rocky, pristine

options.name: Piscina Olimpionica Malaspina (Public Pool)
options.quality: Olympic-sized, well-maintained

options.name: Palermo Fitness Club
options.facilities: Free weights, cardio machines, classes, sauna
options.notes: Popular, central, modern. Italian-speaking but welcoming. No pool.

options.name: Aquatic Club
options.notes: Affordable, includes Olympic pool. Family-oriented vibe. Convenient for cardio + swimming combo.

options.name: Energia Fitness
options.notes: Near beach. Good for morning swim + gym routine. Tram 1 access.

gym.running_options: Foro Italico (waterfront park): 3 km loop, flat, scenic. Start from Politeama; 15-min walk.
gym.yoga_pilates: Yoga Natura (Centro Storico): classes €12–15/drop-in, English+Italian. Strong vinyasa community.

day_trips.hiking: Mondello Cliffs (30 min bus + 1 hr hike to viewpoint). Aspromonte Regional Park (90 min train; alpine trails). Riserva dello Zingaro (60 min bus; coastal Sicilian hike, pristine beaches). All transit-accessible.

schools.name: Isola delle Femmine Sailing School (Freedome/SicilYing partner)
schools.certification: IYT (International Yacht Training); beginner intro + certification track

schools.name: Palermo Sailing (independent school, city waterfront)
schools.certification: Local Italian federation; intro lessons available

sailing.best_season: April–May, September–October (calm seas, warm, fewer tourists)
sailing.notes: Tyrrhenian Sea: moderate winds (12–18 knots spring/autumn). Sailing hub: small fleet, tight-knit scene. Intro packages common (6×1-hr lessons €180–200). Regattas rare but occasional summer events. Scenic: Aeolian Islands day-trips (3 hrs offshore) popular.

new_hobbies.venue: Gattopardo Ceramics Studio (centro storico)
new_hobbies.notes: Palermo historically a pottery hub. Studio offers weekend classes, wheel-throwing basics. Pairs well with Sicilian artisan culture. 2–3 hr sessions.

new_hobbies.venue: Studio Danza Palazzo Mirto
new_hobbies.notes: Palermo's Arab-Norman heritage → fusion dance scene. Spanish-influenced choreography. Drop-in classes welcome. Community-driven.

new_hobbies.venue: Officina Creativa (artist collective, Cantieri Culturali alla Zisa)
new_hobbies.notes: Palermo's street-art/urban culture. Zisa is creative hub. Perfect for AI builder wanting hands-on analog craft. Workshops rolling.

new_hobbies.venue: ConversazionEtaliana (Politeama area)
new_hobbies.notes: Riza speaks B1 German; Italian similar. Conversation circles 2x/week. €15 drop-in. Helps with local integration, client rapport.

coworking_retreats.notes: No curated co-living retreats (Feel.travel, Coworkations, Outsite, etc.) currently active in Palermo. Sicily's remote-work retreat scene is nascent. Magnisi Studio + changes Coworking act as informal community hubs but are day-pass focused, not 7–14 day group experiences. Recommend contacting Magnisi directly for possible founder/AI cohort events.

car_free.walkability: Excellent within Centro Storico (Kalsa, Vucciria, Capo, Politeama); compact ~2 km radius. Cobblestones, narrow streets, lively pedestrian culture. Markets, cafes, coworking all walkable. Outlying areas (Mondello, Zisa) require transit.
car_free.public_transport: AMAT buses + Tram 1 (Politeama–Mondello line). Integrated tickets: €1.80 single (90 min), €5 day pass, €35 monthly. Reliable daytime; some evening gaps. WiFi on some trams. Slow but comprehensive. Taxis backup (€45 to airport, €8–12 local rides).
car_free.airport_to_center: Falcone-Borsellino Airport (Mondello, 35 km west). Bus 444 'Prestia & Comandè' direct to city center (~50 min, €7, daytime frequent). Tram 1 connects nearby. Taxi: €45–50 (avoid unlicensed). Transit: cheapest + reliable for Riza (car-free profile).

startup_community.scene_summary: Palermo's AI/startup scene is emerging (2–3 years old), punching above expected weight for a Southern Italian city. bitRocket Studio (AI fintech hub, 18+ collaborators), Magnisi (AI impact venture with onboarding). Government backing (Bravo Innovation Hub, regional initiatives). Not SF/Berlin scale, but genuine momentum—founders return from north Italy, Dubai, abroad. English limited among local entrepreneurs; Italian/EU networks strong. Suitable for Riza (indie AI builder, n8n/Claude API focus); expect tight-knit, English-sparse but welcoming circles.

key_venues.name: bitRocket Studio
key_venues.what: First AI innovation hub (2023+). Fintech, AI research. Community of developers, startup founders, mentors. Free training model (99% retention). Partners: Unicredit, Banca Sella. On-site: Via Vittorio Emanuele 188.

key_venues.name: Magnisi Studio + Magnisi Venture
key_venues.what: AI-focused venture studio. Founder-first. Monthly AI Club meetups (continuous learning). Portfolio: Edgemony, DigitalMakers, LexHero, MUV. Onboarding: Palermo → Dubai. Piazza Politeama hub.

key_venues.name: Cantieri Culturali alla Zisa
key_venues.what: Hosts Bravo Innovation Hub cohorts (12-week programs, 20 startups/cohort). Cre.Zi coworking. ThinkYoung hackathons. Community events, demos, networking.

key_venues.name: Vulcano (AI Startup Studio)
key_venues.what: Europe-focused AI startup accelerator, based Palermo. Builds, funds, advises tech startups. Emerging presence; newer to scene (2024+).

startup_community.recurring_events: Magnisi monthly AI Club (in-person, founder talks); Bravo Innovation Hub demos (quarterly); TEDx Palermo (2026); university hackathons (ad-hoc); digital nomad meetups (informal, Digital Nomads Palermo group on social media).

bonus.flight_from_vie: Vienna (VIE) to Palermo (PMO): ~2.5 hrs direct (Ryanair, easyJet typical). €30–80 if booked 3+ weeks ahead. Morning flights recommended (coworking same day). Return: same.
bonus.classical_music: Teatro Politeama (concert hall, Via Maqueda): season Oct–May. Historic neoclassical venue. World-class orchestras, chamber music. €15–50 tickets. Season schedule online. Sinfonica Siciliana performs regularly. Cathedral (Cattedrale di Palermo): occasional baroque/choral concerts (free/donation). High-quality but limited English program notes.
bonus.dance_scene: Strong bachata/salsa presence: multiple studios, weekly socials. Palermo Salsa Club (regular Saturday socials). + emerging flamenco/fusion scene (Gattopardo, Studio Palazzo Mirto). Latin dance culture embedded; Riza's interests aligned.

concerns: Internet reliability: hit-or-miss outside coworking. Backup mobile plan (SIM card TIM/Vodafone) and tethering recommended for client calls.
City cleanliness: trash accumulation in some neighborhoods (Borgo Vecchio, outer Zisa); centro storico maintained but occasional sanitation issues. Monitor walking routes.
Startup community small + Italian-language heavy: networking requires Italian fluency or local introduction. Riza's B1 German helps, but Italian different. Plan language meetups.
Limited nightlife/clubs if seeking evening energy beyond bars/cafes; pace is slower than Northern Europe—not an issue for focused work but matters for team hangouts.
August closure: many restaurants/coworking reduce hours mid-July–August. 10-day stay fine; longer bookings avoid August.
No curated co-working retreats: expect day-pass coworking, not immersive group stays. Community-building more organic/ad-hoc.

summary: Cyprus's mild-climate Mediterranean resort city: UNESCO archaeology, Aphrodite's Rock, the warmest winters on the island, and a big established expat/retiree community. Non-Schengen EU. A calmer, gentler base than Limassol — beach-and-ruins, with a smaller work scene.
remote_work_pitch: Paphos is the warmest, gentlest corner of Cyprus: a UNESCO archaeological site (Roman mosaics, Tombs of the Kings), mild Mediterranean winters (often the warmest spot in the EU in January), an established British/Scandinavian expat scene, and good beaches. For a recon it tests calm Mediterranean Cyprus; the work scene is lighter than Limassol's, but it's gentler and well-set-up for remote-working retirees and nomads. Same Cyprus visa/Pobyt verification applies.
best_months: Year-round mild; sunniest Apr-Oct, warmest winter on the island (~18C high in Jan)

summary: Plovdiv offers excellent value (under €600 for 7 days accommodation+work), blazing internet, and visa-free access for Riza as a Slovak Pobyt holder. However, the expat/startup scene is small and quiet compared to other digital nomad hubs, and the local culture emphasizes smoking and conservative business hours.
remote_work_pitch: Fast, affordable, visa-free. <cite index="3-10">Bulgaria has some of the fastest internet in the world at low prices.</cite> The Old Town and Kapana district offer walkable work cafes, and coworking costs €5–12/day. But be aware: <cite index="17-9">Plovdiv's expat scene is very small and lacks regular social activities</cite>, making it less ideal for long-term community or retreats.
best_months: April–May, September–October (spring/fall shoulder seasons; mild weather, fewer crowds, festivals)

internet.notes: <cite index="10-17">Average internet speeds estimated at up to 100 Mbps</cite>. <cite index="18-14,18-16">Vivacom provides 110.7 Mb/s; A1 mobile delivers 189.5 Mb/s</cite>. Fiber widely available, call reliability excellent.

work_spots.name: Cat and Mouse Coworking
work_spots.notes: <cite index="3-2">Part of network with Mouse House guesthouse, convenient for short trips</cite>. Brewery on-site, welcoming to long-form work.

work_spots.name: Networking Premium Coworking
work_spots.notes: <cite index="3-3,3-4,3-5,3-6">Well-loved favorite with sprawling open layout, private offices, kitchen; hosts events; part of 50+ location network</cite>.

work_spots.name: Coffee Therapy (Kapana)
work_spots.notes: <cite index="1-2">Coffee places in Bulgaria not frowning upon digital nomads</cite>. Good WiFi, good vibes, accepts long sessions. No dedicated booths but generally quiet morning hours.

work_spots.name: IMD 24/7 Coworking
work_spots.best_for: Night owls, budget-conscious, solo focus work
work_spots.notes: <cite index="3-8,3-9">Most affordable option, basic setup, ideal for non-traditional hours</cite>.

stays.name: Villa Flavia Heritage Boutique Hotel
stays.why: <cite index="27-1,27-2,27-3">2-minute walk from Roman Theater, gem location in historic charm with peaceful retreat. Staff go above and beyond</cite>. Central, excellent for coworking access. ~€595 for 7 nights fits budget.

stays.name: Boutique Hotel Boris Palace & Restaurant
stays.why: <cite index="26-28">Within easy walking distance of Old Town, boutique feel</cite> (US$82 ≈ €75–78 at current rates, market offers €70 nightly for 7-day bookings). Restaurant on-site, good for client dinners.

stays.name: Hotel Jägerhof
stays.why: Budget-friendly 3-star option, gym on-site. <cite index="36-11,36-12,36-13">Convenient, 5km from Central Station, 16km from Airport</cite>. ~€385 for 7 nights leaves €1,615 for coworking + food (ample margin).

visa.details: Turkish citizen with Slovak Pobyt (residence permit): EU/EEA visa-free. Bulgaria is non-Schengen, separate 90-day tourist visa-free entry. <cite index="3-14,3-15">Bulgaria is non-Schengen with 90-day tourist visa; great for spending 3 months outside Schengen zone before other EU adventures</cite>. After 90 days, residence permit or visa-on-arrival options available.

air_water_safety.air_quality_notes: <cite index="17-2">Current air quality great at 37 µg/m3</cite>. <cite index="17-6,17-7">Air pollution from vehicles is consistent issue; smokers can smoke freely inside restaurants</cite>. Worst months likely winter (heating season).
air_water_safety.safety_notes: <cite index="3-21,3-22,3-23,3-24">Plovdiv is very safe—one of safest destinations in Europe, low crime, well-lit streets, clean public spaces. Solo female travelers favor it</cite>. Watch for taxi scams at airport.

weather.conditions: Mild spring/fall; warm, dry summers; cold winters

options.name: Neptune Swimming Complex
options.quality: Olympic-size (50m/20m), small (25m/12.5m), plus leisure pool 800m²

options.name: Sila Sports Complex
options.quality: <cite index="30-9,30-10">25-meter pool, coaching available, group workouts, boxing ring, spa</cite>

options.name: Colibri Pool Bar
options.quality: 22-meter leisure pool, Jacuzzi, tropical vibe, snacks

options.name: Pulse Plovdiv
options.facilities: <cite index="34-8,34-11,34-13,34-14,34-16,34-18">Cardio (50+ Life Fitness machines), 1,000m² strength zone, Kangoo Jumps, spinning, SPA (pools, sauna, steam, relaxation)</cite>
options.notes: Premium, friendly staff, excellent condition. Call-friendly environment; ask for 7-day pass (~€50).

options.name: Total Sport Fitness & Squash
options.facilities: <cite index="30-6,30-7">Latest-generation equipment, Cybex Eagle brand, proven instructors</cite>
options.notes: Well-regarded, professional. Squash courts available.

options.name: Athletic Fitness Clubs
options.neighborhood: Multiple (Plovdiv Plaza 2nd fl., Markovo Tepe Mall 3rd fl., Westpoint)
options.facilities: Cardio, weights, personal training, modern equipment
options.notes: <cite index="37-4">Numerous fitness centers offering day passes and one-off access</cite>. Budget-friendly, traveler-friendly (TrainAway integration).

gym.running_options: <cite index="40-15,40-16,40-17,40-18">Plovdiv is bicycle-friendly with newly-built 48km cycling trail network covering city center, main boulevards, riverbank, Rowing Canal</cite>. Tsar Simeon Park best urban run; riverside paths along Maritsa.
gym.yoga_pilates: <cite index="30-17,30-18">Aerobic Studio M offers high-intensity combat, callanetics, pilates</cite>. <cite index="32-6">Ramada Trimontium sports center has water aerobics and yoga classes</cite>. Most taught in Bulgarian; English-taught classes rare.

day_trips.hiking: <cite index="10-32">Asen's Fortress nearby, stone city, surrounding villages accessible</cite>. Rodopi Mountains foothills ~1.5 hours by bus. Trigrad Gorge (waterfall, hiking) ~2 hours. Shuttle/bus from city center; car-free doable but slower.

sailing.notes: Plovdiv is inland (Maritsa River). No sea access. Not a viable sailing destination.

new_hobbies.venue: Boris Palace Winery (on-site)
new_hobbies.notes: Bulgaria famous for wine/rakia production. <cite index="37-37">Plovdiv known for powerful 'Rakia' brewed since 1000s</cite>. Wine-bar culture strong; tasting events regular in Kapana.

new_hobbies.venue: Kapana Artisan Studios (collective)
new_hobbies.notes: <cite index="22-20">Kapana transformed into Bulgaria's coolest creative quarter</cite>. Active artist community; drop-in pottery workshops available.

new_hobbies.venue: Art Gallery & Workshop Space, Kapana
new_hobbies.notes: <cite index="8-3">City's vibrant creative scene, art exhibitions and festivals create inspiring atmosphere</cite>. Regular open-studio nights in Kapana; welcoming to travelers.

new_hobbies.venue: Cultural House Svoboda or Dance Studios, Kapana
new_hobbies.notes: Folk traditions strong in Plovdiv; dance workshops less formal than salsa/bachata but available. <cite index="4-27">Bulgaria loves festivals and cultural events</cite>.

coworking_retreats.notes: No organized co-working retreats (Feel.travel, Coworkations, Outsite, Sun and Co, NomadX, Mokrin House, Work Wanderers) currently operating in or near Plovdiv. Sofia or Bansko (2–3 hours away) may host retreats; Plovdiv lacks curated group-stay infrastructure.

car_free.walkability: <cite index="3-18">20-minute walking radius in city center, mostly car-free streets</cite>. Old Town, Kapana, main boulevard pedestrian-friendly. Hilly but manageable.
car_free.public_transport: <cite index="39-28">Bus ticket 1 lev (€0.50)</cite>. Buses reliable, 5:30am–11pm. Day pass ~€1.50–€2 (estimate). No metro/tram. Shakier outside center but walkable routes abundant.
car_free.airport_to_center: <cite index="39-1">Taxi €8–10 (15–20 BGN) from airport to city</cite>. ~15km, 20 min. No shuttle bus; public transport doable (45 min bus + walk) but taxi cheaper. Return €9–10 via TaxiMe app.

startup_community.scene_summary: <cite index="1-8,1-11">Plovdiv emerging hotspot for digital nomads with creative energy; growing community but less going on than larger hubs</cite>. <cite index="17-9,17-19">Expat scene very small, lacks regular social activities; even in peak season quiet with little to do</cite>. No known startup incubators, accelerators, or pitch events.

key_venues.name: Networking Premium Coworking
key_venues.what: Events, networking, occasional talks

key_venues.name: Cat and Mouse Coworking / Brewery
key_venues.what: Informal meetups, nomad hangouts

startup_community.recurring_events: Ad-hoc meetups via Facebook (Expats in Plovdiv group). No regular demo days, pitch nights, or founder meetups documented.

bonus.flight_from_vie: ~2 hours, one-stop via Sofia (Ryanair/Wizz Air) or 3.5 hours driving. Direct flights rare; Vienna → Sofia → Plovdiv (internal flight or 2-hour bus) typical. Budget €30–80 return Wizz.
bonus.classical_music: Plovdiv Philharmonic Orchestra (seasonal concerts Sept–May); ancient Roman Theater hosts classical performances in summer (July–Sept). <cite index="4-12">Music, fashion, history, architecture spanning Antiquity to Art Deco</cite>. Limited compared to Vienna or Sofia.
bonus.dance_scene: Salsa/bachata: small but growing scene in Kapana bars/clubs. Classes rare (mostly local instructors). Better options in Sofia (1.5 hours by bus).

concerns: Small expat/startup community—limited peer network, fewer events, potential isolation after 2–3 weeks.
Pervasive indoor smoking in restaurants/cafes; allergies/health-sensitive travelers may struggle.
Limited classical music/concert calendar (seasonal, fewer venues than Vienna).
Language barrier outside tourist zones; Bulgarian-only instruction common.
No sailing/water sports due to inland location.
Limited coworking retreat ecosystem (vs. Bansko, Sofia, or Mediterranean hubs).
Slow social rhythm—fewer late-night dining/nightlife options for extroverts.
